room. He looks around the room. There is a wide glass window behind him. He gets up from the
chair, still weak from the sedative. He walks to the window and starts banging on it, shouting for
Sarah. Avery enters the room startling Miles. She walks over to the table where there is a chair
opposite to where Miles was sitting. “Come take a sit Mr Kelly.” She says to him. He takes a seat
and waits. After a pause she starts talking. “You know Miles, it is very important that we don’t
tell anyone about what happens here.” She says to Miles, looking him in the eyes. He cannot
seem to speak, so he nods his head. She continues talking. “Do you understand what you are?”
She adds. Miles does not hesitate and answers. “I am monster.” Avery continues looking at him
and responds. “You have no idea.” Miles looks down at the floor in frustration, unable to
continue the conversation anymore. Avery puts a hand on his hand and says to him, “Listen
Miles, we aren’t here to harm you. We only want you to cooperate.” She removes her hand from
his and continues speaking. “All you have to do for now is give us some information about you,
your family, how they treated you or even about your past life.”
“If I tell you something, will you leave me alone?” Miles questions. Avery nods her head
and responds, “Of course.” Miles begins to explain how he lost his mother and sister in an

accident when he was 6 years old. Their car crashed into a building. As Miles told his story
Avery did not look surprised or sad. She just sat there, taking notes and staring at Miles. “It took
me years to learn to live without them. My father he. He wasn’t the best.” Avery leans back on
her chair and says, “Well, go on.” Miles looks at her but stays quiet. He wonders if she wants
him to talk about the murders he has committed. “We know about the murders.” Avery says to
him. He looks puzzled by this as he was just thinking about it. “My boss and I are very interested
in people like you. We had hoped that you would become a part of our project.” She tells Miles
with a smug look on her face. Miles suddenly felt extremely uneasy. “What is the project?”
Miles asks her curiously. Avery smiles at him and says, “First we have to know what you have
hiding under there.” They talk for several minutes as Avery explains the concept of demons to
Miles and how he might have one possessing him. When Avery finishes talking, Miles jumps up
from his seat and shouts at Avery. “Stop! Stop talking! You’re making everything worse!” Avery
smiles again and replies “Don’t worry Miles, I am going to help you.” she turns away from him
to write something down. Miles sits back down and asks, “How am I supposed to trust you after
all that you’ve said?”
“You have no choice.” Avery replies simply. She then starts asking Miles a series of
questions that he had no idea how to answer. “Tell me Miles, do you know what makes you
special?” She asks him in a calm but demanding voice. Miles still had no answers. Avery
continues. “Why do you kill people Miles?” He lifts his head up and looks at her, still at a loss
for words. Miles then recounted the first time he ever killed anyone, hoping she would gauge
from that his reasons. He tells her how it started. When he was just 16, Miles met an attractive
19-year-old transgender black woman in his rural hometown. He recalled her name was Susan.
Miles says Susan was between 5’6” – 5’7” tall and approximately 140 pounds. He first met

Susan at a bar known as The Yard. A few days later, they met again at another bar in Overtown,
where Susan offered to give him a ride home. They drove in Susan’s car—a gold 4-door Pontiac
LeMans. “As she was driving across north on Highway 27, I asked her to pull over on a
driveway near a sugarcane field.” He goes on to tell Avery what happened next. “Suddenly, out
of nowhere and without a second thought I just. I bashed her head in, killing her instantly. Then I
drove further down Highway 27, onto the Glades, turned down a dirt road that led to a swamp.”
Miles dragged Susan’s body into the thick, muddy water and dumped it. “Without provocation,
without planning I just. I just killed somebody. And you know what? I think I enjoyed it. From
then on I just needed to kill and the more I did, something in me felt more powerful.”
“And what about now?” Avery questions. “Now I’m afraid that I’ll kill someone else.”
He states calmly. Avery laughs at him for a while before continuing. “So, you have never done
anything else since then? Nothing other than being a killer?” Miles thinks about her question for
a few seconds and eventually replies, “Not exactly.” Avery takes out a syringe filled with a dark
liquid and walks over to Miles. She presses the needle directly against his neck and injects it into
his skin. He starts convulsing violently and falls backwards onto the floor. Avery then comes
over and sits on the edge of the table watching him. After another minute, Miles stops convulsing
and lies motionless in a state of unconsciousness. Avery signals through the window and men in
cloaks walk in. She tells the men to take Miles downstairs while she prepares herself for a visit
from Mrs Williams. The two men drag Miles’ lifeless body off towards another area of the
facility. Mrs Williams walks out of her office and follows Avery into the vault basement. They
are followed by four armed guards. They then walk past one of the cells where they see Sarah
lying on a bed. “What about this one?” Asks Mrs Williams. Avery responds, “We will deal with
it later, I want to finish with Miles first.” Mrs Williams raises her eyebrow at Avery. “No one

touches that girl but me.” She states. “Yes ma’am.” Avery turns around and says to her as she
walks away. Mrs Williams watches as Avery disappears down the basement. She turns towards
the cell and sees Sarah sleeping peacefully in the bed. She then walks over to Sarah and stands
next to the bed looking at her for a moment before turning around and leaving the room.
Summoning a demon requires these ingredients- blood of the summoner, an acacia plant
and oil of Abramelin. Avery had previously mixed the ingredients and placed them in the syringe
she used on Miles earlier. The second step of the summoning ritual involved placing the vessel
atop the Sigil of Baphomet, with candles placed on each point of the pentagram. Once the altar is
set up, an invocation in Latin must be spoken: Attenrobendum eos, ad ligandum eos, potiter eos,
coram me. Which translated means: Command them, bind them, bring them to me. Avery
watched as the men in cloaks performed this task, placing Miles on the sigil and waiting for
Avery to recite the invocation. Once completed, the demon who hears the invocation will
manifest in the nearest vessel and be bound to the will of the summoner. If Avery was right, the
demon possessing Miles was one that could be cleansed. In this type of event, Miles would be
exorcised of the evil, his dark impulses would be removed, leaving only remnants of the demon.
This ritual gave back control to the possessed, freeing them forever to truly decide their own
actions and motivations. Once finished, the guards left the warehouse with Miles, placing him
back in the white room. Mrs Williams walked over to the room that contained Miles and looked
inside at his peaceful expression. “Finish up.” She sternly tells Avery. “Of course, Mrs
Williams.” Avery responds. Inside the room, Avery sits right beside Miles, who looks rather
different. She tells Miles, “There are more out there. Some of them like you but most not as
lucky. Innocent people taken over by vile creatures destroying this world. Like you did. Tens of
thousands of people needlessly dying to serve evil. They need to be stopped.” Her words send

shivers through Miles’s body. “This world is not good and sometimes I think, neither are the
people that we have to protect. We cannot sit idly by anymore; we must act to protect everyone.
That is why I will show you my vision for the future.” Miles begins questioning her again, but
she interrupts him saying, “We have time for your questions. The important thing is what you
must do now, so listen carefully to my instructions.” She closes her eyes and opens her mouth.
She starts reciting something that she had seen in a vision. “The future shall not pass until all is
well and every soul has found peace.” She opens her eyes and looks at Miles. “That’s my
warning. I want you to get rid of those thoughts that are tormenting you. Take a nap, you have a
long day ahead of you.” Avery says as she gets up from her sit. Miles nods his head in agreement.
He leans back into the chair closing his eyes. Avery leaves him alone in the room closing the
door behind her. He lays quietly in the chair staring straight ahead. Miles slowly starts drifting
off to sleep, exhausted from what has just happened. He starts to feel strange. His whole body is
shaking and the muscles in his jaw begin tensing up slightly as his eyelids begin to droop. He
feels tired but unable to stop himself. Miles is trying desperately to keep his eyes open and
conscious, but he cannot fight it any longer. Suddenly Miles feels like he is going to throw up. It
takes almost everything in him to force back the contents in his stomach. He tries his best to
focus on staying conscious, but he cannot do it any longer. He feels himself slipping down into
the depths of slumber. He lets his eyes slip shut. Just as he does, he drops his body on to the table
and a huge dent appears. Avery was standing in the doorway smiling at the sight in front of her.
She looks outside the room and notices the sun has already begun setting. Avery knows she
needs to prepare and soon.

chapters.title: Sarah May
chapters.text:
Sarah’s consciousness finally returns after what feels like forever. She immediately
begins looking around frantically to try and find herself. After a couple of seconds of searching,
the darkness begins to clear up revealing a large room, with a high ceiling. She remembers that
whole ordeal with Miles and wonders what happened to him. She collects her thoughts as she sits
on her bed. A woman enters the room, finding Sarah just sitting there, calm. “Are you OK
sweetie?” The woman asks. Sarah answers, “Yeah, yeah, fine.” Sarah then proceeds to ask her
what happened to Miles. “He went away.” The woman answered. “I hope you aren’t scared.”
She continues. “I’m Mrs Williams.” Sarah does not answer or move, thinking about what she
should say. Finally, she speaks. “I don’t remember much from last night.” Mrs Williams tells
her, “You were drugged dear. Come now let’s get you washed up.” She helps Sarah stand up as
she leads her to a bathroom. As she showers, Mrs Williams asks Sarah what she thinks about the
shower. Sarah shrugs and replies, “It isn’t terrible.” After getting dressed, the women leave and
head downstairs to the basement where the cellar is. They go down into the room where Miles
was questioned. Mrs Williams goes over to the table and grabs a bottle. Opening the bottle, she
pours some of its contents into a glass. Then she offers it to Sarah saying, “Drink it all down
sweetie.” Sarah accepts and swallows the entire thing. Mrs Williams then tells her, “Now we can
begin.” Sarah looks at her puzzled and shakes her head. “It’s okay dear, trust me, everything will
become clearer when you wake up.” She tells her. Mrs Williams then walks up to Sarah and
gives her a dose of the same black substance given to Miles a day earlier, knocking her out cold.

As Sarah drifts into unconsciousness, she hears a hoarse voice calling her name. In the
dim recesses of her mind Sarah hears this voice. She follows in the direction it is coming from.
As she searches her psyche, a raven appears to her. She gets close to the raven but it flies away.
She follows behind it. Finally, the raven comes to a halt, hovering above her. She wonders in a
soft voice, “Is that a bird.” The raven speaks to her. “I am Naberius”. Sarah looks shaken for a
second and quips, “So it speaks.” Naberius repeats himself this time saying, “Come with me
Sarah.” Sarah hesitates, but eventually walks toward him. Before walking any further though,
she stops and asks, “Why did you call me here?” The raven replies, “You will know shortly.”
Sarah starts to feel nervous and afraid as Naberius approaches her. He starts circling her, she
turns around once he reaches her. He stares into her eyes and whispers something to her. “The
truth may hurt, but it will also free you.” With that she feels Naberius’ power consume her mind.
The pain becomes unbearable and Sarah screams as loud as she can. The pain subsides allowing
Sarah the room to breathe again. Naberius disappears from the scene, and Sarah falls to her knees.
She is crying uncontrollably as she tries to take deep breaths. The sound of footsteps approaching
causes her to look up. A figure emerges from the shadows. Which she recognizes immediately.
“Father?” she questions weakly. Her Father pulls her into a hug, sobbing hysterically as he holds
her tightly. When she calms down enough to hear his words, she cries even harder than before.
Her Father explains what he did, how he tried to kill her. How he thought she died. Her Father
asks if she wants to go home. Sarah agrees and stands up. As soon as she does, her father’s
words hit her. “You tried to kill me? Why father” He explains to her how she was not being
herself, how something had taken over her mind. “You were gone dear, your soul it. It was taken.
But Neberius saved you, I know that now.” He continues to explain. “He has given you a fraction
of his power. You need to trust him. The people out there are going to hurt you and you need this

power to protect you.” Sarah stays silent listening to her father. She feels so confused and lost.
She didn’t understand anything. She could not process what her father said to her. The darkness
surrounding her begins to crumble. The image of her father disintegrates and she is left standing
alone as the world around her starts to vanish. Slowly back in the real world she opens her eyes,
finding herself in a dimly lit room inside a circle on the floor with candles all around her. Out of
the corner of her eyes she notices Mrs Williams and a couple of other guys in cloaks. She does
not scream. She is not afraid. Mrs Williams was in the middle of reciting an invocation when
Sarah woke up. She assumes it worked. “Go tell the others we are ready.” She says to one of the
men. Mrs Williams walks up to a weak Sarah and leads her out of the cellar back to the white
room. Once there, Mrs Williams begins her interrogation of Sarah assuming the ritual had
worked. She now was interested in getting Sarah to work for them.
“Where is Miles?” Sarah inquires. She refuses to hear anything Mrs Williams had to say
and demands to see Miles. Unable to control her, Mrs Williams obliges her request. She leads
Sarah to a different area of the facility. It is different here. The rooms are brightly lit, creating a
cool atmosphere. There are nice rooms where everyone sleeps and there’s even a cafeteria filled
with amazing, delicious looking food. Deep within the area is also a training ground. With
equipment for recruits to test out their new abilities and sharpen their combat skills. Mrs
Williams leads Sarah into the room where Miles is. She finds him waiting for them there. He
greets Sarah with a kiss on the forehead and hugs her tight. He then directs her to sit beside him
at the table. Mrs Williams glances at him and says, “I hope you enjoy each other’s company.”
She then turns on her heels and walks away leaving Miles and Sarah alone together. The only
sounds heard in the room are those coming from the radio set playing music. Sarah takes the
chance to look closely at Miles. She sees how pale he is, his eyes look sunken in and bloodshot.

He does look exhausted as well. Sarah decides to speak first. “Miles, are you ok? Is there
something wrong with you? Are you sick? Do you need to go to the hospital?” She is asking him
rapid fire questions hoping to make sure he is alright. Miles is struggling to put together a
response. Eventually, he tells Sarah, “I’ll be ok Sarah, you will never believe what happened to
me.” He seems very concerned for her wellbeing. His voice cracks as he tells her what happened
to him. Sarah sits there in shock while listening to Miles’ story. Miles then looks over at her and
smiles trying to lighten the mood. Sarah smiles back. He gets up off the chair and walks towards
Sarah slowly. He kneels before her and cups her face in his hand as he kisses her passionately.
“God Sarah, I missed you so much.” He says through muffled kisses. ‘Miles what are you
doing.’ Sarah thinks. After a while Sarah pulls away. He rests his forehead against hers and tells
her, “You have no idea what my life was like before I came here.” He places both hands on
either side of Sarah’s face and whispers, “I am saved.” He brings his lips to meet Sarah’s and
they start kissing passionately. Sarah wraps her arms around Miles and kisses back. After a while,
the guards come to collect Sarah. They tell them it’s time for sleep.
In her room, Sarah begins to hear Naberius’ voice again. With a hoarse voice he tells her:
“I am Naberius. I am a valiant marquise, shewing thineself in the forme of a crowe. I maketh
man amiable and cunning in all arts, and speciallie in rhetorike. Nineteene legions heare and
obeie him.” Sarah appears annoyed and exclaims “English!” To which Naberius responds, “You
would do well to respect me girl!” He continues. “I am a demon; I can make you more amiable
and cunning in all arts. When you speak you speak through me and all men will listen.” He says.
“A demon huh, well I figured as much.” Sarah says to Naberius. “Why should I trust you, you’re
a freaking demon.” This comment makes Naberius furious. “What has happened to the sweet,
innocent little mouse who used to love me? What have you done?” Naberius yells at Sarah. She

looks visibly confused by this statement. “What?” Naberius laughs. “How dare you try to act all
innocent and clueless about everything that has happened!” He screams. Sarah is flustered by his
outburst and she retorts back defensively, “Excuse me?! How dare you! You have no right to talk
like that!” He notices that she does not remember their time together. “Well sorry missy, but
you’re the one who hasn’t done anything in the past three weeks. That hag, Williams, kidnapped
you can’t you see.”
“She didn’t kidnap me! If anything, she might have saved me from you.” Naberius laughs
mockingly at Sarah. “She only saved you so that you could use your magic for herself, that
means nothing because you’ll still be enslaved.” He laughs at Sarah once more. She clenches her
jaw angrily. She then realizes what has become of her. Tears stream down her face, the pain she
felt was worse than ever before. She then looks up at Naberius with her head hung low and she
whispers quietly to him, “If it’s not too late.” Naberius then grabs her face with one hand and
forcefully pushes it up until they lock eyes. His eyes glow bright red. He begins to loudly
exclaim which causes the walls in the room to shake. “Oblivion, Oblivion. Oblivion, Oblivion.
Oblivion.” Naberius keeps shouting and laughing at Sarah. “It’s too late Sarah. You belong to me
now.” She tries to struggle out of his grasp but fails. Sarah’s body gives in and the world fades to
black. Now inside her mind, Naberius shows her everything they have ever done while he
possessed her. “You see child, you belong to me.” He tells her. The ritual Mrs Williams had
performed on Sarah had only aided in pushing Naberius deep into her psyche. Even though he no
longer possessed her, he still had some influence on her soul. Her thoughts begin to spiral and
turn to anger. Her screams echo throughout the building, causing Mrs Williams to rush over to
check on her. After a few minutes of screaming and yelling she stops. Mrs Williams looks at her

and asks, “Are you alright?” Sarah just looks at her blankly. After a moment she speaks. “I’m ok.
Just a nightmare that’s all.” Mrs Williams nods and leaves the room, closing the door behind her.
The next morning, Sarah wakes up feeling drained and exhausted. The door to her room
is not locked. She walks out, making her way to the cafeteria where Miles and a bunch of other
people are enjoying breakfast. One guy spots her walking in and calls out to her. “Hey! What
took you so long?” He laughs and she replies smiling awkwardly, “I overslept.” Everyone else
stops talking and looks at her, surprised that she spoke. A short brunette wearing an outfit similar
to the girls in school walks up to her and asks, “What are you doing here?” The girl glares at her
and snaps. “You must be some kind of monster or something.” Sarah does not know how to
respond. Mrs Williams comes over. She puts her hand on the brunettes' shoulder. "Amala stop
being rude.” Amala frowns but says nothing. Sarah stares at her and then looks at everyone else.
Everyone starts whispering and staring at her. Mrs Williams notices that Sarah is not reacting
anymore. She turns to look at Sarah with concern written all over her face. She looks at Sarah
and then whispers, “Don’t worry dear, they are harmless.” After eating and then showering,
Sarah heads back to her quarters. As soon as she enters the room she goes over to her bed and
lays down, she closes her eyes and drifts off into a deep sleep. When she finally wakes up the
sun is already setting outside her window. She gets up and heads to the bathroom, freshening up
before heading back into her bedroom. She changes into a pair of pyjamas Mrs Williams laid out
for her and walks back over to her bed. Sitting there with her head resting on her knees. She
starts thinking about everything that has happened since she left home. The events the led to her
imprisonment in the first place replay themselves in her head again and again, almost like a
movie she sees each moment. After spending quite some time thinking, a knock sounds on the
door followed by Mrs Williams entering the room with a medical kit in hand. “I’m here to check

on you dear.” She says to Sarah. Sarah allows Mrs Williams to do her thing and as she is doing
that, she asks her, “You still haven’t told me exactly what I’m doing here. And that weird
voodoo crap, how did you know to do that?” Mrs Williams finishes taking her vitals and leaves a
journal and a pen on a table near the bed. She tells Sarah “What you’ve gone through is a lot for
anyone, so here, a journal. You could write all your thoughts down, maybe dreams you might
have or anything you are feeling. And don’t worry, you will learn soon enough why we need you
here.” She then walks out of Sarah’s room, not waiting for a response. Sarah gets up from her
position on the bed and walks over to her desk to grab her journal before returning to sit on her
bed. She picks up her pen and playfully flips through the pages for a while until she stops on the
first page. On it she writes, ‘I went back to the hall today. I had fun playing games with Miles
and we talked a bit. I hope things are going well between us.’ she sighs as she puts her journal
back on the desk. She is so worried about Miles and what has happened with him lately. Sarah
looks down at the ground and shakes her head. She then looks over at the clock which reads,
2:50. It’s almost 3 am but she decides to get up and go for a run anyway. After dressing herself,
she walks out of her room. Sarah starts running through the hallway trying to remember which
direction leads to the courtyard. She runs for a couple of minutes before turning left. She runs
past several corridors before reaching a large open space. There she finds Miles sitting on a
bench looking out toward the ocean. Sarah runs up to Miles and says, “Hey.” Miles looks at her.
“Hi.” She sits beside him. “So, what were you thinking about?” She asks. Miles looks at Sarah
and smiles softly. “You.” He says. Sarah raises her eyebrow at him and asks, “Me?”. He simply
nods. She leans closer. “You can’t just say things like that.” Miles smirks at her and replies, “Yes
I can.” She just rolls her eyes at him. “Really, what was on your mind?” Miles takes a deep
breath and looks at the water. “Well, I was thinking that you seem different somehow.” He

continues. “And I was also wondering if…” He hesitates “No forget it”. She takes his hand into
hers “You can tell me Miles; you do know you can trust me, right?” He looks at her intently and
responds, “I told you about all that demon stuff, right? I had one like inside of me and now that it
is gone, I feel freer, stronger even I just feel like I can do anything.” Sarah looks at him with
surprise. “I wasn’t aware that demons existed.” She says quietly. “Not many do.” He looks away
and continues looking at the ocean. She reaches over and pulls him onto her lap. He rests his
head against her chest and listens to her heartbeat. They stay like that for a while without saying
a word. Sarah looks down at Miles and kisses him softly on the lips before pulling away. She
starts humming a song softly to him while caressing his hair, her fingers playing with his curls.
Soon Miles falls asleep after about 30 minutes of listening to Sarah’s soft singing. Once they
wake up, Miles returned to his room and Sarah to hers. She brushes her hair getting ready for bed.
She grabbed her journal from her desk and began to write what happened in her dream. She
wrote down all that happened and all of the horrible things that she saw. Afterwards Sarah fell
asleep and woke up in her bed at. She looks down at her journal and notices that she still has ink
stains all over it from when she wrote about the torture. With a frown she opens up her notebook
to try to wipe them away, but she quickly closes it once she realizes they would never come out.
She sets her journal on her nightstand and lies down. Not long afterwards she hears Naberius
again. “You would reveal me child?” He says to her. Sarah looks blankly at the wall as a moment
of realisation reaches her mind. She gets up from her bed and grabs the journal looking at it
intently. Suddenly the book begins to smoke, catching fire as Sarah holds it in her hand. The
book burns and soon after it turns to ash. Sarah frowns before opening the window to her room,
letting the cool brisk air inside, blowing the ash away. Miles rushes over to her room. “Hey did
you hear that? They are calling us to the yard, something about beginning our training. Come on

let’s go.” Sarah smiles at him and they run out of her room together, excited to begin their new
adventure.

chapters.title: Atticus Finch
chapters.text:
Mr Finch is an instructor at Defiance. He teaches the recruits all about the new world
they are going to be involved with. He gives them a crash course on everything there is to know
about demons and supernatural beings and how to fight them. The first lesson was on rituals. He
teaches them that rituals are a prescribed form of ceremony performed in order to achieve a
transformation of consciousness. Every religion, spiritual, mystical or magical tradition has its
own rituals. A person who conducts a ritual does so as a means by which to contact divine or
supernatural will or forces. In the tradition of magic for instance, rituals are used to change
consciousness and to bring desired things into being- “What do you believe a person might want
from these rituals?” He asks. Everyone seems to shrug and none of them say anything. “Well,
there is always something a person wants.” He looks around the room. “Ask yourself, what am I
hoping to accomplish with this ritual?” A man standing at the back of the class raises his hand
“To attain power I guess.” Mr Finch chuckles a bit before replying, “Sure no that works. But
You must always consider what you’ll be using that power for. Some people abuse it. Using this
power to enslave weaker men and bend them to their will. They use this power for their own
selfish gains. They covert this power. They hide in the shadows pretending that they are helping
those weaker than they are. They deceive others into doing their bidding. They take advantage of
anyone who is weak and vulnerable. And then they use them to achieve their ends. The worst of
which are those who don’t even understand what is being done to them. Those are the ones who
suffer greatly.” Mr Finch continues to teach them about rituals and the history surrounding them.
He also explains how they work in various forms. “Some rituals involve sacrificing animals,

others involve consuming humans. But most use human sacrifices to make the transition easier
and therefore quicker.” Mr Finch continues teaching them, but after a while he realizes that most
of them have no idea what they are actually learning. Most seem confused by all of the terms and
concepts he’s using and no one shows any signs of understanding the information they are
getting. He continues nonetheless. He adds “The most effective rituals, even if they are simple,
engage the senses. This transforms the consciousness and opens the gateway to perceptive and
imaginative thinking. Rituals are accomplished mainly through recitation, chanting, prayer,
evocation and invocation, burning incense, candles and even merely using fire alone as an
element. Together with offerings and sacrifices, these elements create psychological and physical
changes intended to help achieve the goal of the ritual. All elements of a ritual must be observed
correctly to ensure success. Elements such as invocations are always spoken aloud, activating the
magical properties of sound through sacred and magical words that align and harmonize the
consciousness with the spiritual. They are carefully composed to attract a particular entity or
spirit. The invocation must address their attributes and offer the right praise.” The student who
interrupted Finch earlier asks, “But how do we know if they’ll accept our requests and use their
abilities for us?” Mr Finch then answers “You will know.” After that lesson, Mr Finch dismisses
the students for the night. When they leave, Sarah walks past Finch, giving him a curious glance
as she passes by.
Sarah goes outside and finds herself a quiet part of the facility and enters another room
filled with bookshelves. She starts walking across the room, looking for some kind of book that
contains knowledge that could help her. After passing several stacks of dusty books, she spots it.
She grabs it and places it onto a nearby table, flipping through the pages. A name catches her
attention and she stops to read it: John Adams. Sarah quickly flips to the other side and reads a

passage explaining what it entails. Then she flips back to the beginning and reads further,
discovering how the process of sacrifice might play a key role in a ritual. As she reads, she
wonders if that is really necessary. If a sacrifice would help her, it would mean more bloodshed
and suffering. She did not want that at all. Mr Finch walks into the room finding Sarah buried in
the book. “How goes it young lady?” He says to Sarah. “I’ve been reading some stuff.” Sarah
replies, turning her attention away from the pages to look toward him. “Really? Anything
interesting?” He asks, taking notice of what she is reading. “Not really.” She shakes her head. Mr
Finch smiles and walks closer to where she is sitting on the table. “Would you like some tea?”
He offers. “Sure…” she replied, closing the book. The two walk down the hall, entering another
room. There, Mr Finch made her a cup of tea. “Thanks.” She said as she takes a sip. “Now tell
me what you were really reading.” Mr Finch says leaning against the table. “Well, it was talking
about rituals, and the way sacrifices work.” Mr Finch chuckles a bit. “Sacrifices aren’t necessary
in many cases. Most people find that sacrificing oneself, instead, helps them achieve their goals.”
Sarah stares at Mr Finch curiously. He does not react and keeps on talking. “It is a very common
belief among humans that sacrifices give one power and that they are beneficial and strengthen
you. However, in reality, they are not helpful at all in all situations. The rituals are designed to
draw upon the energies of the universe. They are meant to bring certain entities here to be
granted access to your consciousness. That is to say, to possess your body.” He passionately
explains. “Are you implying that there is someone controlling me and making me do all of this?”
She asked. Mr Finch shook his head and smiled. “No, no, you are missing the point. You are free
to choose to follow this path. What I’m saying is power comes from nature, there is nothing
natural about sacrifices.” They continue talking for a few moments and Sarah asks him how he
knows so much about this and about he came to do this for a living. “You seem so normal Mr

Finch, how did you end up with demon fighting on your job description.” Atticus had always
been in the business from the beginning. He tells her “I was born into this. But I only recently
started working with Defiance.”
“I was an assistant professor of religious studies down State, at a rapidly growing state
college in Saint Marcos. The college offered a minor in religious studies and had recently created
a major. I was hired to assist in building the new major and generate student interest in religious
studies. I was encouraged to teach a class that would ‘get butts in seats’ and show why religious
studies is a fascinating field. I had just finished editing an encyclopaedia on Spirit Possession
around the World and this seemed like the perfect basis for a high-interest course. I designed a
course centred around that called “Possession and Exorcism in the practice of Demonology.” It
was based on my exploits as a demonologist myself. As a demonologist, I cleared people’s
homes of negative and dark energy. Demonic entities often attach themselves to certain people or
their homes. I open the literal gates of hell and throw them back. I can actually communicate
with the demons. I’ll talk to them as I would to people. That is my gift. The biggest challenge I
had teaching a demonology course was getting my students to find a balance between
credulousness and scepticism. When looking at claims of extraordinary experiences I find that
there is some difficulties rooting out the genuine from the fake. The course took place over a
period of six months, and during that time I had a group of students come up to me in class and
ask if I could share some of the techniques that I used to clear people’s homes of demonic
entities. Of course, it was important to elaborate on these topics for them and let them absorb
what I had learned so they could use it in their research. My job was to get students interested
and learn about this subject. One of my students, Catherine, came to me saying her mother was
possessed and that she needed my help. I was a bit sceptical at first but I did not dismiss her. She

told me it started with flies. Big black flies suddenly swarmed their front deck in December the
previous year despite the winter chill. They killed them and killed them, but yet they kept
coming back. Her mother was afraid to go near the house because the insects bite her all the time.
She couldn’t sleep and when she tried eating, the bugs flew out from her food. She didn’t feel
safe anymore. When Catherine told me this, I knew it was definitely something sinister and I
agreed to help her. When I got to the house, I could smell something in the air, there was
definitely a demon in the house. I gathered my materials and prepared for a ritual. Her mother
stood in a ceremonial circle I drew across the living room while I recited my invocations.
Suddenly a swarm of flies came out through her mother’s mouth. The buzzing was loud and they
surrounded us from all sides. I told her not to be afraid and continued with the invocation.
Immediately after, there were loud thunders and flashes of lightening and then, a huge flash of
energy hit a window next to her and destroyed it completely! The lightning struck me too,
leaving a wound on my shoulder and a burn on my arm. The flies attacked me relentlessly until I
finally managed to complete the invocation. There was silence for a few seconds. Soon after, her
mother went flying across the room. It was dizzying. Then a large hole appeared beneath the
floor. There were flames and scattered screams coming through. I had performed exorcisms
before but this was something I had never seen. The mother came flying and was sucked into the
hole which immediately closed. Me and Catherine looked at each other shocked at what we both
just witnessed. She let out a deafening cry. Her only parent was lost into what she could only
imagine as hell. I was shaken too. From that day on I could never perform another ritual again.
The supernatural and I were finished. Then one day a man visited me. He told me his name was
Dr Andrew Reed and he told me some crazy things. He explained to me that that girl’s mother
was only one of the many victims. He told me about a society of powerful men that had power

over these creatures. He called them the Devil’s den. He asked me to help him in destroying
them. I surprised myself and agreed to join his crusade.”
Sarah looks amused at the story Mr Finch had just recalled. “What happened next?” She
asked curiously. Mr Finch sighs and continues. "I have been here since then; I won’t bore you
with the details in-between. I have to leave now, see you in class tomorrow.” Mr Finch exits the
room, leaving Sarah staring at the empty space where he once stood. She opens the book again
and continues reading, trying to ignore her thoughts and worries. Atticus returns to his office and
begins planning for the next day’s lesson. He looks out of the window to see the sun setting over
the horizon, illuminating the sky above him. He closes the blinds and sets his glasses on the desk
behind him. He thinks for a moment then turns to his bag and pulls out a bottle of scotch. He
pours himself a shot and downs the entire thing before grabbing a glass, filling it with water to
drink. After which, he proceeds to pour a second shot for himself and drinks it. He sits in front of
the window sipping his drink, watching the sun set. Atticus sits there alone remembering the
feeling he felt when those flies flew out of Catherine’s mom. His eyes begin to close slowly as
he drinks the rest of his drink and eventually, he passes out, falling into an uneasy sleep in his
chair. The night comes and goes.
The next morning, Atticus wakes up from his office chair, hungover and tense all over.
He groans slightly before standing up and walking towards the door. As soon as he reaches it
however, his hand shoots forward and unlocks it before he even has any idea what’s going on.
He looks at the doorknob and lets out a chuckle. “Damn it, Finch.” He whispers to himself. Finch
pushes the door open, walks down the hallway and stops at the kitchen. He grabs a glass and fills
it full of water before taking the glass, turning on the coffee machine and filling the pot. After
waiting for a few seconds, he pours himself a mug and proceeds to sit down on the barstool. He

stirs the coffee gently before adding the sugar and milk. He brings the mug up to his lips and
takes a sip of the coffee. He sets the mug down and leans back in the stool. He takes another sip
of coffee and decides to go get ready for the day ahead. As he stands up he notices that the coffee
table has changed. Instead of two pieces of furniture, it now has four. “I’m going insane!” He
says to himself. “Maybe I should go for a walk first? Yeah, let’s go do that.” He puts the mug
down on the counter and heads out of the kitchen and up the stairs. After his run, his mind was
clear. He gets dressed for the day and walks out his room with purpose. He enters the lounge area
where he sees Sarah sitting on one of the couches, staring into the distance and looking slightly
disoriented. Atticus raises his eyebrows at her. She seems to notice him and jumps up from the
couch. “Hey, I have something to tell you.” She goes on to tell him about what has been
happening to her and how she has been keeping it a secret. “This monster inside of me, Naberius.
What is it? How do I get rid of it?” she asks. Atticus is surprised to learn this information. He
believed Mrs Williams had taken care of the demon that once housed itself inside Sarah, but
from what she was telling him right now it appeared that was not the case. He shakes his head
slightly in surprise, but still remains silent. Sarah waits patiently for his response. A few
moments pass by before Atticus speaks up. “I don’t know exactly what is happening to you right
now, but I will try to help you figure it out. You are very strong and smart; we can figure this
out.” Atticus contemplates informing Mrs Williams of this new development but quickly decides
against it. Ever since he joined Defiance he was kept mostly out of the loop. Relegated to
teaching duties and kept out of the real gritty stuff. He was itching to get back into it so he
decided he would help Sarah on his own. This would mean dealing with the demon within her on
his own, but he figured if anyone could figure it out, it was him. Sarah smiles, relieved to hear
what she thought was good news. “You’re not angry that I kept this for so long?” She asks.

“Why would I be mad? We can deal with this.” Atticus reassures her. Sarah nods in agreement
before continuing. “Thank you for helping me. I really appreciate it.” She says. “No need to
thank me, Sarah, it is my job.” He tells her that he knows of an evocation that could bind the
demon long enough to speak to it. “I don’t think you are actually possessed by this thing; in fact,
I am pretty sure the ritual Mrs Williams did on you did what it was meant to do. It was just the
wrong invocation. We are dealing with a different breed of demon and Mrs Williams did not
realise that.” She looks at him worried but eager to begin. “But what do I do, how do I get rid of
it?” She asks. Atticus is silent for a while thinking about an answer. At last, he replies. “Well,
you already know the ritual, correct?” He watches her nod and continues. “It is probably best we
do the evocation together, that way it functions better.” They head downstairs to the creepy cellar
where these things were done. It is already set up, the circle on the floor, the candles and some of
the herbs needed. Atticus knows the evocation by heart. They begin the ritual. When they finish,
she collapses onto the ground unconscious. He looks at her with concern. “Are you alright?” He
places his hand on top of her body, trying to assess her condition. It does not take him too long to
find signs of breathing. She moans loudly and opens her eyes. She looks around confused. Then
suddenly she starts speaking in a hoarse voice. “Little Atticus, come to play?” Atticus backs
away a few feet. “You know my name?” He asks. “We all know you down here, you are pretty
famous, for your failures at least. That woman you let get dragged down to hell, she says hello!”
Atticus appears to be intimidated by this but does not back down. He stares at the demon that is
in front of him. “Who are you?” He asks sternly. “Me? I am who will destroy you.” The demon
chuckles sinisterly and snaps its fingers, causing everything around Atticus to turn blurry for a
brief moment. “Look at you little boy, you are weak!” He glares at him. Atticus glares back at
him before uttering a spell different from his initial evocation. The demon squeals and falls down

in pain. He continues, speaking louder and louder. The demon laughs but still in pain. Sarah’s
eyes turn bright read. Atticus continues reciting his spell getting bolder and bolder. The demon
then disappears from Sarah’s consciousness and she comes to. She looks at him and asks “What
happened.” He looks at her with fear and dread in his eyes. “We have a huge problem.”

chapters.title: Jonathan Reed
chapters.text:
Jonathan Reed was currently sat in his study doing research. The lights were dim and the
room was quiet and calm. The sound of pages turning filled the air accompanied by the
scratching of pens as Jonathan worked hard to complete the work he assigned himself. He was
still reeling from all that his father told him. Since then, he dedicated all his time to finding
evidence to everything that was revealed to him. He did not trust his father and so he went out on
his own to find out if it was actually real. There was nothing he could find, which only further
convinced him that the old man had gone crazy and was trying to fool him. What was truly
bothering him was learning that there existed people who actually had dominion over the
supernatural. He believed that he might have actually experienced it himself. Jonathan still did
not fully understand what happened to him on that highway. He had read plenty of stories about
demons in books and saw them movies. In truth, he always suspected it was all real, but actually
coming across it was messing with his rational mind. He continued flipping through various
pages until the book closed and shut. He leaned back and let out a deep breath. This is certainly
not a subject he’d want to approach too quickly. He knew that what his father showed him was
far from normal. He also knew that if anyone found out what happened on the road, things would
become a lot more complicated than they already were. Jonathan was happy that his father
returned in his life again. But that did not change his feelings towards his father. He knew his
father loved him, even after he disappeared. And he also knew that despite that, he was afraid of
him. He was aware that this feeling would always be present but he did not know how to act
about it. So instead, he opted for silence as usual. He got up from his desk chair and walked over

to the window of his apartment. He opened the curtains and looked outside. The view from his
windowsill was stunning, the city lights shone brightly above and illuminated the surrounding
buildings below. The moon was out and it seemed to be shining brighter than it ever had before.
His eyes wandered down towards the river below him and watched as the waves crashed
rhythmically against the shore. For some reason, he felt like someone was watching him, making
him feel uneasy. He jumped when he heard something move behind him. He turned around and
immediately relaxed upon seeing it was only his cat. He smiled to himself and took a deep breath.
He returned his attention to the view before him, taking in its beauty. The view reminded him of
home. Jonathan remembered how happy he was during those times when he lived at home with
his parents. His mother used to sing lullabies when he was younger, sometimes even playing
them on the piano. She used to read him bedtime stories and read to him books of mythology. He
grew to love these stories, even asking his mother to take him to conventions where they
showcased the occult. He remembers a photo he took with his mother on one of these trips. He
took it with him wherever he went. It was even hanging in his new apartment. When he looked
over to where it was hanging, it wasn’t there anymore. He frowned, but shrugged it off. The
photograph must have gotten knocked to the side somewhere he thought.
He sat there in silence, feeling uneasy about the missing photograph. He has a thought
but was not quite sure about it. Jonathan then went downstairs following his hunch. He heads to
the security’s office and politely asked to see the security footage for the past week. “Hi, I’m a
cop and I live on the sixth floor. I think someone broke into my apartment last week and I want
to see the footage from that week, thanks.” The guard looked up from her computer screen and
said, “That’s fine, follow me.” He followed her down several flights of stairs, to a storage closet.
She pulled open a door at the end of the hall and gestured for him to go inside. Inside was a large

monitor. After going through the footage for several hours he then came across footage that he
was interested in. He gazed at the monitor displaying a video feed showing two figures entering
the building. He remembers the face of the man who appeared with his father at the highway
accident. One of them was definitely him, he was tall and thin. He wore a hooded jacket and had
his face concealed behind a scarf. He recognised that coat and scarf. The other person was short
with greasy curly hair. Jonathan could make out his face. He had no security cameras inside his
apartment and so he had no idea what they did inside his apartment. The men who broke into
Jonathan’s apartment were putting up hidden surveillance equipment in his apartment at the
behest of his father. Jonathan had no clue. They also took the photograph of Jonathan and his
mother. Dr Reed had always spoke about how he missed his family and so they took the picture
for him to remember them. Jonathan took a screenshot of the men that broke into his apartment
to run facial recognition and identify the men. He figured that this was the lead he needed.
Jonathan left the storage closet, going back to his apartment. He has been thinking about what his
father had told him all day and now he had a lead to follow. What his father wanted him to do
was to look at all of this from another perspective.
Jonathan went upstairs to his bedroom and laid down on his bed staring at the ceiling. He
started to drift off into a dreamless sleep. His phone rang. A familiar ringtone echoed through the
empty room. The number displayed on the screen indicated it was from Maeve, the wife of his
partner who died at the highway accident. He answered without hesitation. “Hello?” He heard
nothing. He looked at his clock, the hands said it was 1am. “Maeve? Hello???” He shouted
worriedly. There was no response. He waited a few seconds and tried again. “Maeve?” Still, no
response. He became increasingly anxious, his thoughts racing. Where was she? Why hadn’t she
said anything? He called her again. And again, she did not answer. Jonathan was worried about

her. She had a kid, her husband had just past way and no one could tell her how or why it
happened. Jonathan did not have a way to explain it her that did not seem crazy. At this point of
the investigation, everyone just assumed he died from the crash but even that was lacking in
details. It seems that the coroner had not yet determined cause of death but said it was highly
likely that it was an accident. Jonathan called Maeve’s phone once again. Again, there was no
response. He then decided to call their mutual friend, David.
“Hello? What’s up?” David answered. “I need your help…. something has happened with
Maeve…” he replied in a serious tone. “Wait hold on, hold on, I need to get dressed first.” Said
David. Jonathan agreed and waited on the phone. “What’s up?” David answered. Jonathan tells
him he received a call from Maeve but that she did not say anything and he tried calling her over
and over again and she did not answer. They both were worried about her. They planned on
going over to her home and checking on her. When they arrived, David knocked on her front
door and she did not respond. David decided to use force to enter her house. He knocked very
loudly at the door and eventually, she opened the door and greeted them warmly. “David!
Jonathan! How nice of you to come visit!” She hugged them both and then said, “What are you
guys doing here?” They both were surprised and noticed she was acting a bit odd. “We…we’re
worried about you.” David replied. “What’s wrong?” asked Jonathan. “Nothing’s wrong,
everything’s fine. Come on inside.” She invited them in and led them into her living room.
Jonathan noticed that she was still wearing the same clothes she’d been wearing from two days
ago. Maeve offered them coffee and they both declined. She then sat them down and asked why
they had come. “Well…um we came because we were concerned. You didn’t seem yourself
today and we thought that maybe something bad happened to you.” David explained. Maeve
shook her head and replied, “No I’m fine. Just tired I guess. Maybe I should go lay down for a

while.” Her voice sounded distant as she stared blankly into space. Jonathan couldn’t believe
how different her demeanour was. Usually she was full of energy, talking and laughing as much
as possible. She was too quiet right now; he could clearly see that her mood had completely
changed. “Okay but remember to get some rest okay.” David reminded her. She nodded slightly
and then stood up. “If you insist, I’ll try to get some sleep. Thanks for visiting.” They both
answered “Of course, anytime.” Jonathan and David stood up and headed for the door, Jonathan
closing it behind them. After seeing Maeve and how distraught she was, he was even more
motivated to get to the bottom of what happened. Maeve needed an explanation and so did he.
He was determined to find it. He made his way to his car and drove back home. It was nearly
dawn as he walked up the steps to his apartment, the sun already beginning to peak over the
horizon. The sky was filled with streaks of orange, red and pink. As he entered his apartment he
was met with a strange sight. There was a white envelope lying on the front steps of his
apartment and an unfamiliar note was attached to it. As he got closer to it and read it, his heart
dropped to his stomach. He knew exactly who it was from. He picked up the envelope and
carefully tore it apart before ripping open the letter. His eyes scanned over the contents of the
envelope. It contained five photos; each one depicting people his father had supposedly
identified to be part of the secret group he has been up against. “Devil’s den? Ridiculous.”
Jonathan mutters to himself. The next morning Jonathan heads to work. The first thing he does
when he gets there is to go to his work computer and begin the process of identifying the photos
of the men who broke into his apartment, including the identities of the five men in the photos
his father sent him. As he worked, Jonathan continued trying to connect all the dots but still he
could not understand how the men were connected to his father or how they were involved in the
incident that ended in the death of his partner. One of the photos gets flagged by the system,

identified as a Carlos Valdez. He was arrested for multiple incidences of attempted murder,
aggravated assault, and robbery. The man had a comprehensive rep sheet. On his list of known
associates, it was listed that he often ran with the ‘South east grotto'. The name sparked
Jonathan’s interest. He searched his database, learning that the group often operated out of a
Southland club. He found the address of the club. It wasn’t too hard to find. Once he got the to
the location, he parked his car outside, going over his game plan and coming up with a rough
idea of how he would approach this. He walked into the club. He sees a couple of the patrons
were sitting on bar stools, drinking and having a good time. He approached one of the men. A
man a tending the bar. The man greeted him and introduced himself. “Hey stranger, what can I
get you?” He asked Jonathan. “I would like to talk to someone named Carlos Valdez.” Said
Jonathan. The man smiled. “Carlos? Oh yeah sure. He’s right over there.” The man pointed to
one of the guys seated near the back exit. Jonathan thanked him and made his way towards the
man he indicated. As soon as he stepped within speaking distance of Carlos he raised his hand,
“Hey, sorry man. I need to talk to you.” Carlos looked up at Jonathan. Jonathan introduced
himself and told Carlos that he knew him. Carlos looked confused. “How do you know me?” He
asked. “My buddy used to work with you?” Jonathan replied. Carlos looks at Jonathan
dumbfounded; he did not know who Jonathan was talking about. “Anyway, my buddy told me
you could get me in with the grotto.” Jonathan was trying to be slick and get invited to this man’s
gang. But there was a problem. Jonathan hadn’t the faintest idea what a grotto was. He neglected
to do research on it as he was in such a hurry to follow up on the lead. That was his first mistake.
Carlos was amused by Jon’s request. He began chuckling. “Jon, you’re a pretty funny guy.”
Jonathan insisted, “Look, I really need to talk to you.” Carlos was growing suspicious. He
motioned Jonathan to take a sit beside him. “Sit down and we’ll go over what I can do for you.”

Carlos suggested. Jonathan hesitantly followed Carlos, seating himself down on a barstool.
Carlos then sat down beside Jonathan. He took a sip of his beer and smiled at Jonathan. “So, Jon,
why don’t we start off slow, eh? How about you tell me what you think you know.” He paused
and added “And then I’ll tell you what I know.” Jonathan was totally blindsided but he was quick
on his feet, replaying, "I suppose what I know is that you are part of some sort of secret gang?”
Carlos was intrigued with what Jonathan had to say and he leaned forward in his seat. “Yeah,
that sounds right Jon. A secret society, operative word being secret. So how the fuck do you
know about it? Unless you’re a cop.” Carlos grew even more suspicious. “Are you a cop Jonny?
Huh is that it?” Jonathan just chuckled and responded with, “Nah man, I’m not a cop. I was just.
My buddy he told me about it, that’s all.” Carlos dropped his guard and laughed at this statement.
He proceeded to order some more beers and him and Jonathan just sat in the bar knocking them
back one by one, eventually becoming close.
It is 9pm, Carlos is now drunk out of his mind. He makes a crucial mistake, failing to vet
Jonathan any further and still inviting him to a meeting. He gave Jonathan the meeting location
and everything. Jonathan called a cab and went back to his apartment, satisfied with the results of
today’s objective. The next morning, Jonathan wakes up hungover but eager to continue working.
He starts by learning as much as he can about grottos, what to expect and who exactly the south
east grotto was. He learns that a grotto within the Church of Satan is a clandestine association, a
gathering of Satanists who lived in close proximity to each other. This shocks Jonathan and also
amuses him. In the police database, these groups were often dismissed as a bunch of harmless
idiots with outrageous beliefs who got into trouble a lot for slaughtering livestock in public
spaces. Other than that, the police had identified no immediate threats. Only keeping an eye on
special interest individuals within these groups. Jonathan knew that this was no coincidence. If

the Devil’s den really did exist, he figured that Carlos and this South east grotto was his way in.
He spent more time researching these groups after that, spending several hours searching every
site available, every database, everywhere.
The day of the gathering arrived. Jonathan was prepared this time. Ready for any
surprises. When he arrived, he was greeted at the door by someone calling themselves ‘High
priest'. They asked for a password. Jonathan then utters some stupid phrase given up to him by
Carlos when he was drunk. “ábreme la puerta oh exaltado.” He was worried he might have said it
wrong. "You may enter.” Says the high priest. Soon after, the festivities begin. There are about
30 people in the venue, all led by a black-robed child carrying a candle. In front there is an altar,
decorated with human skulls above it and the Symbol of the Baphomet, the five-star inverted
pentagram. “Surely the human skulls must be fake” Jonathan muses to himself. The room is
quiet. The silence is broken by sound of a gold-coloured gong, which was supposed to be the cue
for the high priest to enter and precede over the ritual they apparently performed at these things.
It is supposed to be a ceremony of chanting and singing, calling the forces of darkness and of
summoning Satan to possess the body of the priest. Participants would then proceed to ask Satan
to grant them their desires. Whether it be- money, new cars, good health or even the destruction
of another person. This all seemed all too ridiculous to Jonathan. He entered with an open mind
and this was still too difficult to take seriously. After it was all over, participants share their
goodbyes and go their separate ways. Jonathan was visibly disappointed. He goes over to meet
Carlos who still hadn’t left yet and conveys his disappointment to him. “Was this it?” Jonathan
exclaims. Carlos busts out laughing and is glad that Jonathan sussed it out. “Nah man this is just
to weed out the posers. Looks like you passed Jonny.” Carlos is proud of himself, as per usual.
The two friends have a drink together. Carlos tells Jonathan more about the underground cult and

the various members that operate it. At the same time Jonathan starts getting concerned that he
might be moving to quickly, this was sure to implode. Carlos invites Jon to meet his crew, the
actual south east grotto. He leads Jonathan through a labyrinth, which eventually gives way to a
hallway leading towards an altar at the end. They arrive to a room where the crew gathered.
Carlos introduces Jonathan to his crew, the three people that will later become the most
important figures in his life: Ambrose, Jennifer and Ruby.

chapters.title: Dr Avery Melblac
chapters.text:
Nineteen, that is how many possible new recruits Avery had in her stable. After their
successful mission at SunCity, Defiance was nearly ready to mount their next strike at The
Devil’s den. Avery was cognizant of the fact that some candidates were just not nearly strong
enough for the fight they had in front of them. Avery and her team succeeded in exorcising every
inmate they absconded with from SunCity and Overcity women’s correctional, but they had to
throw some fish back in the ocean. Five weeks in and nineteen had dwindle down to just four
people. The rest were all given new identities and relocated somewhere new where knowledge of
the crimes which they committed while under the influence of demon possession was a not
known. Most of them were determined to be deviants in the traditional sense, meaning they were
not possessed in the first place. In these cases, they were merely returned to the police. During
their incursion on SunCity, Defiance had prior knowledge of a list that was being kept in a secret
safe at the prison. The list contained information which was vital to the dismantling of the
Devil’s den’s ultimate goals. It was the complete list of inmates whom the Devil’s den had
demonic contracts with all across various prisons within the nation. With this list, Defiance
would be in a great position to liberate these People and loosen the Devil’s den’s hold on the
prison system in this region. Avery had been tasked with acquiring the information that would
enable them to destroy the Devil’s den. Going forward, she knew she will have to tread lightly.
She would use all caution during this next mission. Avery was preparing the new Deviants
mentally. Preparing them to face a challenge that would be far more challenging and terrifying
than anything they would ever have done previously. She spent countless hours fortifying them

psychologically to begin their duties within Defiance as the new class of Deviants. Her team
included: Sarah May, Miles Kelly, Amala Jones and Steven grant. All of these people were once
monsters in their own right. Hopefully they would be willing and able to channel that same
energy to a greater cause. As long as these Deviants are willing to dedicate themselves to
Defiance, nothing is impossible. Avery was very confident in their abilities. Although they were
criminals, they are intelligent people who understood and could work together in a small group.
She could already see that they would make fine additions to the organisation. They also carried
a heavy burden. Avery knew that they all wanted a normal life. They were all trying to live
normal lives and achieve happiness for the future, despite whatever trials they faced before.
Avery hoped they would succeed in their endeavours, they deserved it. She looked around at her
fellow Deviants, some of them were already looking around the room, assessing the situation.
One person caught her attention: a tall woman with long brunette hair, dressed in a black leather
jacket, boots and a red shirt. Something about her eyes made Avery think she could relate. The
woman spoke quietly to her comrades, her voice sounded familiar, almost as if they had met
before. Avery was not sure though. It did not matter. She shook her head to clear her thoughts.
These people would have to wait for that normal life for now. They were about to be thrown into
a world of insanity. Avery believed she could count on their loyalty. She had seen it before,
when a person no longer has the influence of a demon on them, they had clear minds. This was
not all positive. They had perfect recall of all there devious things they did when they were
merely puppets. The guilt does not go away. This opportunity that Defiance gives them, the
opportunity to help others like them, it gives them a sense of redemption. They become
dedicated to the core fundamentals that Defiance is based upon. They deliver, putting in
maximum effort. And since deviants are highly trained and nurtured, this effort goes a long way.

The deviants were all gathered in the briefing area as Avery laid out their plan. “Ok group, we
are team 6. There are 6 teams in total, each being a critical piece of the bigger whole. Our
mission: infiltrate this building.” She says while pointing at a blueprint of a maximum-security
prison.
“The key is to blend in well with your surroundings, as a group, get close to the facility,
infiltrate without alerting anybody. We need a way inside and we need to do it quickly. We don’t
want to attract attention, so our priority is the guards and their routines. Once we’re inside, we’ll
split up. We should be able to infiltrate the entire building without arousing suspicion. From
there on we should only focus on finding what we’re looking for. I will lead the infiltration
mission because I know the layout better than anyone else here. We need to find the central
command centre, the main office. Once we have the location, we can then set off the explosives,
The objective is to find the inmates on this list and lead them safely to the bus that will be parked
at the side of the building. Is everybody clear?” Avery finishes the briefing. Everyone nods
affirmatively. “Is that all?” Says Sarah sarcastically. She did not think any of this was simple at
all. The Deviants are all called to the training arena by Atticus Finch to go over each of their
roles one more time. Sarah feels a bit nervous in the beginning, but her confidence soon begins
to grow as the other deviants start taking on roles. They each try their best and in the process
manage to impress Finch. First up was Miles. He had been possessed by a demon of the Aeshma
classification. They are the demons of wrath, rage and fury. Miles was exorcised using an A-
class ritual that expelled the dark energy associated with Aeshma but left him with its abilities.
Miles was able to master up incredible power and durability when his emotions were stimulated.
He closed his eyes and focused on his breathing and how he was feeling. When his ability was
unleashed he became a power house, able to take down men twice his size with little effort. He

was the muscle. Couple that with his natural ability to be unassuming and highly charismatic and
he was an efficient deviant. He stood beside Sarah, giving her directions. When she asks him
questions about his technique he always tells her everything he knows and does. She appreciates
that Miles is helping her. Next it was Amala’s turn. She was a beautiful, petite woman who looks
like she could be in her late twenties or early thirties. She is extremely smart, she managed to
figure out exactly where the cell blocks were located thanks to some computer hacking she did a
few weeks ago. She can easily navigate around and find the prisoners. She had been a host for an
Abigor demon. Within the lore, an Abigor demon was depicted as a demon of the superior order,
conjured for his power to foretell the future and to provide military aid and advice. He is usually
painted to resemble a handsome knight or a ghostly spectre riding a winged horse. For all their
knowledge, the research team at Defiance still had many gaps in their knowledge of
Demonology, but they made due with what they had, which was still a lot. Amala could not
foretell the future as of yet but she was a highly talented strategist. She had been the one to assist
Dr Avery in the planning for this mission. Next up was Steven, another Deviant who was
recruited after being taken out of jail by Defiance. His ability was a lot simple. He had a Surgat,
a minor demon who simply opens anything locked. After testing it out on a few locked doors in
the facility, he was set to go. Lastly there was Sarah. Hers was a bit complicated. Sarah and Mr
Finch were keeping it from everybody else, but Sarah was being haunted by the powerful demon
king Naberius. Her abilities were still undetermined. Avery was not convinced that Sarah was
ready for this mission. Mr Finch managed to sway her, telling Dr Avery that Sarah was highly
knowledgeable, almost supernaturally so. She read and quickly absorbed hundreds of books in a
short period of time. She knew basically every evocation, every spell, every ritual and herb ratio.
Atticus argued that she could be a great asset on the field with everything she knew about most

things. Avery relented and told Atticus that Sarah could be considered for this particular mission.
She felt that her skills would be suited for the task at hand. This was an incredible collection of
individuals. Their valour as a team in a real situation was yet to be tested. Dr Avery met with Dr
Andrew Reed to iron out the plan. She was his right hand. The trust they had in one another was
vital. She needed to ensure he fully believes in her decisions. They began discussing possible
approaches to infiltrating the facility and setting the explosives. Both agreed that hers would be
the team that would be the most difficult to identify, given their individual abilities. The success
of this team was essential.
On the day of the mission, teams one through four all got in the getaway bus and headed
towards the prison. They packed their vehicle behind an ally besides the prison building. It was
obscured from view but could still be identified as it was packed in an awkward position. These
teams consisted of two members per team. Teams one and team two stayed in the bus. The first
team was responsible for driving and navigating, respectively. Team two were the herders. When
the bus pulled up on the exploded side of the building, they would be responsible for shuffling in
all the inmates onto the bus. Teams three and four were the decoy. Should the alarm alert the
cops, they would pose as the first responders. Team six was infiltration. Team five would
intercept the real authorities before they even got to the scene, delaying them and buying more
time for the teams. On paper this plan had its flows but it was the best they had. Their best was
often enough. Team six gets into position, led by Dr Avery. She arrives at the gate with fake
papers showing she had a psychiatric appointment with an inmate today. She was requested by
one of the inmates. They had an inside man who made this more plausible. Steven was posing as
her driver. Miles and Amala, her nurses. The four of them waited patiently for the guard at the
front gate to open up. He did and allowed them entry into the prison. From that point they had no

problem moving through the building. As they walked down the hallways, Sarah directed them
from a van outside that had access to the camera feeds from inside the prison, playing a looped
video to fool the guards monitoring the security screens. Avery kept glancing back to make sure
the coast was clear. Every step closer to getting to their destination filled her with a sickening
feeling deep within her. Her gut told her something terrible was going to happen. She could not
explain why but her instincts wouldn’t let her go against her intuition. They eventually arrived at
their destination and slipped inside the main office room. There were two guards on duty, as they
expected. Miles knocks them out by hitting them both on the head really hard. Avery went to
check the window, ensuring it was sealed shut before turning to the group. “Ok guys, I am going
to give you each one second to change into your uniforms. Amala, go get our inmates, take
Steven with you he’ll take care of the locks, I’ll stay here with Miles and set up the explosives.
I’ll see you later.” Amala turns and leaves the room, her hands shaking slightly. Her and Steven
were both now dressed in guard’s uniform and were rushing through every locked gate and door
in front of them. With Sarah in their ears directing them to avoid any stray guards. After a long
trek, Amala leads Steven to a small storage room just off the hallway where the prisoners’
wardens keep their belongings. She carefully pulls the door open and peeks inside. She signals
Steven to follow her in, and then closes the door softly. “Ok Steven, we have to stay in here for
at least five minutes. The guards on this section do a walk by every few minutes. We have to stay
in here until they pass, then we can quickly go through those gates over there. You think you can
unlock it, Steven?” Amala can tell that Steven is even more nervous than she is. Before she could
even ask him if he can do it for the second time, he has already opened the door silently with his
powers. She found his abilities to be quite amusing but respected their usefulness. On the furthest
side of the prison, Avery and Miles were setting up the explosives. In the middle of it she started

reminiscing about how far she has come, from being a mild-mannered psychiatrist and now
playing with explosives while leading a prison break with a group of demon fuelled young adults.
She quickly snapped out of it and remained focused on the task at hand. One mistake and she
would not only blow up the mission but herself and Miles included. She glanced over to Miles.
He seemed to be trying to control himself, his face expressionless and his body tensed. He was
clearly anxious and worried. Avery could feel his anxiety and fear radiating off of him.
Regardless, he was doing his part. The plan was going off without a hitch thus far. She decided
to start talking to Miles to ease his nerves, hoping it would help him calm down a little. “Hey
Miles?” He turned to face her. “I’m glad that you are working with us. I know this isn’t easy on
you and if you ever want to talk about anything or just vent, I’m always willing to listen. But this
will be very dangerous and it won’t be fun, so let’s try our best to not screw this one up, ok?”
Miles did not say anything. He just nodded his head. She smiled at him and continued speaking.
“Okay Miles, once the signal is up, we carry..well you carry these two somewhere safe. Amala
and Steven should be on their way here with 10 of our demis.” He looks at her intently and
mutters, “I hope they come willingly. By the way what is a Demi?” Avery chuckles. “Oh, that’s
a new name for the possessed. I have been work shopping it, do you like it?” Miles nods at her
and turns his gaze back to the door. A loud clatter could be heard outside. Amala and Steven
come charging in. “Hurry, they are right behind us. I timed it so the guards making their next
round miss them. Now let’s blow our way out of here!” Amala says. Avery signals to team one
and team two to start their engine. She starts the timer:10 seconds on the clock. Miles quickly
grabs the two unconscious guards and removes them from the blast zone. After the 10 seconds
there is a huge explosion. A hole is blown through the side of the building. Alarms start to go off,
it was pandemonium. Every guard in the immediate vicinity is running towards the sound of the

explosion. Right behind them the ten inmates on their heels, running away towards the hole in
the wall. The bus gets in position and team two enters the game. They come through the wall and
begin evacuating the inmates, with Amala, Steven and Miles in tow. Avery is the last one
through the hole. Once in the bus, team two drives off with haste. Just as they are driving away,
teams three and four arrive in their police vehicles making sure to obscure the bus driving off.
Teams three and four are made up of actual law enforcement and their main duty is to remain at
the scene and keep everyone distracted so that the rest of the teams can get away easy. Sarah is
right behind them in the van driving towards the first rendezvous point. In the bus, everyone is
cheering at mission success except Avery Melblac. She had done this enough times to realise that
the mission was not truly complete until they have completely vanished. The team finally makes
it to the first rendezvous point despite deliberate slow driving maintained to remain
inconspicuous. Once there, Avery let’s out a sigh of relief. She finally felt safe and she lets her
guard down. She takes the list of names they had and goes through the bus checking attendance.
“When I call out your name say here.” She says to the inmates. At the back of the bus, Amala is
busy on her laptop. She is accessing the prison’s private database checking ID photo for each of
the men they had taken. While busy searching, she realises that there are at least five names that
do not match the faces of the guys they had in the bus. The tone in the bus shifts. Amala begins
to sweat, struggling to convey this new development to Dr Avery. “What’s wrong? Is everything
okay?” Miles asks. Amala pauses for a second before replying, “We have five people missing
from the list.” Amala hands Miles the list and he begins to look through her laptop looking for
IDs matching the inmates in the bus. When he notices it, his blood begins to boil. He turns
rageful. He initially tries to suppress it, fearing what would happen to his friends if he went all
out with the rage. Dr Avery is at the front, none the wiser and busy with calling out the names

from the original list. Miles then gestures to Steven to come closer. “Hey man you think you
could unlock the back of the bus? We need to get out of here.” Whispers Miles. Before anything
could happen, an inmate burst out of his sit, breaking the metal poles that formed part of the sit.
“Look out!” Miles screams at Dr Avery while charging at the inmate. The man turns quickly to
catch Miles by the neck and proceeds to throw him out of the window like a ragdoll. He gets
larger by the second, his skin turning grey and scaly. Before Avery could react, he jumps onto
her, pinning her to the ground. Choking her and tearing bits of her skin off with the claw like
appendages he has grown from his fingers. Everyone else scrambles back, screaming in terror.
Amala screams at the top of her lungs, her voice growing weaker as she does. Miles is on the
ground on top of broken glass. He is still alive but barely conscious. “This isn’t over!” Miles
shouts, with anger evident in his tone. There’s screeching coming from behind the bus. It was
Sarah pulling up in the truck. She sees Miles on the ground, bleeding and barely moving.
Without thinking twice, she runs to him. As she comes up behind him, she stops suddenly when
she hears him scream again. “Sarah! Get away from me!” She continues to approach slowly. The
sounds around her grow louder and louder. Sarah continues forward. “Miles please! Don’t make
me go!” He looks up at her pleadingly, knowing full well that she would never leave him. She
