was too close. “I’m sorry I love you…just please run! Please don’t get involved! I’ll be fine.” He
says as he turns around, facing her fully, with a smile on his lips. “No, you’re not leaving me
Miles! If you die, I will never forgive myself for leaving you alone.” Tears stream from her eyes
as she tries to reach Miles with her arms to pull him close. Miles puts his hands up in surrender
trying his hardest to reassure her that he is ok. He manages to get her to stop crying and backs
away. Something in Sarah Snaps, her demeanour changes completely. She is no longer afraid.
She quickly recites something into Miles ears. Miles’ injuries begin to heal. Sarah let’s go of him

and moves towards the enemy. She was determined to vanquish them. Two of the inmates in the
bus swarm her while the big monstrous creature holds Dr Avery down. With a single word:
“Inhabilis.” Sarah sends the two men flying. Amala and Steven are on the floor of the bus scared
out of their minds. The other inmates were still sitting in their sits, flabbergasted at what they
were witnessing. Dr Avery was being suffocated by the big grey, scaly beast. Sarah does not
appear intimidated at all. She brazenly walks towards him and begins uttering a familiar
invocation “Exorcizamus te, omnis immundus spiritus, omnis satanica potestas, omnis incursio
infernalis adversarii……” Before she could finish this petition invocation, she suddenly falls to
her knees grasping at her chest. He body becomes hot and hotter until the sleeves on her jacket
spontaneously caught on fire. A now healed Miles runs over to her and puts the flames out. As
Sarah lied there coughing her lungs out, the big creature stops mindlessly chocking Dr Avery,
almost like he was given an order to stop. From the wreckage of the bus, a man with slick black
her and a confident grin on his face walks out. He stares at Miles and Sarah devilishly. Sarah’s
now exposed skin burned to the touch, causing Miles to Instinctively let go of her. This man was
the one producing the heat. The beast walks out right behind him with Dr Avery on his shoulder
like she weighed nothing at all. Miles looks at him intently and exclaims, “Who are you?”
“My name is Damien Videl, and you are?” He responds mockingly. Damien displayed a
smug attitude, as if he was always the most interesting person in a room. This worked for him
seeing as how that was often the case. He placed his fingers on his lips and blew a whistle. “Hey
you two in there, I’d probably get out if I were you, unless you want to get turned into toast.” He
says to Steven and Amala. Steven unlocks the back of the bus and he and Amala exit. The
remaining inmates try to get out as well but Damien gives them a look and they know to stay put.
The bus started smoking and then fully caught on fire. Avery and her team look on in horror as a

bunch of people they were meant to save die a brutal and agonising death, burning alive as their
screams echoed through the flames. Damien steps towards them as if taking a stroll along the
sidewalk with some friends on a pleasant summer day. “What a gorgeous day.” He says. Dr
Avery looks on totally defeated. “You’re their number two, aren’t you? God what did we do to
deserve you.” She chuckles.
Back at the prison, teams four and five are still at the scene providing a cover up. They
had no idea what was happening at the rendezvous and so they continued their part of the
mission as if nothing had gone wrong. The warden demanded to have a word with them in his
office, apparently to discuss where they were with any leads. The men obliged and casually
headed to see the warden. This casual attitude was an essential tool in selling their cover. It was
obvious they had been working hard and had done well in the field. The warden, sensing this
relaxed attitude, asked what they had found out so far about the escape. The men gave a detailed
report. The warden took them to task for letting the escape happen in the first place. He angrily
asked them to live his office, but when they went for the door, it was locked. The warden let’s
out an incongruous laugh. “It was fun playing along but fun’s over now boys.” He says to them.
Shots come through the door killing them all right there and then. Back at the rendezvous,
Damien pulls out a pack of smokes from his prison blues and puts one cigarette in his mouth.
The four guys who made up teams one and two all managed to get out of the bus before it burned.
Damien walks over to them, cigarette in his mouth. He looks at them for a moment, his cigarette
then lights up, he takes three drags and then throws the rest of it at those guys immediately
lighting them on fire. Before their screams can become annoying, his cohorts shoot them all in
the head with their own weapons. After that they begin loading Sarah May, Miles Kelly, Amala
Jones and Steven grant into the van. One of the men with Damien asks, “What about her?” While

gesturing at an injured Avery Melblac. Without even looking back at her, Damien answers
“Leave her, she is not on the list.” Damien Videl drove off with the Deviants team, taking them
back to the Devil’s Den.

Chapter 10
Dr Andrew Reed
When Dr Andrew Reed tells the story of his life, he will return again and again to the
same horrifying tableau – a satanic cult that murdered him and the friends that brought him back
to life. With its details of devil worship, torture and human sacrifice, his is a story quite strange.
Though surprisingly not very different to scores of others that have suffered in recent years from
the abuses of the Devil’s den. Yet, as gruesome and macabre as it may sound, his story has one
significant flaw – the truth. It is just too unbelievable. Andrew has always known that his life
would end in a tragic death. He was ready for it. He lived long enough to learn the true nature of
the evil that had consumed this world and he hoped he would only live long enough to witness
it’s demise. Still, he lives his life in constant fear. Fear that his crusade to vanquish the very
tenets of evil would fail. Fear that the people he cared about and entrusted with his crusade
would fall victim to these horrors of evil. Fear of being alone. But, as much as he fears these
things happening, that is not the real reason he hates his own existence. He has had to resort to
less than moral things, done terrible things in the name of righteous good and in order to fight the
darkness. He gets lost in it sometimes. He always wonders if it is worth losing his own soul in a
bid to protect the soul of everyone else. Now the people who were willing to put themselves in
danger for his cause are in trouble. It has been a day and a half since Andrew was expecting the
Deviants to return from their mission. He has heard nothing from them since. He sits in his office
anxiously waiting, praying they would return, praying that they were not dead. The only thing
Andrew can do for now is to wait. Waiting is the one thing he can never seem to do well.

Andrew hears through his police connections about a tragedy and he wonders if his
people were somehow involved. Four people believed to be members of a Satanic cult have been
arrested following the death of a woman from injuries inflicted during a satanic gathering. The
victim, identified by police as Rosaline Jean, was a 43- year-old Haitian mother of two children,
aged eight and five years. Her Spanish husband was among those detained, together with two
other men and a woman, in a villa near the resort town of Bennie. What got Andrew’s attention
were the suspects’ identities. He noticed that their names were also on the list that Avery and the
team were going off of on their mission. Three of those suspects were all already supposed to be
in prison. According to a local police spokesperson, the victim and suspects were celebrating
black magic rituals on Tuesday night when Rosaline went into a coma after being wrapped in a
blanket and almost suffocating. Her body was covered in knife wounds and burn marks. She died
the following day. Police found bloody knives, candles and other satanic artefacts in the villa,
which neighbours said was often used for by the suspects. Andrew looks at the names and photos
of the suspects on the headline. “This cannot be.” He thought to himself. He goes to his office
and rummages through the endless clutter looking for a copy of the list. “Sid Wells, Fargo
Smith…this people are…it is the same people on my list.” He is shocked and angry. He quickly
pieces it together. The whole thing must have been a set up from the beginning. The list was
designed to lure them to the prison. He had no doubts. Andrew realised that his Deviants may
have been captured by the Devil’s den, if they were not already dead. “Dammit.” Andrew cursed
as he looked at the photos of the suspects. He called a meeting with all the other members of
Defiance to try and figure out what to do. They are all gathered at the guildhall. Every important
member of his organisation had collectively come together around the large table that they
frequently used for meetings. Andrew begins to tell brief them all about his suspicions. Everyone

agrees that something drastic needs to be done to stop the Devil’s den, that there must be no
further losses at their hands. Martin Creswell, a long-time friend of Andrew’s and a founding
member of Defiance is present at the meeting. He informs them of a woman found by police
whose description matches that of Dr Avery Melblac. “The 29-year-old was discovered by a
member of the public near the steps between Frasier and Union Road just before 06:00. Police
have been trying to trace the woman’s movement between 23:00 Tuesday up until she was found.
She was taken to Saint Belford Hospital.” Martin says as he briefs the group. Andrew insists that
they need to go to the hospital and find this woman. Martin quickly interjects. He argues that
there might be another trap waiting for them. “We need proper planning this time before we just
go rushing in like you and your so-called Deviants did.” Andrew is visibly upset by this
statement. He knew Martin was never in favour of the Deviants program. Everyone in the room
agrees with Martin. “I know Martin, but we don’t have much of a choice. We must do whatever
is necessary to find our people.” Martin is silent for a minute as if debating on something before
speaking again. “Well, I don’t want to get ahead of myself, but I don’t think you should risk it
just yet.” He says. “Why do you say that?” Andrew replies. “Because it is very obvious that there
may be some sort of ambush. I mean we don’t even know that the woman is Dr Melblac.”
Andrew shakes his head disagreeing with him. “I don’t believe this. You’ve been listening to me
all evening, haven’t you? If we don’t find them soon, they are all dead. Don’t tell me you’re
giving up already?” He asks him. “No, no I am not. It isn’t that simple. I’m just saying we should
take things slow. There is too much at stake right now.” Martin insists. Andrew sighs heavily as
he gives up and nods to Martin in agreement. “Alright Martin. But we’ll move forward as soon
as the situation changes. If you have any suggestions for our next step, please let us know now.”

The meeting then ends with everyone agreeing to work harder and keep working until a way
forward is available.
Andrew was beside himself. He decides right then that he must find them himself.
Without the help of Defiance and its vast resources. He could only think of one other person who
could help him. His son, Jonathan, a officer with resources of his own. As Andrew is walking out
of the facility, Atticus Finch approaches him. “Hey, I know what you’re planning to do and I
want to help.” Says Atticus. Andrew respected Atticus and had always valued his knowledge and
skills. “Sure, I could use all the help.” Atticus looks at Andrew and smiles. “Great, what’s the
plan?” He asks. Andrew does not say anything and they continue walking towards the car. They
drove towards Jonathan’s. When they arrive, Jonathan is not pleased to see his father. “What are
you doing here?” He asks. Andrew pleads with his son, telling him about his missing Deviants,
but Jonathan wants nothing to do with it. Atticus insists he hears them out. Finally, Jonathan
relents and lets them in his apartment. He leads the way inside of his house. He sits down in an
armchair and motions for Atticus and Andrew to sit on a couch across from him. Atticus takes
notice of Jonathan’s demeanour and immediately knows that he is hiding something. After some
moments of silence, Jonathan finally breaks it and explains why he wants nothing to do with his
father’s business. “I’m still a cop dad. I know you’ve got something shady going on. You are
making enemies everywhere you turn.” Jonathan says, sounding annoyed. “But Jon, this is no
ordinary case.” Andrew tries to convince Jonathan. “I don’t know how many times I have to tell
you. It is the Devil’s den. It is a place where evil lives and thrives. People do evil things there!
That is why I am asking you to help me. If you don’t help me, I promise you a lot of people will
die.” Andrew begs Jonathan to reconsider. “No! I can’t.” Jonathan exclaims, surprised by his
father’s boldness and insistence on getting involved with them. He is very reluctant to do so.

While Andrew and his son were arguing, Atticus noticed something familiar on Jonathan’s
counter, a manual of sorts, for witchcraft. He recognises where it is from and confronts Jonathan
about it. “What are you doing with a Grimoire? Where did you get it?” Atticus asks Jonathan.
“It’s mine!” Replies Jonathan. Atticus walks up to Andrew and whispers something to him.
“He’s hiding something.” Andrew looks over to see Jonathan glaring. “What have you gotten
yourself involved in Jonathan?”
“Nothing you should concern yourself with. I’m just trying to understand everything
you’ve told me.” Jonathan says in defence. With this statement both father and son turn away
from each other, clearly upset. “This book! It’s only given to new initiates of a coven or Satanist
pact.” Atticus says accusingly. Andrew looks at Jonathan. He was worried. “Did you join a
Satanist group? Does your boss know that you’ve gone undercover?” Andrew knew that is what
Jonathan was probably doing. “No, I haven’t told anyone.” Jonathan responds. His father looks
weirdly proud of him. “Don’t you think this is too dangerous? He has zero experience with this.”
Atticus inquires. Andrew only sees this as an opportunity. He pulls Atticus to the side and tells
him to stay with Jonathan. “You can convince him to help us, I’ll go to the hospital.” Atticus
nods in agreement. Jonathan intends to assist them, only if it can get rid of his father. He figures
that if he helps them then they will leave. “Alright Jon meet Atticus. Atticus, Jon. Now that you
two are acquainted I believe you will work well together. You do plan on helping us right son.”
Jonathan is not confident of this alliance but he agrees to lend a hand anyway he can.
At the hospital, there is a heavy presence of police. There is someone they are guarding.
Andrew arrives at the hospital. He gets the hospital administrator to allow him to search for the
woman identified in the police report. He makes his way into the main hospital ward where he
asks for a nurse to show him the patient in the isolation cell. Andrew enters and finds her lying

unconscious on the bed. He is stunned by the injuries on her body. One of her legs is broken in
several places. She is covered with bruises. She has cuts on her face and hands. She looks
exhausted and weak. Andrew is pleased to discover that it was indeed Dr Avery. He quickly calls
for the nurse and demands they wake her up. They are reluctant to do this as she was still
recovering from her injuries but he tells the nurse that he is an officer and shows her his badge.
“Listen, I work with the police and this woman is important to my investigation, you need to
wake her up.” The nurse is convinced by Andrew’s deception. “I was wondering why there are
so many cops outside.” Says the nurse. She precedes to administer medicine to wake Dr Avery
up. Avery slowly opens her eyes as she regains consciousness. “How are you feeling Melblac?”
Andrew Asks. She replies in a weakened voice “Ughh… My leg hurts, but I guess I can tolerate
the pain.” Andrew frowns a little. “I hope you are being honest.” He says, looking at her.
“Honest? What do you mean?” She replies. Her voice sounds a bit hoarse due to severe
dehydration. “I just don’t want you to be putting on a brave face for me.” Says Andrew. “That
doesn’t make sense, I’m not putting on a brave face.” Avery replies. “You look horrible and
you’re obviously very tired.” Andrew insists. He is really concerned about her. “My leg hurts,
yes, but I’ll live. I’m not stupid. I’ve experienced worse injuries, besides I’m not afraid of pain.”
Avery goes on. Andrew sighs in frustration. “Fine, what happened? Where are the others?” He
asks. Avery tells him what happened. How Miles, Sarah, Steven and Amala were taken and how
the rest were all lost during the chaos. She tells him how she was able to get away and not get
captured or killed. “So, what now?” Avery asks him as she finished explaining. “Well, we’re
going to look for them.”
“How? Are you kidding? We don’t even have a clue where they went!” She exclaims as
she jumps from the bed. Andrew grabs her hand, preventing her from falling. “We’ll figure it out.

We’ll find them and bring them home safely, don’t worry. Trust me, they won’t be hurt.” He
reassures her. A moment of silence passes between them before Avery speaks up again. “I trust
you.” Just at that moment a man walks into the room with a message. “Are you the doctor?”
Asks Andrew. The man simply shook his head indicating that he was not. “I have been instructed
to give you this note.” He hands the note to Andrew and walks out. The note reads: ‘Come to this
location and your friends will be released. The address is 221B Baker St.’ Andrew stares at the
paper, surprised. They both share a shocked expression. “We have to go.” Avery tells him. “Yes,
we do.” Andrew agrees. It turned out the hospital was indeed a trap, just not one Andrew
expected. Avery masters up the energy to leave the hospital. He leg was still quite injured. Both
Dr Andrew Reed and Dr Avery Melblac are prepared for what is to come. They return to
Jonathan’s. Atticus was still with Jonathan, studying him. When they arrive, Jonathan is
surprised to see yet another one of his dad’s weird friends. Atticus sees Dr Avery and hurries to
take her into his embrace “I am so glad you’re alive.” He says. They were never really close but
Atticus had a massive crush on her. He liked how smart she was and how she dedicated her life
to helping others. In the next room, Andrew and Jonathan are having a discussion. He tells him
of the Den’s proposition. “Why do they want you so bad?” Jonathan asks. “There must be
something special about you.” He continues. “It doesn’t matter now.” Andrew tells him. Atticus
enters the room suddenly. “We should go.” He says. Atticus looks at Dr Avery for a brief
moment and then turns away as he leads them down the stairs and out of the building. They
quickly make their way towards Baker Street. Andrew parks his car in the parking lot behind the
building and they continue to enter through the front door. As soon as the door closes, they hear
voices coming from upstairs. Andrew and Avery wait until the voices disappear and then quietly
make their way to the back of the building where a basement door is located. Andrew opens the

door and Avery peers around the corner. She spots a set of stairs leading upwards. She points at
them and Andrew nods in understanding. They begin their journey to the top floor. On their way
there, the sounds of footsteps echoing around the hallway stops them dead in their tracks.
Andrew signals for Avery to stay where she is, he continues forward and listens closely for any
sound. Hearing nothing, he continues forward as silently as possible. At the top of the staircase is
another door, which Andrew opens. Andrew gestures for Avery to remain hidden while he
checks the room. The room is empty apart from a bed and chair. He moves towards the bed,
careful not to disturb it. He notices a small glass bowl on the night stand. He picks it up and sees
that it contains a dark red liquid. The room smells strongly of burnt herbs. Andrew puts the bowl
back on the table and notices the blood-stained clothes next to the bowl. They continue searching
the whole building but no one is there and no one comes. As Avery, Andrew, Atticus and
Jonathan are gathered inside the building, they discuss their next move. Avery wonders why the
Devil’s den did not show. “What is going to happen to the others now.” Atticus asks. Jonathan
suggest he consult the south east grotto to check if they knew anything about this building. He
also recognises the concoction that was left behind, the bowl with the dark red liquid smelled
familiar. It smelled of herbs described in his grimoire. “I think I might get an idea of what they
were doing here.” Atticus frowns deeply, he also knows what happened here. “No need, this was
a sacrificial ritual.” He says with confidence. Andrew questions him. “Sacrifice for what?”
“When a formerly possessed person is sacrificed in this way, the demon that once
possessed them can be brought back.” As soon as he heard this, Andrew had a sudden realisation
why the Devil’s den wanted him so badly. His face is flushed and pale. “let’s get out of here.” He
signals to everyone. “Where are we going?” Asks Avery. “Back to defiance.” Andrew responds.
They all nod in agreement and quickly move through the corridors and out the building. As he

moves, Andrew’s mind starts going over all the possibilities of what the Devil’s might have
planned. ‘I cannot allow them to bring him back. I won’t let them’ Andrew thinks to himself.
The group splits up. Jonathan and Atticus head towards the bar to find Carlos. Andrew and
Avery head toward the defiance facility.

chapters.title: Sarah May
chapters.text:
Sarah May was back inside a cage. But this one was unlike the other cages she has been
in before. It was pleasant. If it were not for the fact she was captured, she would feel right at
home. Her and the others were taken to a lavish manor at the outskirts of town. She was locked
in a room at the topmost floor. The fields surrounding the manor became a mere map beneath
Sarah. She could hear the heavy thud of boots on the creaking wooden floor beneath her. She
stood quietly in this room facing the door, ready to take on whatever came through. The doors
opened and a strikingly beautiful woman stood facing her. Sarah’s guard was up, but the moment
the woman spoke to her, Sarah relaxed. The woman spoke with a sweet cadence. Introducing
herself as Alexandra Turner. “I will be your guide while you stay with us.” Says Alex while
speaking to Sarah. She could not understand why, but Sarah felt she could trust this woman.
Alexandra takes Sarah by the hand and leads her out of the room. She led her through a large
foyer. The floors were made up of bone matt finish ceramic floor tiles arranged in smooth
patterns, and the walls were made of stained glass, slightly and tastefully obscuring additional
views of the field and the mountains to the west. Elegant iron flush lighting fixtures hung from
the ceiling. Unique pendant lighting and a wrap-around staircase brought the whole foyer
together. Everything in the manor was lavish. Sarah found herself awed, and also envious that
she never had been in a place so luxurious. She could feel the stares of many people on her, from
those who were sitting in chairs and lounging around on sofas near the entrance. All these people
seemed like servants. Alexandra addressed them politely. She led Sarah to a beautiful dining
table surrounded by beautiful vases filled with blooming roses. As Sarah sat down at the table, a

maid began bringing food and drinks for her. The entire time, Sarah could not take her eyes off
of the maid. She did not seem to care about the attention given to her, instead she focused all of
her attention on serving Sarah her food. Alexandra looked curiously at Sarah. “You seem rather
fascinated by our maid, aren’t you?” She asked. “She’s amazing!” Sarah replied enthusiastically,
making Alex smile in amusement. “Your friends will be joining us shortly.” Says Alexandra. A
few moments later they all arrive. Miles, Amala, and Steven walk in the dining room looking
afraid and eager to get out of the manor. Alexandra greets them happily as they sit down. The
maid serves them food and drinks. They all enjoy a meal and begin to talk about the events that
transpired in the past couple of days. As they talk, Alexandra watches intently at Sarah’s reaction
to everything. Miles stares at Alexandra with deep loathing in his eyes. “Listen, I don’t care why
you brought us here. You killed my friends and I swear if you try to even put anything in me
again, I will kill you. Do you understand?” He says to her. Alexandra just smiles at his remark.
“Yes, I do understand Miles. I know exactly what you are capable of.” The tone of her voice was
calm and soothing. She did not react to Miles’ words the way he was expecting her to. Miles and
Steven glance at each knowingly. “Do you know where you are?” She asks. “Of course. We are
at the mercy of the Devil’s den.” Steven Answers. Alexandra simply laughs at this assertion,
insisting that that name is inaccurate. “Devil’s den? Who ever came up with that.” Alex muses.
After they have all ate, Alexandra gets up from her sit and instructs them to follow her
somewhere. She leads them to the undercroft. An important part of the manor. The undercroft is
most notable for its central octagonal pier with concave sides. Within each concavity there is an
attached shaft with a rolled molding capital. The vaulting is in two rings supported by the central
pier, columns in the middle and attached to the walls. The vaulting ribs have simple chamfers. A
few steps above the vaulting is an opening in the wall. Inside, there are multiple rooms connected

to the room they just entered. One room is clearly used by someone who spends a lot of time in
there. There are various items scattered around it. Books, papers and tools. A collection of
weapons and armour, swords, knives, bows, spears, shields, guns, and more. The rest of the room
is a mess. There are pieces of clothing and objects everywhere. Alexandra walks to one side of
the room. She pulls open a metal door and reveals another hallway. The hallway is littered with
boxes. Each box is marked with symbols that Sarah cannot identify. Alexandra opens one of the
boxes and pulls its contents out. All the boxes are covered with dust. She picks up another one of
the smaller boxes and examines it. She holds it up to the light. “They are called sigils.” She says.
Her voice is quiet and calm. She places it back down and picks up a bigger one and holds it up to
the light. “Sigil stones. These are valuable, very rare. Whoever used these belonged to an
organisation that believed in magic. When the sigil stone was lost, it caused a chain reaction that
destroyed the entire group.” Alexandra speaks, telling them a story. The room falls silent. “This
organization was called the Black Order.” She finishes.” The sigils all have a different meaning
attached to them. There is a sigil for; Alchemy, another for angels, a sigil for demonology and
many others. Sarah is once again awed by what this manor had to offer. ‘What kind of secrets
does it hide?’ She wonders. “The Black Order was known throughout history. Their members
were strong fighters and mages, but they always had some weakness in their power. The leader
of the order was a Devil. Many believe he was the founder of this organization. He used to use a
black sigil stone to unleash hell fire upon any who would go against them.” Alexandra looks up
from inspecting the sigils. She looks at Sarah and continues, “He was defeated in battle.” She
pauses to look at Sarah again and adds, “Lucifer is the greatest warlock that ever lived in human
history.” She then shows the sigils to Sarah one by one. Sarah stares at the sigils in awe. She
remembers reading about the existence of these sigils in ancient literature. She does not know

what to make of all this information. Miles hears all of this and mutters under his breath, “I guess
know we know why they call you the Devil’s den.”
“You, like everyone else have a deep misunderstanding of the history. The devil, Lucifer,
they are not who you believe they are.” Alexandra retorts with a cold stare on her face. “The
devil you all learn about is nothing but a myth created by humans to frighten themselves. This
so-called Devil’s Den is a place where people like us can gather to unlock the true potential of
our nature. That is all that we are.” Sarah listens intently to every word coming out of
Alexandra's mouth. She feels enamoured by her words. Miles turns towards Sarah and speaks up,
“Can you believe this woman.” Sarah then starts to scold Alexandra. “You say these things but
you kill people indiscriminately. You force people to do evil things and you misuse the power
that you possess. This is where evil lives and you cannot say anything to convince us different.”
She finishes. Sarah was angry. Alexandra looks at her with a blank stare, which causes Sarah to
become even more angry, her voice becoming louder and her words sharper as she continues to
speak. “How can you stand there and act so calm knowing that you’ve murdered so many
innocent people! How can you tell me you’re not evil? Tell me what you think of yourself!”
Sarah shouts. “It doesn’t matter what I think about myself. That’s not going to change the fact
that people need to be taught a lesson. That is just the way of the world we live in.” Alexandra
says. Miles is getting annoyed by this conversation. “Stop acting so calm. You’re going to kill us
all.” He exclaims. “No, I’m not going to kill anyone. If I don’t need to. Just because I brought
you here doesn’t mean I want to harm you.” She calmly replies. Miles continues to stare at her
with a glare. Before either of them can continue talking, Sarah interrupts them. “Then let us
leave!” She says sternly. “Not yet, we want your boss. Him for you.” Alexandra responds

After the conversation at the undercroft, the Deviants group are huddled together by the
fireplace discussing plans to escape. Miles and Amala discuss how they should attack the manor
tomorrow morning. They also discussed ways to sneak out the manor during the day and try to
find a way off the estate. After a while, the whole group goes to bed. In the middle of the night,
Sarah suddenly wakes up, scared and panicking. She is having terrible nightmares. She keeps
shouting and crying, saying horrible things. Finally, she manages to calm himself down enough
to fall asleep again. Sarah was again awakened, this time by the sound of someone calling her
name repeatedly. She slowly opened her eyes. She is surrounded by darkness. Then, she realized
that he was not in her room anymore. Instead, she found herself lying on the cold hard floor
inside the undercroft. At first, she thought she must have fallen unconscious and that it might be
all a dream. Her head throbbed in pain. She stood up to assess her situation. She feels herself
being drawn to the room Alexandra showed them earlier. She takes the lantern hanging next to
the entrance and lights it, illuminating the room. Sarah begins to walk inside, looking around for
the object that was calling to her. She spots one of the boxes with the sigils on them, picking up
one of the boxes. She opens it. Inside there is a number of materials, but what caught Sarah’s eye
was the book inside. It was the book of shadows, a book of witchcraft which contained the
beliefs, rituals, witchcraft laws and ethics, herbal and healing lore, chants, incantations, dances,
spells, divination methods and miscellaneous topics that serve as a guide for witches practicing
their craft and religion. A loud bang makes Sarah drop the book and the lantern, sending glass
shards flying everywhere. She jumps away from the book and looks around frantically,
wondering what startled her. She sees movement from her left and turns to see what it is. It was
Alexandra walking out of a doorway. Sarah quickly tries to put out the flames from the lantern
but she fails. Seeing this, Alexandra rushes over to help. She puts the flames out. She then helps

Sarah clean up the glass before returning the lantern to its position. As they finish cleaning up the
glass shards from the lantern, Alexandra asks Sarah if she is alright. Sarah nods her head. “I’m
fine.” She responds. Alexandra gives Sarah a warm smile. “Good, I am glad to hear that. Now
you can tell me why you are in here so late?” Alexandra asks. Sarah looks around at the
destroyed room and unsure what excuse she could give. “I’m sorry for being in here. My
sleep…I’m just restless.” She frowns. Alexandra let’s out a soft chuckle and replies, “Don’t
worry about it.” She then takes the book of shadows and exits, leaving Sarah alone in the
undercroft. Moments later Sarah walks over to one of the many doors leading to the outside.
Upon pushing it open she finds herself surrounded by the moonlight. She steps out into the cool
breeze and inhales deeply. The air smells different. It reminds her of pine trees and fresh grass.
She takes in a deep breath and lets the cool breeze wash over her. She continues walking through
the garden, passing by various flower beds, bushes, and ponds. An unexpected view for a place
ran by Satanists. She passes by a large oak tree and stops. Sarah grabs the rope that hangs from
the branches and climbs up onto the branch. She begins to climb the tree until she reaches the
highest point of the branch and gets a clear view of the stars. She watches as the moon reflects
off the surface of the pond, causing the surface to ripple and reflect the stars. Sarah continues
climbing until she is at the top of the tree. She sits atop this tree for a while, reminiscing about
her past. As she does. Naberius’ voice speaks to her. “So close, we had it at the palm of our
hands.” He says. “And yet it still ended in failure.” He continues. Sarah frowns before she replies
to him. “Sure.” She continues to sit in silence, staring up at the stars. A noise catches her
attention. “Who’s there?! Show yourself!” Sarah yells. As she shouts, Naberius appears behind
her and he starts laughing at her. Sarah screams in horror, trying to not fall of the branch. She
ends up falling to the ground anyway. Naberius continues laughing while approaching Sarah

slowly. He stands in front of her, looking down at her in amusement. Unexpectedly, Miles walks
up to Sarah. Seeing her on the ground and shivering from fear. “What are you doing down
there?” Asks Miles. Sarah is the only one who can see the demon who haunts her. Apart from
Atticus, nobody else knows Naberius is still inside her tormenting her. Sarah gets up and dusts
the dirt off her clothes. “What’s up?” She asks Miles while attempting to hide her embarrassment.
“We’ve finally figured out a way out of here.” He replies. “Well good, tell me about it.” She says
to him. Sarah glances over at Naberius who is standing next to Miles. He smiles evilly at her.
She does not return the smile and continues looking at him. “We can finally get out of here.
Come on.” Miles tells her while walking back inside. Sarah walks alongside him, still avoiding
the demon who follows them closely. She tries to ignore the feeling that he is watching her.
“This way.” Says Miles as they turn left towards the door that leads to the Undercroft. Once they
reach the path, the demon Naberius disappears. Miles and Sarah walk into the room to find
Amala and Steven. “Are you guys ready?” Miles asks them. Steven and Amala are eager to
reveal the plan they came up with to escape from the Devil’s den. “There is a hidden pathway to
the other side of the undercroft that leads to an exit near the lake. We will use that entrance to
sneak out of the mansion.” Miles tells Sarah. She looks sceptical.“How did you find that out?”
She asks. “Tell her Amala.” Miles says. “Well, Steven used his powers and opened that door
over there. We found a bunch of stuff inside, including plans for this very building. I deciphered
a bit of it and spotted the hidden pathway.”
Sarah scoffs. “That sounds totally insane.” She replies, slightly more sceptical. “Oh, trust
me, it’s not!” Steven reassures her. Amala then explains further. “The path we have here is made
from a series of traps. Each trap requires a key. So, the key should be located somewhere nearby
if we needed it. But we have Steven. We can go right out the back entrance.” Amala continues,

“Once we’re gone, it should take them a while to notice.” Miles nods his head approving of her
plan. Sarah finally relents and agrees it could work. “When are we doing this?” She asks. “They
let us move freely so it should be quite easy to sneak back here after breakfast tomorrow
morning.” Says Miles. “Wait, we are going just when it’s light out? Won’t they see us?” Sarah
wonders out loud. “That is why they won’t be expecting it.” Steven arrogantly replies. Sarah
stares at him with an irritated expression on her face. She knows that Steven may not be the
smartest guy in the world, but he is not stupid either. He would never dare try something so crazy
without knowing how stupid it would be. “But what if they see us?” She asks. Miles shakes his
head, “They won’t. They don’t have any reason to believe that we would actually try something
like this.” He tells her. She stares at him sceptically, unsure whether he really believes in his own
words or if he is just saying that to keep her quiet. However, she decides not to argue with him.
“Ok.” She says. “Ok.” Replies Amala. Sarah sighs and looks at Steven. “Where exactly is this
hidden passage?” She questions him. Steven is apparently the one who found the clue to the
hidden pathway. “On the other side of the wall.” Steven answers. “Let’s do it!” Amala exclaims
excitedly. Everyone agrees and heads back into their rooms ready for the next day.
The next morning, the gang meets up at the dining hall. Alexandra is there, she tells them
that the plan to exchange their freedom for Dr Reed is already underway and that they will be
living soon. Sarah and the rest of the deviants do not intend on letting this happen. By this time
tomorrow they will have already escaped and Dr Reed will not need to give himself up. Sarah
now feels confident about the plan. While they wait for breakfast to end, everyone prepares
themselves for what is to come. Sarah remains calm and collected. She wants everything to go
according to plan, otherwise, someone whom she admired would be sacrificed for her. That
thought alone makes her sick. After breakfast, Sarah and the others leave to begin the escape.

Sarah walks down the hallway and enters the kitchen as she always did after breakfast since
being here. In the middle of the table is a piece of paper with writing on it. The paper reads: ‘I
know you are interested in the Book of Shadows. I took it from you because it is too dangerous
in inexperienced hands. If you are really serious about exploring your gifts, I can teach you to
practice.’ The note was from Alexandra. The rest of Sarah’s team were keeping to their usual
routine to try and not be suspicious until their escape. After which, they all gathered in the
undercroft. Except for Sarah. “Where the hell is she!” Amala exclaims. Miles and the rest look
concerned. They could not leave without Sarah, but she was not showing up and that threatened
their whole escape plan. Sarah was still in the kitchen staring blankly at the note. She was
remembering the time she first got possessed. She remembers how she was playing around with
her grandmother’s grimoire and how she believed the entire thing was a joke. She accidentally
opened herself up to something sinister and it led her down a dark path. She was seriously
contemplating accepting Alexandra’s offer. Maybe this way nothing bad would ever happen to
her again. Suddenly she gets knocked back to reality when she remembers her friends need to get
out of this place. She quickly struts to the undercroft to catch up with them. The group waits
impatiently for Sarah to arrive. As Sarah arrives at the Undercroft, she notices the demon
Naberius lurking outside the entrance to the undercroft. She approaches the demon, glaring
daggers at him. “Why aren’t you leaving me alone?” Sarah shouts angrily at the demon.
Naberius simply turns its head away from Sarah and smirks. Sarah continues yelling at the
demon as she tries to get closer to it, “I’m telling you to leave me alone now!” She repeats,
growing angrier by the second. Suddenly Sarah feels a hand on her shoulder. She flinches in
surprise and turns around. She sees Miles holding onto her, staring at her coldly. “Who are you
yelling at?” He whispers worryingly. Sarah begins to tear up from guilt and shame. Miles

removes his hand from her shoulder, taking a step back. “Come on let’s go, we will talk about
this later?” His voice trembles and his eyes show concern. Sarah lowers her head. “Yes, Miles.”
She says. He turns and walks away, allowing her to follow him inside the undercroft. “Finally!”
Steven says. The group crawls together towards the secret pathway leading them outside.
One by one they all squeeze through the passage. It is narrow but they manage to make it
safely through. They crawl beneath for a long time. Finally, after what feels like hours of
crawling through the murk, they get to the end of the passage. They climb out, up and over the
fence covering the entrance and finally, the exit the manor. “Stay close” Miles instructs. They all
stay huddled together. Once they were far enough from the property they relax and take a
moment to admire their effort. “We are almost far away enough! Just keep moving forward.”
Miles reminds his friends. “Did you hear that?” Steven wonders as he begins to hear noises
coming from the forest. “It is probably nothing, let’s just keep walking.” Says Sarah. As they
continue to walk, the group becomes increasingly aware that they may not be alone. Their
attention immediately goes to the source of the sound. Standing before them is a tall, muscular
and very menacing looking guard. The guard’s gaze shifts from Miles to Steven and then to the
girls behind them. The smirk that appears upon his face causes the skin on the back of the girls
neck to become taut. The guard suddenly steps forward, causing the girls to stumble backwards
in fright. “Step back!” Miles tells his friends as he gathers the power to take out the guard.
Suddenly, the guard raises his fists and swings it towards Miles. Before Miles can react to the
attack, the guard has struck Miles across the stomach causing to fall down to his knees. The
guard then proceeds to strike Miles repeatedly on the head. Blood spurts everywhere from Miles’
head. His friends stand there in horror watching the scene unfold. The guard lifts his fists to
strike Miles a final time, but stops when Sarah runs towards him, throwing herself at him. The

guard catches her mid-air before she can hit him. She loses consciousness. The guard looks
puzzled for a brief moment before realizing what happened. He releases Sarah from his grasp
and stands up facing the group. “You will pay for your insolence!” He threatens. Miles interrupts,
standing up. He musters up the power to fight back. He begins throwing blows at the guard. Who
just shrugs it off. Soon after, Steven and Amala start picking stones and throwing them at the
guard. He starts getting annoyed. He takes out a gun from his holster, pointing it at the team. He
ready to shoot them all right there. Then, the unconscious Sarah opens her eyes. Miles is happy
to see Sarah alright but when he sees her, he becomes concerned and afraid for her. He sees her
eyes turn bright red and her Hair begin to float in the air. “Sarah!’ He shouts. She lets out a
scream, which creates a shockwave that knocks out the guard. A few seconds later Sarah’s red
aura dissipated. She stops glowing and her hair comes back down. She collapses to the ground.
Her friends rush over to her side in a panic. Miles calls out to her worriedly. “Sarah!" He
screams her name but she does not respond. Miles kneels beside her, inspecting her injuries,
trying to see if she has been hurt badly by anything. She opens her eyes slightly. “Miles.” She
says in a weakened voice. When he finally hears her speak, Miles lets out a sigh of relief.
Meanwhile back at the manor, there is someone at the door. A servant goes to see who it is and
to let them in. She opens the door and standing there is Dr Andrew Reed.

chapters.title: Dr Andrew Reed
chapters.text:
Andrew arrives at Defiance headquarters with Avery after the harrowing discovery that
his enemies wanted to revive a monster he believed had been destroyed for good. The Devil’s
den had given him an ultimatum. He must give himself up for the freedom of his Deviants. But
when he arrived at the location of the exchange, all he found was evidence of a sinister ritual.
After piecing it together, Andrew figured out that it was their way of telling him why they really
wanted him. It was not a coincidence. Andrew does not believe that they made such a huge
mistake to leave behind the ritual material at that building. Andrew cannot help but feel
responsible for what happened to his Deviants. “Damn them all!” He cursed. At Defiance,
Andrew tells Dr Avery to go and research any evidence of these kinds of rituals being performed
while he goes and alerts the other members of this new development. “Sure, but won’t the others
want to hear that I am ok?” Avery asks Andrew. “They don’t care!” He answers. “Well…ok.”
She hesitates. “I’ll call you when I find something.” Avery states before leaving Andrew. When
Andrew finally meets up with his partners, they are busy discussing a different case. “Dr Reed,
come sit.” Says Martin. Andrew sits down, feeling defeated. “What’s wrong Dr Reed?” Inquires
Charles. Andrew does not answer but instead just stares into space. “The doctor is fine. His
investigation will be resolved soon enough.” Martin answers on Dr Reed’s behalf. Martin sighs
in relief. “I hear you found Dr Melblac.” He asks. “Yes, but I’ve found a something else too!”
Andrew replies horrified. “Is it good or bad news?” Martin asks anxiously. Andrew pauses,
thinking about what to tell Martin. Eventually, he decides to tell him the truth. “It might be a bit
of both. I can’t guarantee that I will make any progress with it.” Andrew says. “Can’t you tell us

more about what it is?” Charles asks. Andrew shakes his head. “Not yet, there is something I
must do first.” He states. “And what might that be?” Martin questions. “I must go and investigate
a solution to a problem.” Andrew admits reluctantly. Charles frowns, “A solution? What is the
problem?” Andrew does not respond. He simply stands up, turns and begins to walk away.
“Wait!” Charles calls out. Andrew looks back at Charles questionably, waiting for him to say
what he needs to say. “I heard that there were some strange movements from the den tonight.”
Charles explains.“Could it be connected to that?” He asks. Andrew is taken aback by Charles’
question. He thinks hard about whether or not to just tell them what is going on. He eventually
comes up with a conclusion, “I don’t know Charles.” He knows they would never allow him to
sacrifice himself, more so if they knew what that meant. “I guess we will find out soon then!”
Charles says to Andrew. “Yeah, I suppose so.” Andrew says slowly before turning and heading
off.
Andrew immediately heads to Dr Melblac’s office. He knocks lightly on the door,
waiting for permission to enter the room. The door opens and reveals Melblac busy at work.
“Doctor Reed.” Melblac greets. Andrew enters the room and closes the door behind him. He
makes his way to Melblac’s desk and stands next to her. “How did your hunt go?” Andrew asks
curiously. “I couldn’t find out much about the rituals to recall an exorcised demon back from hell,
but I did find out something interesting. Apparently the only other demon who can be called is
one which was once alive. We know that all demons are created as well, demons. The lore says
there are some which were born as something else, maybe human perhaps?” Andrew nods
understandingly. “There is one demon in particular. All the books say his title is the Devil, but he
goes by a number of names — Satan, Lucifer, Beliar, or Beelzebub. Sounds familiar?” Melblac
continues. “Go on.” says Andrew. “But I don’t believe it’s referring to thee devil, do you think?”

Andrew looks at her wanting her to come to her own conclusions. “Why do you believe it isn’t?”
He questions. “Within the Wicca tradition, Lucifer was a master of illusions and a master of
magic. It appears they are describing that same entity, a different being than the one we’ve all
been taught about from the Christian faith. You know, the angel who disobeyed God and was
cast out of Heaven.” Andrew nods, understanding. “Do you remember the story I told you of my
first brush with the occult, Avery?” He asks. Avery nods wondering where Dr Reed is going with
this. “The group I was investigating, they were a cult connected to the Devil’s den. They
followed some manifesto called The True Identity of Lucifer. More importantly, they believed
they could summon the devil. They almost succeeded when they killed me so it could take over
my body.” Andrew recalls. “I think we can safely assume that they are also involved with this
ritual. This means they have all of the information they need so they could do it again.” Andrew
concludes. “What exactly do you plan to do?” Avery asks. “As you have said earlier Dr Reed, we
have to stop the ritual.” She continues. “We are going to confront the leaders of the cult.”
Andrew adds. “That sounds good to me, but we will need a plan.” Avery agrees. They both leave
Defiance and go meet up with Atticus and Jonathan. They meet at Jonathan’s apartment.
Atticus is sitting on a couch at Jonathan’s reading the grimoire. He looks furious and
frustrated. “Hey guys, how’d it go?” Jonathan asks enthusiastically. Dr Reed was happy to see
his son again. He knew it could be the last time they were ever in the same room since he
planned on giving himself up to the Devil’s den. Andrew was prepared to die. “We found
somethings out, I’ll tell you all about it.” He announces, taking a seat next to Atticus. As Andrew
began to recount everything they discovered since the night before, Atticus became agitated. “Dr
Reed, your son is involved in some serious business.” He says. “Never mind that for now, we
need a plan to rescue the Deviants.” Andrew speaks with urgency in his tone. The room falls

silent as everyone waits for Avery to explain further. She takes a moment to collect her thoughts
before she begins talking. “Well Dr Reed and I discovered that the den plan to bring back Lucifer,
who apparently once possessed Dr Reed.” Jonathan looks at her in disbelief. “You have to be
kidding me.” He says. “The devil, really? First demons now this! My suspension of disbelief is
getting thinner.” He continues. Atticus chimes in. “The fun thing about the Devil is that every
culture and tradition has a different take on him. In some beliefs he Is the classic cartoony red-
skinned monster with horns and a pitchfork, in some he is a slobbering demon, or a talking
serpent and in others a beautiful angel damned to Hell! It’s all fair game. Over the centuries
we’ve been introduced to the charming Devils and grotesque Devils, sympathetic Devils, fiddle-
playing Devils, and many, many more.” Atticus went on to explain. He had well vested
knowledge all things supernatural. “So, which one is it?” Jonathan asks seeming confused. “It
doesn’t matter. The fact is, whenever people get involved with this, innocent people die. We
cannot let that happen.” Dr Andrew tells him. Jonathan becomes serious. “Ok, I reached out to
my contact, he gave me information about the people who own that building. It belongs to a shell
corporation which I traced and learned they own multiple properties across the state. One of
which is a manor at the outskirts of town. My best guess is if these people wanted to do
something shady, they would do it there, far from prying eyes.” He did not really believe in all of
this talk about the devil, but Jonathan was ready to face whatever was ahead. “What’s the
address?” Avery inquires. Jonathan pulls out his phone. He scrolls through a few pages before
finding the location of the manor. “That’s it. There’s no street name, but this is the right place. If
we move quickly, we might still find your people there.” Jonathan concludes. Everyone
exchanges nods of agreement. “Atticus, how’s the research coming?” Andrew asks worriedly.
“Almost done.” Atticus assures him. “Good… Now we just need to figure out how to actually do

it.” He says. “I have an idea, but it’s risky.” Says Atticus. “Let’s hear it.” Avery requests. “It is
called apotropaic magic. It is mainly used as an anti-magic magic spell. We could use it on Dr
Reed. That way, when they try, they won’t be able to complete the ritual. But there is a catch.
The Devil’s den won’t know that the ritual did not work only after they complete it.” Atticus
explains. “What does this mean?” Avery asks. Atticus pauses for a moment. “It means if they
perform the ritual, they will definitely fail but, Dr Reed will probably be killed. Which is
something I’m pretty sure we don’t want them to do.” He informs them, looking directly at
Andrew. “What are we going to do then?” Jonathan asks, worried for his father. Atticus’ gaze
shifts to the floor as he tries to think of an answer. “I don’t have any other ideas and to be frank,
the spell won’t last for long. Once it wears off, they can still do the ritual if they haven’t killed
him yet and they wouldn’t even realise the spell was there. We need a more permanent solution
to stop them from releasing Lucifer from hell.” He answers. “Well, that’s why we’re here. To try
and think of something.” Avery responds. “And we’ll keep searching until we do come up with
something.” Jonathan finishes. “Alright then. Let’s start with getting into the house.” Andrew
says. “How do we do that?” Jonathan asks curiously. Avery explains her plan. After hearing it,
they all agree that it could work and begin to depart to the manor.
Meanwhile back at the manor. Sarah, Miles, Steven and Amala are still recovering from
their encounter with the guard. Sarah recovers faster and quickly gets back up, helping Steven up
as well. “Thank you, Sarah. Thank you for saving me! I thought we were going to die.” Steven
exclaims. Sarah gives him a sad smile. “I’m glad you’re alright Steven.” As Sarah talks, she
starts feeling weaker, as if her energy had been drained. “How can you do that? I thought you
didn’t have any abilities.” Amala questions. “Yeah, you did it at the bus too.” Steven adds. Miles
senses that there is something she is hiding, but he trusts that she will tell them in her own time.

“Let’s move.” He says to others. At this point, Andrew and his team were just arriving at the
manor. The plan was, Andrew would go through the front door alone while the rest of the team
tries to sneak in, locate the Deviants and use them to attack the Devil’s den, freeing Andrew as
well. Their second objective was to capture the leader of he was present or any other high-
ranking member of the cult. “They’re here!” Sebastian states as he walks over to Alexandra.
“Who is here?” Alexandra asks concerned. “A couple of those defiance guys.” Sebastian replies.
“Go get the hostages and meet us in the study.” Alex instructs. Sebastian runs off to find Sarah
and her friends, leaving Alexandra alone to greet Dr Reed at the door. Dr Reed enters the manor
to find Alexandra, standing by the foyer, waiting. “Hello Dr Reed.” She greets him. “Alex.” He
responds. “You wanted me? Now you have me.” Alexandra smiles at him. “I’m afraid you have
miscalculated. I know that the rest of your friends are somewhere outside the building trying to
sneak in.” Alexandra admits. At that moment, Sebastian rushes in and informs her that Sarah,
Miles and the rest of them are gone. “No matter, we have him already.” Alexandra declares.
“Damien should be arriving shortly and then we will begin. Take him!” She instructs Sebastian.
Dr Reed turns and prepares to fight when suddenly, he feels a piercing pain as something wraps
around his neck. A voice whispers menacingly in his ear. “You aren’t going anywhere.” As Dr
Reed struggles against these invisible bonds, a sudden surge of power bursts throughout the
room making him lose consciousness. When Dr Reed opens his eyes again, he sees himself
standing in a room which looked almost exactly like the lab at Defiance. He hears a voice
coming from behind him. He spun around and saw Damien Videl standing a few feet away from
him, grinning maliciously. “I must admit that I am curious to know how you know so much
about us.” Damien comments. “I’ve heard of you Dr Reed, but it’s nice to finally meet you.” He
goes on, mocking Andrew. “Likewise.” Dr Reed replies. “You see, I’ve found that you, Dr Reed,

are rather clever. And you are quite resourceful, I need to know, how long have you known about
my little secret?” Damien asks. Dr Reed looks at him confused. “Secret? What secret?” Andrew
asks. Damien sighs, annoyed by Reed. “Do not play dumb with me Dr. Not now. You have been
taking my agents from the prisons, which I believed nobody knew about.” Damien tells Dr Reed.
“Oh you mean that secret? I am just that good.” Andrew responds, with a smug grin on his face.
Damien scowls, he was angry. “Don’t play coy with me Dr Reed, I have been aware of your little
assaults against us from the beginning. Ever since you began investigating me back in London all
those years ago. But when you start getting involved in matters this serious, I cannot not let that
slide.” Damien warns him. Dr Reed remains unfazed by Damien’s threat. Damien becomes
angrier, more so annoyed. He pulls in closer to Dr Reed, grabbing him by the neck and squeezing.
“You think this is a game!” He exclaims. Andrew struggles to catch his breath while attempting
to respond to Damian. “Let him go Damien!” Alexandra shouts. “We need him for the ritual
remember. He cannot die just yet.” She adds. Damien lets go of Dr Reed and stands back from
him, glaring daggers at him. “Very well.”
Outside, Jonathan, Avery and Atticus are attempting to sneak into the manor and locate
the Deviants. Unbeknownst to them, their presence has already been detected. They are walking
into a trap. They had planned to enter the manor from the forest, that way they would be hidden.
The Deviants team was also in that very forest, after having escaped from the manor through a
hidden pathway. The moment they stepped foot in the forest, Avery and her team realized that
the trail was already being blocked off by a large number of guards, each armed with a weapon.
The Deviants team were walking towards the guards from the manor and had not yet noticed the
trap ahead. The guards detect the intruders coming in from the back. “There they are!” One of
the guards yells out, pointing at Avery’s group and alerting the others. Suddenly, a gun went off,

hitting one of the guards in the chest. Another bullet lands near another guard, hitting him in the
leg before he could shoot back. That one guard falls forward onto the ground. He was dead. The
remaining guards immediately aim their weapons at the intruders. They are unable to fire off
their guns due to the bullets flying towards them. The other guards quickly run away to hide. The
Deviants team, Sarah, Miles, Steven and Amala hear all the commotion and immediately take
cover. Soon after, Avery and her team arrive near where Sarah and her group were hiding. They
do not notice one another at first. Jonathan takes command of the situation. “I spotted about ten
guards who were guarding this entrance.” He begins to say. “They will be back with more
reinforcements. We should keep moving forward” He continues. Atticus nods. “Right.” They
continue to the front. Atticus hears raffling coming from the bushes and signals everyone toward
it. They go over to investigate it. “Atticus?” Sarah shouts, happy to see him. “Sarah!?” Atticus
exclaims, surprised to see her as well. “What are you doing here?” Sarah asks. “We came here to
rescue you!” Atticus says. They hug one another, both relieved that they found each other again.
“This is our chance to escape.” Sarah says. “No, we can’t leave without Dr Reed, he gave
himself up for you and we cannot just leave him!” Avery tells her. “So what are you
suggesting?!” Sarah demands angrily. “Well…we’re not going to get very far all huddled
together like this.” Avery points out. “But if we split up…” Atticus interjects. “We won’t even
make it halfway if we do that, we must stick together.” He reminds Avery. “I guess we really
don’t have much choice then.” Amala agrees. “Come on.” Sarah orders the others. “We have to
hurry.” She adds. They start running towards the manor as the sounds of gunshots can be heard
coming from behind them. Miles leads the group forward towards the direction where the hidden
pathway was located and away from gunfire. They quickly make their way through the passage
and into the undercroft of the manor. But now they were trapped. They could not go back outside

and they could not move deep inside the manor since it was crawling with people, who would
find them immediately. “Where the hell are we?” Jonathan asks while looking around and
noticing the creepy aesthetic of the undercroft. Atticus is awed at the sight. He sees droves of
materials, all connected to demonology and all things occult. He really appreciated these kinds of
things. “Well, at least we’re hidden underground…” Avery says. Everyone turns their heads
towards the sound of voices coming down the hallway. “What is that?” Jonathan asks. “It’s the
guards!” Steven exclaims. They all duck low to avoid being seen. “They’re close! Get down!”
Miles says, as the guards approach the entrance to the undercroft. They peek through a small
opening in the wall to reveal Damien and Alexandra. They had with them a chalice which
Damien held between his hands as he raises it up threateningly. Atticus notices that it is an
essential part of the ritual the Devil’s to den wanted to perform on Dr Reed. “This is it, the ritual
has started!” He says calmly.

chapters.title: Atticus Finch
chapters.text:
Since the genesis of human civilisation, mankind has attempted to rationalise its primeval
fear of the unknown by creating order out of chaos by categorising its understanding of the
concept of evil. The idea of evil was progressively quantified and refined by assigning names to
negative characteristics such as sloth and greed, but also to ailments of the body and the mind
such as depression, blindness and headache. During the time of the ancient Greeks a word was
found to describe this entire class of characteristics, defined as demons. Supernatural beings with
a malevolent purpose, able to possess the living and interfere with their destinies. The existence
of demons remains an important concept in many modern cultures and beliefs. However, the
nature of demons themselves is not as well understood. They are often thought of as mere myths
or nightmares, but in fact, the most common forms of demonic existence are actually quite real.
Some demons are described to appear to humans as grotesque, monstrous creatures, with horns
and tails, claws and fangs. There have always been many legends of demons originating from
pagan cultures, including the story of Lucifer. In some cultures, demons are depicted as beautiful
and seductive figures, possessing powerful magics, possessing the power to control minds and
bodies. In ancient Rome, the term demon was widely used, but only in the context of mythology
and myth, with the meaning of demon being merely a figure of speech. Demons are responsible
for bringing harm to the world; for breaking the peace and law, for destruction of property and
lives. Throughout history, there have been many people who took advantage of the power which
demons possess. The estate which Atticus finch and his friends are currently trapped in is filled
with such people. It belongs to an organization aptly named the Devil’s den. This organization

has existed throughout history in various kingdoms, empires and nations throughout the world.
“This place is amazing, look at all the occult stuff they have in here.” Atticus says excitedly.
“Yeah. It really does seem to contain all types of magic and supernatural artefacts.” Sarah
remarks. “Let’s find Dr Reed, rescue him and get the hell out of here!” Amala announces. The
seven of them continue their journey further and finally make it to the end of the room. As they
exit the undercroft and start making their way towards a staircase leading downstairs to the
basement, they are met with another group of guards blocking their path. “How did they know
we would try and break in?” Atticus asks quietly. Everyone keeps quiet, waiting for the guards to
reveal themselves. The first one speaks up. “You’re going to pay dearly for trespassing into the
manor.” He states coldly, as he draws his weapon. Atticus then steps forward. “We aren’t here to
steal anything. Just give us our friend, please.” Atticus says calmly. A guard sneers at him “Why
should I listen to you? You’re clearly the ones trespassing!” The guard sneers at him when he
replies. “I can assure you that my companions and I have no intention to cause any trouble.”
Atticus insists. He glances at Sarah, who gives him a frown before speaking in a loud tone.
“What are you doing? You cannot reason with them!” She whispers to Atticus. “Keep going.”
Jonathan tells him. This was a great distraction, a way to keep the guards busy so Jonathan could
slip away unnoticed. Atticus nods his head. “No, but I’m willing to try and talk them into letting
us pass.” He explains. Sarah shakes her head again, “No Atticus, they will not.” She says to him.
At that moment, one of the guards approaches Atticus and his friends. The guard points his gun
at them. “Don’t you think your friends would want you to come quietly?” He says. Jonathan had
taken advantage of the interaction between Atticus and the guard, who was distracted. He was
able to slip away unseen. The guard looks around confused, searching for Jonathan, when
Jonathan makes his presence known by firing a few shots from his revolver. The guard drops to

the ground gravely injured but still alive. This alerts the whole manor to the position of the
intruders. As fast as possible Jonathan and Atticus run down the stairs, with their guns drawn and
ready for a fight. They hear screams of terror from the other side of the room. Sarah and the rest
of the group quickly join them. In front of them stands a group of men, armed and with their
rifles aimed directly at them. “Get on the floor and put your hands above your head!” One of
them yells. But Atticus is determined. He takes a few steps back until he is next to the wounded
guard. He lowers his gun and puts it at his head. Everyone stares nervously and apprehensively at
the group, and at the fallen guard. “Stand back!” Atticus orders the other guards, but none obey.
The guards start to raise their rifles once again to fire, when suddenly, a woman rushes forward
from the direction of the guards. She leaps in front of the them and raises her hand in the air,
commanding their attention to her. Atticus watches in awe as she proceeds. “Enough!” She says
with a commanding voice. It was Alexandra Turner who spoke. The guards instantly lower their
guns, but do not relax their grips on the weapons. The woman then addresses everyone. “The
situation may not be ideal, but we are here. Let us be civilized, give up now and we will allow
you to walk out through those doors.” She tells the group. Everyone starts to slowly relax as the
threat of the guards seems to ease. Alexandra glances at Atticus and motions her head towards
the entrance of the manor. Everyone else follows her gaze towards the door, which leads back
outside and out of the area of the manor occupied by guards. But then, Jonathan steps forward
and questions Alexandra. “What about my father!?” He yells angrily, trying desperately to keep
the emotions out of his voice. Atticus notices that Jonathan is visibly shaken. But there is nothing
he can do to help him. If he goes up against the guards, it will surely be a dangerous proposition.
Neither of them is aware that Alexandra Turner is a strong and formidable opponent. They were
in the Devil’s den now. If they want to survive, they need to smart about their next move, work

together and play along. “Come on Jonathan, let’s not be rash.” Atticus says, looking straight at
Jonathan. He does not take his eyes off of Jon. “I don’t want to leave without my father…”
Jonathan says. Atticus explains to Jonathan that he has a plan and that Dr Reed will be saved if
they to manage to escape at this moment. “Trust me.” He says to Jonathan, attempting to
convince him to leave the manor without his father. Jonathan reluctantly agrees fearing for the
safety of everyone else. “Alright, then let’s go!” Sarah says encouragingly. And so, they follow
Atticus out of the manor.
The Grimoires are handbooks of dark forces and magic that provide instructions for
rituals, the casting of spells and the evocation and control of demons. Grimoires give instructions
for rituals to conjure and control spirits and cosmic forces for protection, wealth, luck,
supernatural power, Curses on enemies, and so forth. While in the undercroft at the manor,
Atticus sneakily took a peek at one of the grimoires scattered within the walls of the undercroft.
Inside the grimoire, he noticed an essential element still missing for the ritual the Devil’s den
wanted to perform on Dr Reed. “You see guys, I was not completely certain but the ritual hasn’t
started. They need a full moon in order to perform it. There isn’t going to be one for the next four
days. We still have time to rescue Dr Reed.” He says to the others. After being let go back at the
Devil’s den, the team all rendezvoused at Jonathan’s apartment. As they sat feeling defeated,
Atticus explained to them what he discovered. “My plan is to fight fire with fire. We will use
Jonathan’s grimoire.” He goes on. Sarah looked puzzled by what Atticus said at first, but she
soon understood. “But…we shouldn’t.” She says quietly. Atticus just shrugs and continues
explaining his plan. “What do you plan on doing.” Amala questions. Using a grimoire is always
dangerous. There can be unforeseen consequences to tapping into such power. No one knew this
fact more than Sarah. But Atticus was very gifted in the occult. He had thought it through and

determined he could use the grimoire with no problems at all. “We will use the grimoire to
summon a demon.” Atticus replies confidently. “Summon a demon? Do you even know what
you are saying? How will we know that it won’t attack us the instant it sees us?” Amala asks.
She was worried. “We’ll use some form of a sigil to bind him. But it’s got to be on something
that attracts him. Like a candle or a dead animal. A candle and a dead animal can bind him. That
might work.” Atticus replies simply. “It sounds simple enough.” Says Avery. Atticus smirks
“Yes, maybe simple is not quite how I’d describe it. For starters, a binding sigil usually isn’t a
good choice for summoning demons. They tend to be territorial creatures. And I don’t remember
seeing a dead animal in Reed’s kitchen.” Everyone is on board with this plan except for Amala,
Steven and Miles. They have all had bad experiences with demons before and are rightfully
apprehensive about meeting another one. Sarah is the only one of them who is not afraid as she
has been contending with Naberius this whole time. Steven swallows his breath and he then turns
to Atticus, “So what happens after we summon a demon?” He asks. Atticus first explains that he
aims to summon a very specific demon, Astaroth -A very powerful demon of the first hierarchy
who among other things can answer every question formulated to him. He is said to give to
humans the power over serpents as well. “After we summon him, we ask him how to prevent the
resurrection of the demon Lucifer and save Dr Reed.” Sarah sighs softly as she listens to Atticus’
explanation. “Oh, God!” Miles exclaims, horrified at the prospect of encountering another
demon who has surely destroyed countless souls. Avery, however, remains calm and speaks to
the rest of them. “Team, I have encountered demons before. Atticus Remember when we faced
the wraith in the crypts beneath St Peter’s Cathedral? I managed to beat the beast back and free
both myself and stupid Martin.” Avery tries to reassure them, although she knows that it is not
really reassuring. “Well…” Miles begins hesitantly. “If anything, that means we must get used to

the idea of battling demons.” He says. “Yeah, I guess we’ll be seeing a lot of them now.” Amala
remarks with a smirk. Everyone laughs nervously and then return their focus back to Atticus.
“Alright, so now that we have decided on the best way to defeat the enemy, let us move forward.
We don’t have much time left, so let’s make the most of it.” Atticus suggests. “Alright.” Sarah
replies confidently.
Two hours later and Atticus has made all the necessary preparations and precautions
needed to summon Astaroth. Even acquiring a live chicken to slaughter for the summoning ritual.
“Remember guys, Once the demon appears, we should not stand too close, as Astaroth gives off
a deadly stench.” Everyone nods in understanding. Atticus continues. “He should willingly
answer all questions regarding the past, present and future and will give up easily any secrets that
he knows.” The team appears sceptical. “What if he doesn’t give us any information at all?” Sara
asks, sounding concerned. Atticus shrugs as he prepares himself for the ritual. “He must.” He
answers. “Then we wait.” Avery concludes calmly. “Okay then,” Atticus says, smiling. He opens
Jonathan’s grimoire and begins the ritual. The ritual itself requires quite common and easy-to-
find materials: angelica root and human blood, as well as fulgurite or common salt. The
ingredients must be mixed with the blood in a bowl which has been placed atop a special
summoning sigil. An invocation in Latin must then be recited: “Te nunc invoco mortem, te in
mea potestate defixi, nunc et in aeternum!” Once done, the demon will materialize and be bound
to the will of the summoner. “Ready everyone?” Atticus asks them. “As ready as we will ever
be.” They reply. “Alright, he’s coming.” Atticus says. The group gathers around the sigil. They
all look anxiously at each other waiting for whatever may come next. After a few moments,
Astaroth finally appears, appearing in front of Atticus. The demon smiles maliciously at Atticus.
“What is the purpose for summoning me?” The demon asks, eyeing the group. “We need your

help.” Atticus replies calmly. The demon narrows his eyes slightly. “How interesting. In what
way can I assist you?” The demon goes on to ask. “To prevent the death of Dr Reed.” Atticus
informs the demon. “And because we’re desperate for help.” He finishes. The demon looks at
Atticus for a moment. He then slowly moved closer to Atticus. “Your desperation is very
amusing. I would love to aid you. Tell me, do you have an object? Something with which you
wished to bind me?” The demon looks at Atticus and then at the others. “Yes, we do.” Atticus
says while showing the demon the binding candle. “Very well then, let us begin.” Astorath says.
The demon Astorath had given Atticus and the rest of the team a way to stop the Devil’s
den from summoning Lucifer back into Dr Reed’s body. But he also warned them that there was
nothing they could do to save Dr Reed’s life. He was going to die. “Why is that?” Avery asked,
hoping against hope that it was not true. “His soul is already lost.” The demon had replied when
questioned. It has been hours since Astorath disappeared in a ball of smoke, presumably back to
the depths of hell. The team was still reeling from what he told them about Dr Reeds fate. “He is
wrong.” Jonathan protests. “How can he be wrong? Astorath can see into the future that is like
his whole thing.” Steven replies. “And he can see our fates.” Miles comments solemnly, his eyes
looking downcast. “And he can predict the deaths of those we love.” Sarah says quietly. This
upset everyone. They were all silently mourning the loss of the doctor even before it happened.
As they were standing near the summoning sigil, a cold draft suddenly swept across them
making them shudder as they felt it hit their faces. Everyone looked up in unison and saw a black
cloud above them, circling lazily. It was almost invisible as it hung overhead, but when the
breeze blew strongly in their direction, they could clearly feel the presence of a demon. “What is
that?” They ask. Atticus is unfazed by this sight and promptly states, “No need to worry about
that, it is just the remnants of Astorath. He was a powerful demon after all.” Everyone let’s go of

their worries. They all trust Atticus’ judgement when it came to anything supernatural. He
continues. “We need to do what Astorath told us. We cannot let the Devil’s den bring back
Lucifer.” With that said, he places the bloodied candle in the middle of the summoning sigil and
he just stares at it. “He said we need to do an Enn? What is that?” Amala asked, confused by
Astaroth’s instructions. Sarah grabs Jonathan’s grimoire and flips through the pages to find out.
“Enns are sentences of invitation spoken in demonic language.” Sarah reads out loud from the
grimoire. “Oh, so like an invocation?” She adds. Atticus pulls his focus off the sigil candle and
begins to answer her, giving them all everything he knows about Enns. “Unlike invocations,
Enns need to be recited exclusively in a demonic language. They do the same thing as
invocations. The difference is, they summon a demon only to then trap it inside of a charmed
object.” Everyone listens to Atticus as he explains. “Charmed objects?” Sarah repeats with a
frown. She thinks for a moment before she asks, “You mean like a curse?” Aside from possibly
Avery Melblac, Sarah was the only other person in the group as knowledgeable in the occult as
Atticus. “Exactly!” Atticus confirms. “But why did Astorath tell us about it? Is he actually trying
to prevent the resurrection of Lucifer?” Sarah asks puzzled as she closes the grimoire. “No, not
exactly. When I summoned Astorath, I used a binding sigil, that means the demon must obey me
without resistance. He had no choice but to tell us.” Atticus explains confidently. Sarah raises her
brows in surprise at what she heard. “Do you think we’re strong enough to curse Lucifer?” She
then asks, looking hopefully towards Atticus and her friends. “I do believe so. You and I together
we can do it.” Atticus assures her. As this is happening, Jonathan is in a corner alone wondering
what is going to happen to his father. Avery walks up to him to try and comfort him. “Don’t
worry Jonathan. Everything is going to turn out alright.” She assures him, holding him tightly by
the shoulder. Jonathan tries to smile at her reassuring words. “You really believe that?” He asks

her. Avery nods eagerly. “Of course. Everything will be fine, I promise.” She reassures Jonathan
