once again. Jonathan seems to calm down slightly hearing this. “Thanks.” He mumbles. Just then,
Sarah comes over to the two of them, having finished talking to Atticus. “Hey Jonathan, I just
want to say that I’m sure your dad makes it out of this alive. Amala is working on something to
return us to the manor and Atticus knows what he is doing. We will stop them from going
through with the ritual and get your father back.” Hearing this, Jonathan rushes to Amala and
inquires about her plan to return to the manor. He finds her on his computer with open tabs
showing the blueprints of the manor along with what appears to Jonathan as illegal access to
security footage from their cameras. “How did you get these?” He asks. “I am very good with
computers.” Amala answers.

chapters.title: Jonathan Reed
chapters.text:
Losing a parent is among the most emotionally difficult and universal of human
experiences. And although he understood that the loss of a parent is inevitable in the abstract
sense, that knowledge did not lessen Jonathan’s grief when he found out from Astorath that he’s
father was destined to die. Jonathan has already lost his mother. That loss was grief-filled and
traumatic, and it permanently altered him. Nothing was ever the same again; the loss of one
parent was a wholly transformative event for Jonathan. He was not ready to go through that
again. His mother’s death was tragic and devastating to him. Even now Jonathan remembers how
it felt when he discovered that she had died. He could not sleep for days afterward. The
nightmares that plagued him at night were terrifying. Amala sat in Jonathan’s living room
studying the layout of the manor where the Devil’s den was holding Dr Andrew Reed. She
looked at Jonathan as he sat in a chair beside her. He has been sitting here quietly for about
fifteen minutes, staring blankly ahead. “Jonathan. Do you think they will be able to pull this
off?” She asks softly. Jonathan does not respond immediately, instead thinking back to all the
things that happened during the last few days. Of course, he’d never forget any of it. “It won’t be
easy but if anyone can pull this off, I have faith it’s them.” He tells her truthfully. “I know they’ll
succeed Jonathan. You and I both know it.” She replies, smiling warmly at him as she puts a
comforting hand on his arm. Jonathan offers a small smile in response and the two continue to sit
there in silence.

Atticus, Sarah, Miles and Steven had gone off on a dire mission to obtain an object
powerful enough to contain the demon Lucifer once they used the Enn to summon and then bind
him. They had only two days until the next full moon and Jonathan was already getting anxious
to storm the manor and rescue his father from being sacrificed in a ritual. “So how do we do this
Enn?” Jonathan asks Amala. The look in Amala’s eyes tells him that she is not satisfied with the
information that she has so far. She opens her mouth to speak, before she does, she notices
unusual movements on the monitor. Amala was monitoring security footage from the gates of the
manor. “Look, it seems a lot of guests are arriving.” She says to Jonathan with a worried look on
her face. Accolades of the Devil’s den were arriving in droves to witness the ritual. Jonathan saw
this as an opportunity to fulfil his father’s ambitions and take down the whole cult while they
were all in one place. But he was also afraid of what this new development meant. All these
important guests arriving at the manor meant that they were closer to going through with the
ritual. His father did not have much time left and Jonathan knew it. He looks at Amala worriedly.
“Are we ready?” He asks, concerned. “I would rather not think about it.” Amala replies.
Jonathan sighs deeply. “Alright let’s go, I’m not going to sit around and wait for them to
sacrifice my father.” He says, standing up quickly. Amala grabs his hand stopping him. “The last
time we couldn’t do anything to them and there were 7 of us, what do you think you’re going to
do?” She asks, knowing that Jonathan cannot restrain himself. He wanted to kill them all.
“You’re right I guess…” Jonathan tells her reluctantly. Amala smiles at him encouragingly as
she holds his hand tight. “There’s no reason for us to fear, we have the best people on our side.
Besides, we’ve got you to help us, right?” She gives him a sweet smile. Jonathan returns the
smile weakly and squeezes her hand before letting it drop. He realises that he is still a police
officer with a lot of resources he could use. The Devil’s den were still criminals albeit unusual

ones, but criminals none the less. He figured he could enlist other cops to raid the manor. If he
just showed them that his father was held there against his will, the police would have to get
involved. Jonathan knew he had to be careful how he presented this case to his superiors and co-
workers if they were to take this case. There has always been a difficulty police officers have in
ascertaining possible criminal aspects of cult activities and the police departments are
inexperienced in dealing with cult cases such as this. Jonathan needed someone who was familiar
with such issues. Someone he trusts. Someone that has worked in the field before. He did not
know this yet but the satanic panic in the recent years gave birth to a police squad dedicated to
investigating satanic crimes. To this day, the status of the unit remains shrouded in mystery.
Jonathan believed that he might have been able to find some guidance in someone with
that kind of experience. He turns to Amala. “I have an idea; I cannot tell you what it is yet but
just trust me ok!” He tells her. She nods in agreement and follows Jonathan as he heads outside
towards the car. Once they reach the car, Jonathan opens the door for her and she gets into the
passenger seat as Jonathan slides onto the driver’s seat. He starts the engine and they head
towards the police precinct which is located on a hilltop overlooking the city. A couple of
minutes pass as Jonathan drives in silence, deep in thought about his current predicament. Amala
leans forward in her seat and places her hand gently on top of Jonathan’s. He instantly looks over
and smiles at her gratefully. “It’s going to work out alright, I promise.” She says to him
reassuringly. He gives a faint nod as he reaches across the gearshift and takes her hand in his,
giving it a gentle squeeze. Amala feels happy that she’s been allowed to help Jonathan in this
crisis and that he’s taken the initiative in reaching out to her. Her thoughts begin to drift and as
Jonathan pulls away from her hand he looks over at her quizzically. “What are you thinking?”
He asks her. “It’s just, your dad he. He saved my life and now I’m working with his son, a cop,

trying to save his life. I just never imagined my life would turn out like this.” She confesses.
“Yeah…” Jonathan murmurs sadly, turning his attention back on the road before looking down
to the steering wheel for a second. Jonathan looks back up again after a few moments and looks
over at her. “…Amala….you said my father saved you right?” He asks her cautiously. Amala
frowns curiously. “Yes?” She asks him. Jonathan does not reply, instead choosing to just keep
his gaze trained on the road before turning his attention back to Amala. “How exactly did he do
that?” Jonathan asks her with a curious expression on his face. “He, uh…well.” Is all that she
manages to get out, unsure of how to answer Jonathan’s question. What could she possibly say?
Should she even mention the fact that he broke her out of prison or should she just play dumb?
Jonathan stares at her waiting for her to say something. Amala clears her throat nervously and
looks down at her lap. She decides to go with her gut and play dumb. “Well, he,
um…well…ummm.” She stammers out. Jonathan watches her nervously and begins to fidget
uncomfortably in his seat. It was not like her to be this quiet and shy and he knows he needs to
make her talk. “Tell me! Just tell me!!” Jonathan insists. “Ok fine.” She goes on. “I am, well I
was in a bad place the last few years. I did so many horrible things. Your father he, he recognised
that these things weren’t my fault and he helped me get past it.” She explains. Jonathan listens
intently as she speaks, watching carefully to see if she’s lying. He tries to figure out if she is.
After a minute or two, she stops talking and glances over at Jonathan who is completely silent.
She looks at him suspiciously and realizes that he is not sure whether he believes her. “Did he
help you get out of prison?” He finally asks. Amala shakes her head slightly, her eyebrows
furrowed together as she searches her own heart for the answer. “No,” She replies. Jonathan
continues to stare intensely at her. She chose to withhold the whole truth from him because she

knew he would not understand. If he ever did figure it out, she felt it would cause more harm
than good. Not just for her sake, but for everyone else.
Shortly after, Jonathan and Amala arrive at the precinct. As he drives up to the parking
lot, he sees a fellow officer waving at him enthusiastically. He parks the car and jumps out
immediately heading over to his co-worker and leaving Amala in the car. “Hey! How’ve you
been doing buddy?” The officer greets him happily, pulling him into a bear hug. Jonathan laughs
awkwardly, returning the gesture. “I haven’t been great.” Jonathan replies. “Oh yeah? And why
would that be?” The officer asks curiously, releasing him from his embrace. Jonathan chuckles
softly. “It is my father. Come inside! I will tell you all about it.” They both enter the precinct and
Jonathan leads his co-worker over to the main desk. Jonathan puts on his best serious face as
they approach the desk. The officer at the desk looks at Jonathan in shock. “Officer Reed? Isn’t
this a bit too early for you to be coming here?” The woman asked, looking at him suspiciously.
“I have been investigating a case ever since the accident that killed my partner and I have found
out some things.” Jonathan answers. The woman looks at him suspiciously again before turning
to her computer and clicking on the mouse. After a few seconds she turns to Jonathan. “We’ve
already cleared that case.” She says to him. Jonathan sighs dejectedly. “But that’s not true
anymore.” He says. “Look.” He goes on, holding up a photo and showing the officer and then
showing it to the woman as well. “This is my father. He was taken by this group, some sort of
cult. My investigation leads me to believe they were also responsible for the incident on the
highway that day.” The woman stares at the photo in silence for a moment as a look of
realization dawns upon her face “You’re right it must be related. I’ll see what I can do.” She says,
smiling at Jonathan. “Don’t worry officer, everything will sort itself out.” She adds with a
reassuring grin. What do you mean by that?” He asks. Jonathan can tell that she knows more

about this than she’s letting on. “What are you holding back?” He asks the officer. “We did not
know what was happening until you brought it to me just now. Follow me.” She leads him to a
back office that has always seemed to Jonathan to be empty all the time. Once there she closes
the door behind them and begins telling him a long-kept secret within the precinct. “About five
or six years ago, a member of our department came under suspicion for murder.” She reveals.
Jonathan’s eyes widen in shock. “Murder?” He asks. The officer nods, taking off her glasses to
clean them with a tissue. “We were informed a month ago that our chief wanted another
detective to investigate the case further but when he couldn’t bring himself to accept it, he
decided to ask your father, Dr Reed to step into it. The reason he requested him in the first place
is because he thought it would be beneficial for the case. Your father is very influential when it
comes to weird cases. It turns out the case involved ritualistic murders. We didn’t know how to
handle it. Ever since then your father acts as liaison with a select few within the police
department to tackle occult cases. Your father’s organisation it is called…” Jonathan interrupts
the officer before she could finish “Defiance...” He says flatly. “Yes…defiance.” She confirms.
“The way he describes that group sounds like something out of mythology. But I did not know
you knew.” She adds. “Give me an hour, I’ll talk to my chief.” She says as she stands up to
leave. “Thank you, Officer.” Jonathan says. As she walks away, he sits down at the desk and
wonders about Amala and the rest of her team. “Why have they not contacted Defiance to assist
in rescuing my father.” He thinks to himself. The truth is Dr Avery Melblac was apprehensive
about informing the other members of Defiance about the current situation for fear that they
would not lend a hand. Dr Reed had not gotten approval to go into the manor and she knew they
were on their own. Jonathan returned to the car to wait with Amala until the officer came back

with a way forward. He had no clue how Defiance worked. He decides it is better to not depend
on them for now.
Atticus, Avery, Sarah, Miles and Steven find themselves inside an occult museum
searching for an ushabti. The only artefact powerful enough to contain Lucifer. “Are you certain
we are looking at the correct artefact?” Steven inquires. Atticus shrugs helplessly. “According to
the records, we are. There are a few in different locations around the city. This is the closest
one.” He admits. It was very important that they obtain this artefact. Even though it was
dangerous as hell and extremely volatile, it was the key to keeping Lucifer out of the hands of
the Devil’s den once and for all. “We should try and get close to it. Steven can use his simple
trick to unlock its case. We’ll just lift it and get out of here.” Miles suggests. Sarah frowns at
him. “You’d have to be pretty stupid to think that will work Miles!” She exclaims. He ignores
her comment and smiles mischievously. “If I remember correctly, he used his powers to break
into the cells before. I don’t doubt he’s capable of breaking into anything.” Miles replies
confidently. “He might be able to, but what about the rest of us? Even if he managed to unlock
the case, how are we supposed to just walk out with it.” Sarah interjects. “Maybe we need a
distraction. Someone could distract them whilst I take care of the case, right?” Steven suggested.
Everyone seemed to agree with Steven. Sarah sighed. “Alright, I guess we do.” Atticus agrees.
The gang decides to split up, with Sarah and Miles distracting the only guard who was on duty
inside the little museum and Steven and Atticus go towards the case. As the team is enacting this
plan, at the same time the officer was heading towards Jonathan to inform him what her chief
decided in regards to Dr Reed. As she approaches the car, Jonathan gets out of the front sit eager
to learn what the plan was. He was expecting the worst. “The chief and I discussed your father’s
situation. Dr Reed he, he has helped this city and the department too many times to count and

now he is the one who needs our help so we will help.” She tells Jonathan, giving him a gentle
smile. “Are we going to raid the manor where he is being held?” He asks eagerly. “Yes, as we
speak the chief is coordinating every cop he trusts with this to prepare.” She replies. Jonathan
gestures at Amala who is watching the two intently from the car. “Hey come here.” He says to
her. “My friend has found a way to get eyes inside the manor, we can see their every move. I’m
sure that could help.” Jonathan explains as he opens the passenger side door. Amala gets out of
the vehicle and greets the officer, afraid that she might recognise her as a fugitive. “Amala? Are
you alright? You look pale.” Jonathan asks. “I’m fine…it’s just… never mind that what do you
need?” Amala says, turning her head and smiling at both officers as best she could. Her hands
remain tightly clenched at her sides. “Reed tells me you have access to the cameras where his
father is being held. We’re hoping that maybe we could use your talents.” The female officer
says carefully. Amala gives her a small nod, not trusting herself not to start babbling nonsense. A
second passes. Then another. Amala is nervous. She does not want to disappoint Jonathan but
she’s scared that the officer is going to send her back to prison. To her surprise, the officer does
not even realise who she is. She finally relaxes enough to speak. “I will be glad to help.” She
says to the officer. “Great! We’ll take you inside and we can begin. I am Naomi and I look
forward to working with you.” The officer replies, leading her inside the precinct. Amala follows
the officer through several corridors to a room which resembles an interrogation cell with a table
and chairs. “Please take a seat. If you’d like some water or some refreshments, I’ll be outside the
door.” Naomi informs Amala, as she heads out the room. Once again, Amala is left alone with
her thoughts. She looks down at the floor as she tries to avoid looking directly at anything. After
a couple minutes, a figure enters through the doorway behind Amala, startling her. She turns
around to see Jonathan. Amala’s heart quickens its pace. “Why am I in here?” She asks

worriedly. “Do not worry yourself. We put you in here so the other cops do not notice you. This
mission isn’t exactly sanctioned.” Jonathan assures her, sitting down across from her. “Ok.” She
says softly as she sits facing him. They sit silently for several minutes, neither wanting to break
the silence. Jonathan breaks the ice by asking Amala, “Why are you afraid to be in here? Is there
something you are hiding?” Amala does not fall for his baiting and remains silent. Soon after
officer Naomi arrives with her chief in tow. “Good afternoon officer Reed.” The chief greets him
politely, before gesturing at Amala. “Young lady I am chief Singh. Jonathan, let us begin.” He
says. Chief Singh is a heavy-set middle-aged man who was often quite friendly but stern with his
employees. He worked with Dr Andrew Reed to establish a secret department within the police
that handled cases which were supernatural in nature.

chapters.title: Miles Kelly
chapters.text:
Aeshma is a demon of wrath, rage, and fury. A fierce class of demons responsible for all
acts of aggression and malice. Unchecked, the powers of these demons can lead to the
destruction of humanity. Miles Kelly was once under the control of this demon. Dr Avery
Melblac cleansed him of it. But that rage was still bubbling underneath inside of Miles. The
demon was a part of him and always would be. His memories, desires and emotions were all tied
together with this beast within. Miles had been trying to push his demonic urges down, trying to
maintain his human side, and ever since he got abducted by Defiance, he has been able to keep it
under control. He, Atticus and Sarah were at the museum with Avery and Steven to steal an
ushabti, a small ancient statue that they could use to trap the demon Lucifer inside. They headed
straight for one of the display cases where the artefacts were kept. Miles took a look at the
displays and noticed something peculiar. “Atticus. What is this?” He asked pointing to the first
exhibit on the display. “What is it, Miles?” Atticus replied. “That’s the ushabti that we need to
open right? Well, it seems theirs is a secondary security measure.” Miles answered. Atticus
looked at the display and immediately understood Miles’ statement. “Oh damn.” He says quietly.
Miles then turned towards Steven. “Can you handle it? Your thing is opening locks right this is
basically a lock.” Miles asks him. “I don’t know. I’ve never done it before. But if we need to
hurry, I suppose it doesn’t hurt to try.” Steven replies nervously. As he finishes talking the two
women make their way back towards the centre of the display where the artefact is located. “We
can do this.” Sarah tells Steven encouragingly. Steven nods. “Okay. On three.” He warns his
partners. They all nod, ready to follow instructions. Three seconds later, Steven reaches out and

touches the surface of the case. A loud clanging noise emits from the case as an electric current
run through the metal and the entire case begins to shake violently. “Shit! That didn’t work.
Damn!” Miles curses as he grabs hold of Steven, pulling him away from the glass containing the
ushabti, which continues to shake as it starts to break apart. Suddenly, there is a loud cracking
sound which makes everyone freeze. The entire case breaks into pieces, falling to the ground,
scattering shards and dust everywhere. Everyone stares at the shattered glass in awe, wondering
how something made of security glass could shatter so easily. About six guards come out
running toward were Miles and his partners were. “There they are! Arrest them!” Miles is pushed
to the ground as more guards swarm around him. One pulls him to his feet and shoves handcuffs
onto his wrists “Don’t touch me.” Miles screams, kicking and squirming against the men holding
him. Miles finds himself on his knees on the cold, hard floor. With a hand shoved onto his mouth
preventing him from screaming. The officer takes a step forward menacingly. “This will only
hurt slightly.” He assures him. He presses down on the top button of his trousers and presses his
gun against Miles’s temple. Miles struggles against the men holding him, as he sees one of the
guards lift a large metal stick. He feels himself getting lightheaded, as he fights desperately to
stay awake. He knows that if he lets himself pass out, he will surely be arrested again. With no
choice but to trust in his abilities, he closes his eyes tightly, bracing himself for the wrath.
Suddenly, he hears someone yell for help. It comes from behind him, causing Miles to look over
his shoulder. It is Sarah. She looks frantic as she shouts at the guards to let Miles go, begging
them to stop. Miles watches as Sarah is dragged away from him. All the while his captor holds a
pistol against his temple. He feels sick, dizzy, and disorientated. Everything starts fading in and
out of focus until he is finally unaware of everything around him. He can feel a warm liquid
dripping down the side of his face. He tries to turn his head to see his reflection and sees blood

flowing down from his forehead and landing in the dust beneath him. His vision becomes blurry
once more. He can sense the museum security surrounding him and teammates. He can hear
people shouting. There is ringing in his ears. And finally, Miles unleashes his rage. “Let her go.”
He yells. Everything stops. Miles can no longer hear the yelling, the ringing or the footsteps
around him. He feels numb yet terrified that something bad may happen to his beloved Sarah and
the rest of them. His next actions and intentions may be the only thing to make a difference.
Miles wants to cry. He wishes he could go back in time when everything seemed normal. When
he was happy. He thinks about his old life and his past relationship with his mother. They spent
almost every day at his home together doing absolutely nothing. Nothing but playing games and
laughing until they passed out. That is the best part of Miles’ past. Ever since he first got
possessed by a demon, it seems like he has lost everything, his world is crumbling all around him.
All he can feel is pure despair and emptiness. Sarah has been the only light. Dr Avery too, for
helping him rid himself of the demon inside of him. His vision goes black as darkness consumes
him completely. He loses all control over his body as it begins convulsing uncontrollably.
“Miles.” Someone calls his name. It sounds like it’s coming from far away as Miles blacks out.
He was conscious but unaware of his actions. Sarah and Avery are worried that his demon might
be resurfacing. Miles has not gotten used to using his abilities just yet. When he called them
forth at this moment, his mind could not handle the raw energy. The pure rage he exuded filled
his soul. Miles felt a sudden burst of strength course through him. He grew larger. He opens his
eyes and looks down to see himself fully transformed. He’s now tall, well over 8 foot with broad
shoulders and strong muscular arms and legs. His hair falls down past his shoulders. Now he has
a face with golden eyes, piercing yellow ones, which remind Sarah of a cat. He looked demonic.

Miles walks up to a guard and pushes him aside effortlessly as he approaches Sarah.
“Please stop what you’re doing. You’re hurting them.” Sarah pleads as she backs away slowly.
Her hands start to shake uncontrollably as she sees him approach. Miles reaches out and grabs
hold of the other guards surrounding them, flinging them across the museum floor. “Stop!!”
Sarah begs him, shaking as she stares straight into his glowing yellow eyes. Slowly, he lowers
his head until his lips are mere inches from hers, causing both of them to tense up. The remaining
guards turn to flee but Miles catches one of them by the arm and throws him to the ground. He
raises his head and snarls at them. “Steven, quick grab the statue.” Atticus says to Steven as he
rushes over to calm Miles down. “It’s okay. It’s me Miles.” He said as he reached for Miles
hands. “Hey we’re safe now.” Atticus assured him. Avery stands at the other end of the room
watching the scene unfold. Her heart beating rapidly as she watches. Her breath hitches in her
throat as Miles begins to transform back. “Hurry guys. We have to get him out of here.” Atticus
urged. Avery snaps out of her daze and moves as fast as she can to the group. “Miles! Buddy,
buddy please listen to me. Can you hear me?” She asked as she kneeled in front of Miles. “Miles,
I am going to take your hands. Please relax ok. Just let me help you. Breathe with me.” She
whispered as she started taking deep breaths. After some minutes, Miles stopped fighting, and
his breathing calmed. “Atticus, move fast. We have to get him back to the apartment.” Sarah
instructed. Atticus quickly grabs hold of Miles and helps him limp towards the exit, while Sara
and Steven follow behind. Steven had the ushabti. “Let me go!” Miles tried to struggle but he
could not because of the weakening transformation. Soon they reach their vehicle and Miles
struggles harder but he cannot break free from Avery and Atticus holding him. He was about to
speak up to tell them to stop when suddenly he feels a sharp pain shoot through him. He grunts
loudly and lets his head fall onto Avery’s shoulder. She was still struggling to get him in the

vehicle. The pain was excruciating. Miles could not fight it anymore. He allowed his muscles to
give up.
Miles is lying in bed in Jonathan’s apartment. His injuries had been treated and his
episode subsided. After being rushed out of the museum, they had taken him to the apartment
where he had collapsed. They tried to keep him conscious, but he had lost consciousness after
only five minutes, making everyone worry. Sarah was sitting beside the bed with him while
Atticus and Avery tried to figure out their plan with the ushabti statue. As Sarah sat watching
Miles sleep, she could not help but think back to when she first met him. He was so kind to her
in the cells where they were first kept. In fact, he always had a gentle smile on his face whenever
she saw him, despite the situation they were in. She remembers how he protected her even
though he was captured and held prisoner as well. “Sarah? How are you feeling?” A soft female
voice asks. She turned to her side to find Avery leaning against the doorframe smiling lightly at
her. “How is he?” Avery asked quietly. Sarah smiled softly and shook her head. “I’m afraid we
don’t know yet. What he did back there I don’t know if he will forgive himself. He’ll never
forget it “Sarah informed her sadly. “So what he’ll remember? if he does, it will change him for
the better.” Avery says confidently. “He needs to get used to his abilities and learn to control
them; he has no choice.” She adds. Avery gives Sarah an approving nod before slipping out of
the doorway. After a few moments, Sarah looked over toward Miles and felt tears forming in her
eyes. “What are you crying for?” Atticus asked as he entered the room carrying two glasses of
water. “For him. For the man I know he is.” She replied sadly as tears dripped down her cheeks.
“He told me how he was before the demon. He was a good person. He was a fighter, someone to
watch your back. To love. He’s got to believe that.” Sarah worried that Miles might not like who
he became when he let the wrath take over. He did not like who he was while the demon used

him to commit countless murders. He was finally at a place where he could be with himself again.
This latest incident would reverse all of that. “Maybe someday…things will be better.” Atticus
offered. Sarah wiped away another tear as she nodded. “Hopefully.” She agreed. Sarah had been
surprised by the transformation she witnessed. It scared her a little knowing he was capable of
such strength and violence. She sat beside Miles for hours as she watched him sleep peacefully.
Sarah did not bother waking him up as she knew he needed the rest. Instead, she had stayed
silent throughout the night while also trying to comfort him by stroking his hair and giving him
hugs. At least she did not have to see his demonic state. She was thankful that Atticus had
managed to convince Miles not to hurt anymore people. Although she doubted he could. She
looked over at her sleeping partner once more as he slept peacefully. His black tresses spilled
over the pillow as he shifted slightly. She smiled at him. “Good night, Miles…” She says softly
before laying her head against her folded arms.
The next morning, Sarah was awoken by someone shaking her awake gently. She opened
her eyes and instantly noticed Miles sitting on the edge of the bed and gazing down at her with
tired grey eyes. She smiles weakly as he leans forward to kiss her forehead. “Good morning,
Miles.” She murmured as she wraps her arms around his neck and pulls him closer to her. Miles
chuckles before kissing her deeply. Once they broke apart, he rests his head against hers. “How
do you feel now?” She asks with a concerned expression on her face. Miles sighs as she looks
into his eyes. “Much better. Thank you.” He answered gratefully as Sarah caresses his cheek
lovingly. Miles kisses her cheek before getting off the bed. He stretches his sore body and then
gets dressed while Sarah waits patiently for him. “Are you ready to do this?” He asks her,
looking at her with warm brown eyes. She nods with a look of determination in her eye. Miles
and Sarah head to the living room to find Atticus and the rest of the Deviants, including Jonathan

standing over the ushabti statue. Sarah and Miles walk over to join them. Atticus turns and
notices them coming. “You should probably stay close by. I don’t want any problems arising
from that creature’s presence.” He explained as he gestured towards the statue. “What do we do
with it after? We have to keep it safe.” Avery spoke up. “Plus, I believe bringing the creature up
will have an effect on the others.” She goes on. Atticus and Avery believed that bringing Lucifer
to the surface might have a negative effect on the Deviants, who each had portions of demon still
inside them. “We have no other choice but to bring him up.” Atticus replies. “Are we doing this
or not.” Jonathan asks impatiently. Atticus sighs before turning to the statue. “If the demon’s
energy isn’t absorbed by the statue, then it may react negatively on the d6eviants. It might cause
harm to them and us.” He informs. “That’s why we’re keeping them away from it...” Avery
explains. Atticus nods his head in agreement. “Alright. All right, you four need to leave.” He
announces as he points to Sarah, Miles, Steven and Amala. “What? No, no way!” Amala states.
“No, you are going too, Amala.” Atticus tells her sternly as he glares at her. Amala crosses her
arms stubbornly before looking over at Miles who was glaring at Amala in warning, and then at
Atticus. “Fine. But we’ll come back once you’re done.” She warned as she and the rest of the
Deviants start to walk out of the apartment. As soon as the doors shut behind them. Avery and
Atticus turn their attention back to the statue. Minutes go by as the Deviants stood outside,
waiting for Atticus and Avery to finish the Enn. “I don’t get it, how is this supposed to work? If
the Devil’s den wanted to summon their god, why do they need Dr Reed why can’t they just use
the same ritual Atticus is doing right now?” Steven asks the question that has been on everyone’s
mind. Sarah thought for a moment before replying. “It’s possible that they don’t need a human
sacrifice but a piece of a soul. So maybe they aren’t summoning Lucifer, but another being. An
avatar.” She replied. “An avatar, you say?” Steven repeats her words. “Yeah, and Atticus told

me that the Enn that Astorath gave us was unique, only known to Astorath himself. So the
Devil’s den simply does not have any other means to summon Lucifer than to sacrifice Dr
Reed.” Sarah finished her explanation. She was uncertain about her assumptions, simply going
off on the little she has learned from Atticus. Miles was staring intently from the balcony of the
apartment building down to the city landscape. “This won’t work.” He whispered. “What?”
Sarah asks, confused by his statement. “I have realised something, none of these people know
what they are doing. Just messing around with forces they have to business in. Look at what
happened to all of us, controlled by demons and now…now we are fighting a war just because
they tell us. We are not free.” Miles explains bitterly. Sarah frowned as she listened closely to his
words. He was right. They were not fighting for themselves, or anyone else, and neither was their
leader. They were fighting because they wanted to live free and in peace. They were doing all
this willingly because they believed that it is the only way to save their families and friends, they
believed that was how their fight would end. But Miles was losing hope. Sarah can see that he is.
Her gaze falls on the young man in front of her and sees the pain etched across his handsome
features. He was broken beyond repair; she could see that clearly. And the more she watches him,
the more it pained her to see him this way. She could not bear seeing her brave friend like this.
“He’s right you know.” Amala speaks up. “Lately I have felt the same way I used to feel when I
was possessed. I don’t know, somehow I think that demon is still inside of me just itching to
make me do those awful things again.” She admits quietly. Sarah looks over at Amala in surprise.
This was not something she expected her to admit, and definitely not today. Sarah turns to look
at Miles who had stopped moving altogether, frozen in despair. “You’ve never mentioned that to
us, why didn’t you say anything sooner?” Sarah asks softly. Amala shrugs. “I figured you would
not care.” She answers. “But we do care about you, you know that.” Sarah responds quickly. She

reaches out her hand to hold Amala’s. “Yeah, we all should care about each other, we’re going to
need to if this is our life now.” Steven remarks. He was right, even Miles could not deny that.
“Miles,” Sarah begins quietly as he continues to stare into space without responding. “You need
to stop torturing yourself.” She says to him. “It’s just so hard.” He replies, with tears forming in
his eyes. “There is nothing wrong with it being hard, you just need to keep moving forward.”
Sarah tries to reassure him. Miles laughs humourlessly as he wipes away some stray tears.
“Don’t pretend like you’re okay too.” He adds. Sarah considers telling them about Naberius, but
she holds her tongue. There was enough stress in their life already. She did not want to add to it.
Miles knows that Sarah does not want to talk about it yet so he decides to change the subject.
“Amala tell us about your little side mission with Jonathan.” He asks her brightly as he smiles
encouragingly at her. Amala rolls her eyes affectionately before explaining everything that had
gone down during their trip. When she finishes, both Miles and Sarah nod approvingly. “Well
done, Amala.” Miles praises her as he claps her on the back and Amala beams at the praise.
Suddenly, they all hear commotion coming from inside the apartment. They can see beams of red
emanating from beneath the front door. They bust through the door expecting a fight. As they
enter the apartment they see Atticus, Avery and Jonathan Around the ushabti statue. The room is
a mess, like a tornado just blasted through. “What happened.” Miles asks. “We did it.” Atticus
responds.

Chapter 16
Dr Andrew Reed
There is undeniably a valuable truth about how peaceful death can be. Even though it
comes as a harsh, cruel thing, death can also be beautiful, inspiring and reassuring. In most cases
it can offer salvation. But not every person wants to die. Death is inevitable whether someone
accepts it or not. That is what makes it such a painful ordeal to watch people accept their own
untimely demise. To watch someone who refuses and struggles to accept death because it means
giving up everything. Death is not always embraced easily. When death is forced upon a person,
they may choose not to accept it. They become afraid of death, terrified of the unknown that may
come after. Most people if they found themselves in a similar situation that Dr Reed was
currently in, a part of them would wish death to take them, to release them from their miserable
existence. But there is a small, weak part of their soul that still believes they should do whatever
they can to survive. Maybe they’re just delusional, maybe they just need time to adjust to the
new reality they’ve been thrust into. That is exactly what Dr Reed felt. He knew he was going to
die at the hands of the Devil’s den, but he held out hope that if he fought, he might live through it.
‘Maybe I will survive. Maybe I will escape unscathed. Or maybe…I won’t.’ he thinks to himself
as he stares blankly ahead, waiting for death. There was silence surrounding him as death stared
at him. He tried his hardest to block out its presence, trying to ignore it but it seemed impossible.
It was not long after Dr Reed fell asleep, which was quite difficult given the circumstances, that
a loud noise woke him up. He opened his eyes, looking around, trying to find where the noise
came from. The noises kept getting louder until suddenly the lights started flickering around him.
As the lights continued flickering, and the sound got louder and louder, Dr Reed looked around

more confused. His confusion soon turned to fear. He sees Damien standing next to his cot. “Is it
time?” Dr Reed asked with a concerned look on his face. Damien nodded. “Are you ready to
die?” He asked. At this point, the anti-magic spell Atticus had cast on Dr Reed has wore off and
Andrew knew it. He was not just afraid for his own life, but also of the fact that the Devil’s den
now had all the means to bring back the most powerful demon there was. Dr Reed dedicated his
whole life to protect the world from these creatures. The knowledge that one of them was going
to inhabit him really made him mad. “I suppose so.” He answered with a resigned sigh. “I have
one favour to ask though! Tell me your villain plan. Isn’t that what you people do once you have
captured your nemesis?” Dr Reed asked with an annoyed look on his face. He hated having to
put up with Damien’s games. Damien chuckled at Dr Reed’s annoyance. “My dear Andrew.”
Damien responded in a mockingly sweet voice as he walked forward to stand beside the doctor.
“What do you believe is the primary goal of demons?” He asks. Dr Reed does not hesitate. “To
destroy.” He quickly answers. Damien shakes his head sadly and chuckles. “Not at all.” He says
with disappointment on his face. “Then what?” Dr Reed challenges, his patience wearing thin.
“No, no their true purpose is to restore. To restore faith to the unbelievers.” Damien declares.
“Faith, huh?” Dr Reed scoffs sarcastically. “Surely it’s obvious, isn’t it?” Damien questioned as
he widened his eyes. “Oh really? Enlighten me then, Mr High Priest. What is your reason for
taking me captive, to give me faith!?” Dr Reed exclaims dramatically. “You see, demons are
purity manifested. The purest of their kind. My all-time favourite demon? He represents the
perfect and ideal human. He embodies everything in the universe, not unlike God himself. He
represents the mastery of the spiritual and physical world.” Damien explains. “And what about
all the darkness and evil they bring?” Dr Reed questions sceptically as he raises an eyebrow at
Damien. “No, no, no, no. Not darkness, not evil.” Damien laughs out loud. “The darkness is only

temporary, for eventually a light shall shine forth. And a great day is sure to come when
humanity finally achieves eternal salvation. Until then, demons must continue to exist on Earth
to sustain mankind, that is their purpose, to be the guardians of mankind. That’s what they are
born for.” He states matter-of-factly. “They’re the ultimate servants of a God.” He concludes.
“So, what is your endgame?” Dr Reed asks, trying desperately to understand what Damien was
implying. “For me the endgame is simply restoration.” Damien replies nonchalantly. “To restore
my king’s rightful place on the throne. And when he returns, he is going to need subjects.” He
goes on. Dr Reed puts it all together. The Devil’s den was systematically unleashing demons
onto the world preparing it for their master’s return. “All these people, so many lives ruined and
for what? Your hubris!!” Dr Reed exclaims angrily. He was beyond pissed off, and Damien
seems to notice this as he laughed. “My dear Andrew.” He replies with a smirk. “What is life
without a sense of drama?” He asks before he continues his explanation. “If you truly want to
know the real truth of the matter, why I’m here, why you are here, why we are here, why humans
ever were created. It’s to help them achieve their goal.” Dr Reed remains silent for a moment as
he processes Damien’s words, as well as the implications. “The true architects of this world, we
all just pieces in their game. I am just a more valuable piece because I realised the truth.”
Damien continues to explain. At this point Dr Reed did not know what the man was going on
about. “You are crazy Damien, you and your filthy cult of followers. Miles, Sarah, my wife and
so many more, so many lives ruined because of you. Just kill me now, I cannot stand your
bullshit anymore.” Dr Reed says. “Oh no Andrew.” Damien coos, sounding absolutely delighted
with himself. “This isn’t about my bullshit; this is about humanity. This is about how the world
needs to be cleansed. How our world needs to be rebuilt.” He says with conviction. “But how do
you expect to accomplish that? By killing innocent people?” Dr Reed asks incredulously. “Why

yes, of course.” Damien responds with a smile as he leans in to whisper conspiratorially to Dr
Reed. “Now enough with talking, let’s get you ready for the ritual.” Damien knocks on the door
two times signalling the guards to come and carry Dr Reed out of the room he was being held in.
They grab his arms tightly and drag him through the hallway and to the back of the manor.
There is a large crowd gathered around a circle with a bonfire in the middle. Everyone
there is well dressed in fancy robes and jewellery, making them appear as if they are all royalty.
“Come my dear Andrew, let us proceed!” Damien orders as he leads Dr Reed towards the centre
of the circle. Once they arrive, everyone starts chanting a prayer that Dr Reed could barely make
out over the loud roaring noise of the fire. Dr Reed tries his best to pay attention to the chants but
he is too preoccupied trying to figure out what is happening to try and focus. The chants get
louder until one last chant rings out that sounds a lot like “SILENCE!” The chants stop. Dr Reed
looks around. He tries figure out why everyone stopped chanting. He looks past the crowd and
sees a woman carrying a knife. She walks in front of the circle and everyone watches as she cuts
her hand open. Blood drips down the knife as the woman takes a deep breath and places her
other hand on the cut and closes her eyes. As Dr Reed scans the area, he spots distinct looking
black candles placed in the cardinal directions across the large circle. These represent the demon
Belial to the north, Flereous to the south, Leviathan to the west and finally Lucifer to the east.
These demons represent all the elements of earth, air, fire and water. Once all this is setup, the
caster of the spell would then proceed to evoke Lucifer to put all the elements in a state of pure
balance. “In nomine magni, dei nostri Satanas introibo ad altare, Domini Inferi.” The woman
proceeds to recite the Incantation as the blood slowly drips from her hand which is raised with
two fingers pointing up. Her other hand with two fingers pointing down. She finishes her
incantation before opening her eyes. Everyone else joins in chanting. As they chanted, Dr Reed

watched with curious interest as nothing happened. After a few minutes of nothing, Dr Reed
began to laugh, falsely assuming that the anti-magic spell was still active. “What are we waiting
for then? Show me how strong your magic is!” He taunts. Damien looks disappointingly towards
the woman, “How did you mess this up you mewling quim!” He shouts at the woman who
stands with an embarrassed look on her face.
Meanwhile, Jonathan and chief Singh are busy briefing a group of cops. They were ready
to raid the Devil’s den. “We are going to send four men first, and hopefully they will find
something.” Singh says. Jonathan nods and agrees. “It better work Chief.” Jonathan mutters
darkly, but Singh notices the tension between them and smiles. “Jonathan, it’ll be fine.
Everything will go perfectly alright.” He reassures him. Jonathan sighs deeply and nods, “Yeah,
yeah, you’re right. Of course, you’re right. I shouldn’t worry myself like this.” He says while
shaking his head in frustration. Singh can tell that Jonathan is not completely convinced by his
words, but nevertheless, he doesn’t push it any further. Instead, he gives Jonathan one final nod
before continuing to address the cops. “Going off the feed provided by Amala, the four will
covertly enter the manor and clear a way for the rest of us to enter. Then we hit them hard. Shock
and awe everybody!” Singh declares proudly. “And once inside we take them into custody.”
Naomi adds. Chief Singh nods and turns to leave the room, but Jonathan grabs hold of his
shoulder. “Chief! We’re missing something!” He calls out anxiously. Singh turns to face
Jonathan confused. “What? What are you talking about?” He asks. Jonathan takes a deep breath,
“Dr Reed! My father. The whole reason we going in, we have to make sure we get him out. He is
not meant to be caught in the crossfire” Jonathan blurts out. Singh looks at Jonathan worriedly,
“Are you certain he’s still alive? I mean after all that you’ve told me about these people….”
Jonathan cuts Singh off by nodding frantically. “Yes, yes, I’m 100 percent positive, he’s still

there. My friends and I, we have made sure of that.” Jonathan states. Singh nods thoughtfully
before speaking again, “Okay then.” Jonathan smiles in appreciation at Singh. “Thank you Chief,
thank you. I owe you one.” Jonathan acknowledges with gratitude. Amala is with Sarah and
Steven at the precinct while Atticus and Avery head to defiance to safely store the ushabti statue
which now houses the spirit of Lucifer. Jonathan decides that for now the only thing he should
really be concerned with is getting his father out of the manor as quickly as possible.
Dr Reed currently lays still on the dirt, surrounded by evil men in fancy black robes. His
body is covered in cuts and bruises courtesy of Damien. “Please let me live!” The doctor screams
in desperation, his throat hoarse with exhaustion. Alexandra Turner, Damien’s right-hand
approaches him with a clue as to why the ritual did not work. “Damien, I believe I know what
happened. We are trying to call forth a spirit from hell which just isn’t there anymore.” Turner
says as she kneels next to the doctor. “Is it possible that the spirit was destroyed?” Damien asks
desperately as he stares at Turner hoping she had some kind of clue on the situation. “No, I don’t
think so. It’s just… Well, it's just that he is not in hell anymore. Lucifer is already on earth
somehow.” Turner answers. Damien groans and runs his hands through his hair in anger as he
looks at Turner, “Well then where the fuck is he?!” He yells out, frustrated, causing Turner to
flinch at the sudden outburst. “I don’t know Damien., It is not supposed to work any other way
but with this vessel.” Turner explains as she points at Dr Reed. “What?!” Damien exclaims
angrily. Dr Reed smirks at this revelation. ‘My god they must have done it.’ Dr Reed thinks to
himself. His Deviants had managed to remove Lucifer from the grasp of the Devil’s den. He was
relieved, happy to know that not only was there a chance he would survive but also that the den’s
nefarious plans were thwarted. A new voice interrupts his thoughts. “The question remains
though, where is he now?” The voice echoes throughout the clearing, a voice that is so familiar

yet so far away. “Tryon?” Dr Reed whispers, almost to himself. Everyone freezes when they hear
the sound of heavy footsteps approaching from behind them. “You’re late Mr. Street.” Damien
says coldly as he turns around slowly to see the source of the footsteps. “Yes well, I didn’t want
to interrupt, and as I remember, I do not answer to you.” He says sarcastically. Tryon Street is an
old acquaintance and dear friend of Dr Reed. They had previously worked on numerous cases
together, the last of which was the investigation on the Devil’s den in England all those years ago.
Dr Reed was shocked to see him there. He believed Tryon had passed away. But there he
was, seemingly in league with Devil’s den. “Why are you here?!” Dr Reed asks in disbelief as he
gets up slowly. Tryon smiles menacingly at his former friend. “I am here to retrieve the victory
you stole from me all those years ago.” He Says. Dr Reed scoffs and shakes his head in disgust,
“You betrayed everything we have ever stood for, siding with these zealots. What has become of
you my dear friend!?” Andrew exclaims. “Oh, you know me, always the rebel, never the
follower!” Tryon says with a proud smile. Dr Reed frowns. “You were never a good follower.
You were a good leader! A real soldier who fought for what he believed in. You were our most
loyal friend.” He tells him. “I am still fighting for our cause.” Tryon goes on. “I am just using
different means.” He adds. “You may have lost your morals, but do not lose you your
humanity!” Dr Reed retorts back to him. This back and forth between Dr Reed and Tryon annoys
Damien. He does not appreciate not being the centre of focus. He glares at Dr Reed in annoyance,
“Will you two shut up!? This is not a goddamn talk show and if you two do not stop being such
children you’ll have more than enough time to argue in death!” He roars. There are a few gasps
from the crowd but no one comments. They are all scared of him. “Enough of this chattering!”
Damien bellows out loud as he steps closer towards Dr Reed. “The question still remains Damien.
If Lucifer is not in hell, then where did he go?” Tryon Street reaffirms his question from earlier.

Alexandra Turner clears her throat to get their attention. “As I said, we don’t know where
Lucifer is at this moment. But I know how to find him.” She explains. “This will complicate
matters greatly.” Tryon announces with disappointment. Dr Reed smirks inwardly to himself.
“Grab him.” Alexandra commands. The guards immediately rush forward at once, grabbing hold
of Dr Reed roughly and restraining him. “Hey, hey guys, easy now.” Dr Reed says as he tries to
get free.
Jonathan and the team of cops breach the manor. Once the posse enters, Jonathan walks
over to the door leading to the main hall and opens it carefully so as not to alert anyone of their
presence. He glances quickly around the room before making a swift step towards the back exit
leading outside. “Stay alert!” Jonathan instructs. His team nods in agreement, and they begin
creeping silently towards the door, guns raised at the ready. As soon as they reach the door,
Jonathan kicks it open with his foot while simultaneously aiming his pistol at whomever might
be on the other side of it. Just then, a loud crowd can be heard and then Jonathan notices his
father on the ground blooded and bruised surrounded by them. He narrows his eyes. It is them.
The men who kidnapped his father. The very men who he had been searching for. Jonathan grits
his teeth in rage. His heart begins to beat faster as he continues looking at them, ready to fire if
they moved even a single muscle to continue hurting his father. “Jonathan be smart, we can’t just
go charging in, we are outnumbered.” Says Khakhu, a cop who volunteered for this mission.
Jonathan does not listen and fires shots at the crowd, killing four and wounding six more. The
rest scatter immediately. Jonathan and his team run towards Dr Reed. Jonathan kneels down
beside Dr Reed to examine him. “Are you alright dad?” He asks as he examines the injuries on
Dr Reed’s face, hands and legs. “Yeah, I’m fine kid.” Dr Reed assures him as he grabs
Jonathan’s hands to help himself stand up. The other cops surround them both with their pistols

drawn. Jonathan stands up as well. They are met with the sight of a dozen armed men running
towards them from inside the Manor, armed to the teeth with guns and knives as well. “Run!”
Someone shouts. Dr Reed quickly starts to push Jonathan along as he runs in the opposite
direction, Jonathan following closely behind him. They run blindly through the winding field.
Jonathan takes aim at the nearest men and shoots multiple rounds of bullets into them. Blood
splatters across his face as his bullet impacts against their flesh. Another man charges past
Jonathan with a knife in hand, but Jonathan sidesteps him and tackles the man to the ground,
punching him repeatedly. Jonathan’s team surrounds him and take out as many men as possible,
but more are coming from every angle. Soon, Jonathan’s team is overpowered by shire numbers.
They start shooting wildly in fear. Damien’s men are too much for them. “Put your weapons
down!” A voice commands from afar. “Put my weapon down? You put your weapons down.”
Jonathan retorts. “Surrender or die.” A second voice says, this time it belongs to an older man
who Jonathan vaguely remembers. He was the detective who his father always helped with
difficult cases. “Ah!” Khakhu exclaims as he drops his gun. “What happened!” Jonathan asks.
“My gun it…it just got really hot all of a sudden.” He responds. “I thought I told you to put your
weapons down.” Another voice repeats from afar. “Be careful son, that’s Damien. He’s their
leader and he is very powerful.” Dr Reed says worriedly. “I’m not scared.” Jonathan says
defensively. Dr Reed sighs in exasperation. “You should be.” He warns him quietly. Jonathan
growls in frustration. He starts firing his gun again at them, forcing them to back off slightly.
Jonathan’s gun begins to emanate heat as well but he does not let go. “Mhaaaaaa!” He screams in
pain as he continues to fire. “Damian!” Dr Reed yells after a few moments. Jonathan drops his
gun in relief as he looks down at his arm, burns covering half of it. “It will heal.” Dr Reed
promises him as he helps Jonathan cover the wound. One of the cops with Jonathan puts a hand

by his ear, where he has an ear piece communicating with chief Singh, “I know this wasn’t part
of the plan but thankfully reinforcement is coming.” He states positively. “Great.” Jonathan
mutters under his breath, but nevertheless he knows they still need to make through this
confrontation. “They’re here.” The cop says. “Dad get ready.” Jonathan adds. Dr Reed raises his
hands and begins to slowly walk towards the enemy. “We’re surrendering. You hear that,
Damien? You can put away your flames.” He calls loudly. “Dad what are you doing?” Jonathan
questions. “Distracting them!”
Suddenly, a police helicopter flies over the manor. Commandos zip line down from the
helicopter with AKs and automatic rifles. They start shooting. “Go! Go! Go!” Jonathan shouts as
he grabs his injured father and leads him towards the helicopter. Everyone on the side of the
Devil’s den scatters, with many running to their vehicles and getting out of there, away from the
manor which was currently surrounded by police. Damien is furious. “Let’s go.” Alexandra
shouts at him as she too heads for the exit. Damien pulls out a weapon from holster of one of his
men. He aims it directly at Jonathan who is running towards the helicopter. As he points and
shoots, Dr Reed notices him and steps in front of Jonathan before the bullet can hit him. The
bullet ricochets onto Dr Reed’s chest, sending him reeling backwards. “No…no, no.” Jonathan
whispers in horror. Before Damian can fire again, Jon rushes to lift his father. He carries him on
his shoulder as him and his team jump into the chopper. “Get us the hell out of here!” Jonathan
demands angrily as he climbs into the chopper with his father.

chapters.title: Dr Avery Melblac
chapters.text:
It was really not an academic or dogmatic view that propelled Dr Avery Melblac into her
current line of work. She was asked by Dr Reed to consult about people in pain. Because of him,
because of his character she was compelled to join his crusade. Presently, she roams the halls of
Defiance, unsure what to do with herself. Her mind wanders and her eyes are fixed to the floor as
she walks along the hallway. Dr Reed’s words ring in her ears. “Do not sit back and let them hurt
anyone.” As much as she wanted to stay strong and not allow others to fall victim to the evil, she
has encountered so many times, she felt torn between wanting to help and wanting to leave this
life behind. In the midst of her deep thoughts, she hears shouting, causing her to stop in her
tracks and turn toward its source, the training area. “What is going on.” Dr Avery mumbles as
she follows the sound of the voices. The closer she gets to the training area, the louder and
clearer the noise becomes. Once she gets there, she sees a familiar scene playing out. Miles and
Steven are fighting alongside Sarah and Amala. “What is with all the screaming.” She scolds
them. “Miles is pushing too hard, hey man not all of us have demon strength take it easy.”
Steven complains. “He said slow it down!” Amala warns him sternly. “Oh, come on. Let me
give you a real workout.” Miles argues back. “Miles stop!” Avery orders. Miles ignores her. He
attempts to punch Steven right in the jaw. Steven blocks Miles’ punch. Miles then manages to
kick him away and attack him again. This exchange goes on for another minute before Sarah
intervenes. “Alright you two, stop it. What are you thinking!” Miles stops moving and looks at
her with pleading eyes. “Sarah,” He begins to say. “Don’t even.” She interrupts him, crossing her
arms. Avery did not have the temperament to deal with this today. She simply sighed and turned

away from the spectacle. “Dr Melblac wait.” Sarah calls out to her. Avery looks back at her with
a questioning look and raises an eyebrow at her. “Thank you.” Sarah remarks sincerely. “I know
you cleared all our names, we are no longer fugitives, thanks again.” Sarah adds, giving Avery
an appreciative smile. Avery smiles back. “Don’t mention it.” Is all she says as she starts
walking away from them. “Wait, I haven’t heard anything about Alex Turner and her group,
Damien and the rest?” Sarah asks her. Avery glances at Sarah in confusion. “What of it?” She
answers. “Well, are we not going after them? For what they did?” Sarah persists. “I don’t have to
tell you. Now if you excuse me, I have some business to attend to.” Avery says as she makes her
way out of the room.
Avery walks briskly down the hall until she comes upon the main office. She opens the
door and walks in, finding Martin Creswell sitting at his desk typing something on his computer.
“Excuse me.” Avery says, interrupting Martin’s work. He lifts his head and looks up at Avery.
“Yes Dr.” He greets her politely. “Where is everyone else?” She questions him curiously. “In a
meeting, we are having an emergency meeting at the guildhall right now, you should probably go
and see them.” Martin tells her. Avery pauses for a moment before replying, “Thanks, have a
good day.” She says with a forced smile. When Avery exits the office, she makes her way to the
guildhall. There she finds Michael pacing back and forth impatiently while looking around
anxiously. He spots her and smiles widely when he sees her. “Finally, you came. How’s your
little circus holding up?” Michael asks frantically. “What’s going on, why are we meeting…”
Avery replies with concern etched into her features. Michael lets out a frustrated sigh. “I am not
certain, that is why I am freaking out right now.” He explains. Martin Creswell approaches
walking alongside some of the dignitaries at Defiance, Daniel Sallies and David Tenebrae, as
well as Charles, whose job was to coordinate with law enforcement linked to Defiance. They

make their way inside the guildhall where the rest of the high-ranking members of Defiance were
already assembled. The men and women in black suits sat silently around a large conference
table, while two smaller groups stood behind them. At the very end of the table sits Martin. He
had a tall, muscular figure, his short black hair arranged in a tasteful afro extenuated his features.
His glasses perched atop his nose, making him seem both more mature and mysterious at the
same time. To his left was a small woman standing slightly shorter than him. She appeared to be
in her mid-thirties, wearing a black blazer, slacks, boots, and a red dress shirt underneath. On the
top of her head was tied a red bandana. Her long blonde hair reached her waist and was braided
neatly. Her eyes were covered with thick rimmed spectacles. Next to her was a middle-aged man
wearing a tuxedo and dark sunglasses, with a bespoke hat and cane resting comfortably by his
feet. To his right were several guards carrying pistols. He appears to be a lawyer; the first thing
Avery notices about him. He gives her a nod of recognition as he catches her staring at him.
“This is an interesting gathering.” Avery states to Michael who sits beside her. “Not really, it’s
just a meeting of convenience.” He explains to her in an effort to keep her from prying into
things further. “I assume the purpose of this meeting is the capture of Alexandra Turner and
Damien Videl” Avery asks. Those two are the identified leaders of the Devil’s den. She was not
completely right. The meeting was actually to discuss the growing number of demon possession
cases across the nation and assign agents to them.
Martin begins the meeting. “We have a huge problem. We must act decisively on the
matter before it gets out of hand. The Devil’s den cult has decided to move against us once
more.” Martin announces. “How do you propose we proceed?” A male voice says from the back
of the room. Martin continues by throwing case files on the table while listening every case.
“Annabelle Michael, a young Irish woman, struggled from an early age with mental illness.

Diagnosed with epileptic psychosis. She also experienced depression and visual and auditory
hallucinations, for which she was hospitalized. With time, her symptoms intensified. Both she
and her Catholic family attributed her condition to demonic possession. Two days ago, she
disappeared from her home. Beatrice Auckland, often heard speaking in tongues and strange
guttural voices. Episodes of levitating and clinging to bedroom walls, disturbed thoughts and
revulsion of holy objects and sacred spaces, also missing.” Martin continues listing off all the
open cases. All the while his eyes never leaving the file in front of him. “We need to find all of
them. We cannot afford to lose more lives.” Martin concludes, his eyes shifting towards the men
in the room, each one with their own set of concerns regarding humanity. It was obvious by their
faces they knew how important a solution to this problem was. The silence hangs in the air like
an awkward tension until Avery speaks up. “We know what we have to do right? All of these
problems can be traced back to two people. Alex and Damien. What we have to do is pull all of
our resources to finding them.” She declares. “That will prove difficult.” Michael points out.
“They have already escaped once. We didn’t even know of them up until recently, how will we
track them down with the little we know about them?” He queries. he had a point. “Andrew Reed
found them once. We will find them again. We can’t risk leaving them out in the open any longer.
We need to focus on them and nothing else.” Avery counters. “No!” Martin responds. “You just
want revenge, that is not what we do here.” He states firmly. “He is right you know. I myself
abhor what those two did to my dear friend, but that should not distract us from our chief design.
We are not in the business of revenge.” The man with the cane retorts. This angered Avery,
considering what she had to witness. After Andrew Reed was shot in the chest, he managed to
get to the hospital before dying. He spent days in the hospital and he soon recovered. Avery was
with him every step of the way, spending her nights perched on the uncomfortable sofa by his

bedside. Jonathan organised a selection of officers to help protect Andrew while he was in the
hospital. They felt the worst was behind them. They were wrong, the worst was yet to come.
On the day Andrew was meant to be discharged, Damien Videl and Alexandra Turner
attacked the hospital. It had been only the two of them, yet they caused so much destruction.
They were ruthless, bloodthirsty maniacs. As they made their way toward Dr Reed’s room,
Jonathan’s officers tried to stop them, but they stood no a chance. With a single glance,
Alexandra had them on their knees begging for mercy. They got to Reed’s room, Jonathan was
by the door ready for them. With his gun aimed at them he commanded, “Stay right there. Don’t
you dare come near my father.” Alexandra and Damien merely scoffed and continued their
assault. “Alexandra my dear, take care of this.” Damien instructed. Suddenly Jonathan could not
move. He was unable to breathe. His body trembled uncontrollably, sweat streamed down his
face. Damien entered the room, finding Reed in the corner with Avery shielding him. “Move!”
He says to her. “No. Why are you doing this? You’ve remained hidden all this time, why cause
such a spectacle now?” Avery demands to know. Damien looks down defeated. “I learned the
truth as to why the ritual did not work. Your friend! He had a mystical spell attached to him.
When we summoned my lord, it destroyed him.” Damien appears devastated as he explains to
Avery. “NOW MOVE!!” He repeats. Avery’s whole body begins to heat up so much that it is
unbearable. She falls to her knees in excruciating pain. “Avery stop.” Andrew yells to her. He
reaches out from behind and stands in front of her, in front of Damian. “Don’t. Please don’t.”
Avery begs with tearful eyes. Damien moves closer to Dr Reed. “You wanted to know why I am
doing this. The answer is, an eye for an eye, tooth for tooth.” He says as he raises his arm and
reached for Andrew’s head, holding him tightly by his face. “Burn to nothing!” Damien
commands. Dr Reed’s whole body becomes engulfed in flames. He’s scream rattles Avery to her

core. Her eyes fill with tears as she watches Andrew’s screams of agony turn into a soft moan.
Damien’s hand leaves Andrew’s face and he turns to walk away. Alexandra Turner releases
Jonathan. Her and Damien depart the hospital, leaving Jonathan and Avery behind.
Remembering this, Avery leaves the meeting in protest. She goes straight home to her
apartment and locks herself in. When she finally makes it to her bed she collapses, her spirit
exhausted. She closes her eyes to sleep. But, as she drifts off, a familiar voice calls her name.
She opens her eyes and is greeted with a vision of an empty field of grass. The trees begin to
rustle and a gust of wind blows over. A familiar figure stands directly in front of her, he wears a
white robe, and has dark grey hair. Avery recognizes him immediately. ‘This is Andrew.’ She
thinks. The figure kneels in front of her and extends his arms towards her. “Please, move on.” He
implores. Avery shakes her head violently. She refuses. Her heart starts racing rapidly as her
mind races through the possibilities as to why and how he would be appearing to her. It could be
either her subconscious or her soul is being beckoned from its prison. The Figure reaches inside
his robes and pulls out a small black box containing a gold ring engraved with swirling designs.
His palm then presses against Avery’s forehead. He closes his eyes he chants something. “Do not
resist this touch. Do not fight it.” He says, opening his eyes. She sees her reflection mirrored in
his green gaze. Her face is pale, her expression is filled with fear, and she clutches desperately
onto the edge of her bed. “Please… please tell me, what am I seeing?” Avery stutters in fear.
“My dear Avery. Look around you. Observe your surroundings. Is anything different?” Andrew
asks, placing a reassuring hand upon her shoulder. “N-no.” Avery replies shakily. “Everything is
exactly the same as it always was. I am dreaming.” She realises. Andrew’s smile widens.
“Remember what I told you. The next time you see this ring, do not resist its touch, do not fight
it.” Andrew tells her, rising to leave. “Wait,” Avery manages to say, grasping at his hands. She

wakes up in her bed, looks out the window and realises that it is morning. Her hands still tremble
slightly. She sits up and tries to make sense of the dream. ‘What does it mean?’ Andrew’s words
play on repeat in her head. ‘What did he mean by do not resist it’s touch’ She feels overwhelmed
by these questions as she gets out of her bed to take a shower. As she was about to enter the
bathroom, another memory comes rushing back to her. The day she witnessed Andrew’s death, it
was terrifying. She had screamed and yelled and pleaded with Damien to not kill Andrew. Yet, it
was pointless. She could not save him, she was powerless. Then there was an overwhelming
feeling of guilt and regret. How could she let things end this way? She believes she should have
fought harder to protect Andrew. And after he died, all hope for humanity seemed lost. She
thought everything would die along with him. The image of his burning body flashes before her
eyes. It haunts her dreams.
After finishing her shower, Avery changes her clothes and heads downstairs where she
finds her mother sitting alone in the kitchen preparing breakfast. Avery takes a seat beside her.
They sit in silence for a while as she listens to her mom hum to herself quietly. She takes a sip of
her coffee and notices the newspaper lying beside her plate of bacon and eggs. “What’s this?”
She inquires, pulling the paper towards her. Her eyes scan the article. Her breath catches in her
throat when she reads the headline. ‘Dangerous Rosebud serial killer Strikes again.’ “The attack
was yesterday. In broad daylight. The victim was just a child.” Her mother informs her. Avery
has been tracking down a lead together with Jonathan. There has been a slew of serial killings
supposedly perpetrated by followers of Damien. The killer would leave a single rosebud on the
victim's body along with a note which always read ‘For D.V’ on it. After reading the article,
Avery was convinced that if she found out the identity of the culprit, Damien would surely be at
the end of that thread. After breakfast, she hurries out the door to find Jonathan.

They meet at a coffee shop in town. “Have you heard anything yet?” She asks him as
soon as he sits. “Yes, I just came from the lab. I found out some information, I think I might be
closer to solving the case.” He tells her. She looks at him expectantly waiting for him to continue.
He tells her that they found DNA evidence and linked it to a suspect. “My superiors do not know
I have this so we have to be careful with our approach.” Both he and Avery are distraught right
now, and they are doing all they can to avoid dealing with their emotions concerning Dr Reed’s
death. “Well, it’s a start.” Avery tells him. Jonathan sighs. “But there’s one thing I don’t get.” He
pauses, looking at her intently. “What is it?” She asks nervously, looking at the table as she
awaits whatever he has to say. “I think the lab techs may have made a mistake.” He says slowly.
“A mistake? What do you mean?” Avery asks him confusedly. “They found a large amount of
sulphur within the DNA. That’s not normal.” Jonathan explains. “No that is good news, it means
we’re on the right track.” She takes a sip of her coffee and continues, “Sulphur is a key marker of
demonic manifestation. It is found in powder form wherever demons manifest, in this case inside
the host’s DNA. The more powerful the demon, the more sulphur they leave behind. Most
demons merely leave traces of the stuff, how much of it was found?” Jonathan ponders for a
moment. “It appears to be around twenty-five grams.” He answers hesitantly. “So, assuming this
is a medium amount, what do we need to know?” She inquires. “We need to identify the demon,
and find a way to destroy it.” Jonathan responds. “Good, we need to identify it but we don’t
destroy it. We need information, right?” Avery reminds him. “Right.” Jonathan agrees. “So what
are going to do?” He asks. “A demon’s trap, all we have to do is lure the demon inside on of this
and we can ask it anything we need.” Avery responds. “Demon’s trap? What is that?” Jonathan
enquires confused. “Oh, demon traps are sigil traps for demons. Demons can’t escape once
inside the trap. All you have to do is just draw the sigil on the floor and get the demon inside of it

somehow.” Avery explains, leaning forward with excitement in her voice. “I’ll try my best to
come up with something to capture the demon. Hopefully, we won’t need to resort to any other
measures.” She adds. Jonathan looks at her with a puzzled look across his face “Where’d you
learn all this stuff.” He questions, raising an eyebrow. “Um, your father actually.” Avery replies
awkwardly. She looks away. Jonathan studies Avery closely; her face appears troubled. He
frowns slightly as an idea suddenly occurs to him. “Avery,” he begins slowly. “Have you been
sleeping alright lately?” He asks carefully. “Um yes, why?” She answers. “You seem
distracted.” He replies. “I will be fine; you just worry about yourself.” Avery mumbles, fiddling
with the rim of her empty cup. Jonathan stares at Avery, deep in thought. “Are you having
nightmares again?” He asks bluntly. “Yes, but it’s nothing I can’t handle.” Avery replies
confidently. Jonathan leans forward resting his elbows on the table. “I still see it too. That yellow
flame around him, the way he screamed. I fantasize about what I am going to do when I find
Damien. Jonathan admits. “Well, you’re a strong man, you can defeat him. But first…we need
to catch him.” She says, placing a reassuring hand on his shoulder.

chapters.title: Sarah May
chapters.text:
Magic once gave Sarah May an avenue to attain all her heart desired—protection,
divination, healing, luck, vengeance and, most of all, a sense of empowerment. It was a measure
of comfort in a cold, dark world. A tool in a world which held nothing for her except fear and
isolation. She practiced the art diligently until an unfortunate meeting with the demon Naberius.
He tried to convince her that her talents were wasted on mundane pursuits, leading her a down a
dark path which eventually ended with her imprisonment and subsequent liberation by Defiance.
She now was at place that could nurture her talent and keep her from the darkness. But she was
still haunted by Naberius. No matter how hard she tries to forget about him or the pain he made
her cause and endure, each time he enters her thoughts. His haunting smile is burned onto her
mind, mocking her every moment she closes her eyes. He continues to torment her. His presence
is forever imprinted into her soul. “Atticus, it’s getting worse.” Sarah is beside herself as she
tries to explain to Atticus, “He’s been appearing more and more frequently and each time he
does, I feel myself giving into him as I did once before.” She states, pacing back and forth
anxiously. “It doesn’t help that I’m practicing witchcraft again and whenever we are in danger I
just automatically tap into his dark energy. I’m so vulnerable to his influence right now.” She
concludes, running her fingers through her hair in frustration. “I need your help.” She adds. “You
must learn to command him.” Atticus says simply. “The cosmic conflict exploding all around us
will have eternal implications. I fear we might need to rely a little on that darkness inside of you
if we are to win.” He warns. “But how do I control it?” She asks, furrowing her eyebrows. “Do
not focus too hard on controlling it or else you might lose yourself entirely and fall victim to its

power. You must learn how to use your talents to protect yourself against the demon’s
influence.” Atticus instructs. “Will you teach me this?” She asks curiously. “Of course. You are
a part of my team. Together we will fight this battle.” Atticus responds.
Atticus has always been a man of hubris, and here again he was certain that he could
assist Sarah to tame Naberius. Sarah gives Atticus a grateful smile as she thanks him. “But I wish
I had some sort of instruction manual to go by.” Sarah states. Such a manual truly did exist. The
Book of Shadows grimoire makes the perfect ally for any practising Witch. It contained
everything Sarah needed. The last time she saw it, Alexandra Turner had it in her possession.
Naberius needed the book as well because it was so powerful even to demons. Naberius had used
the book of shadows before and so understood it’s power. “Defiance is going through changes
right now; I may not have the time to teach you. Be patient, ok?” Atticus tells her gently. “If I
can teach you what I know then it should be enough.” He tells her, smiling encouragingly. “I
hope so.” Sarah responds, giving him a soft smile in return. They part ways and Sarah heads to
the library. There were plenty of books that held second hand spells, rituals and all sorts of occult
knowledge. She loved it there, it had become home for her over the past few months. She walks
towards the aisle filled shelves, searching for the appropriate book. Eventually, she finds it. A
dusty old volume on the far side of the shelf, half hidden behind some others. She grabs the book
and pulls out the chair in front of her desk. She starts flipping through the pages, trying to locate
the spell she originally used when she first got involved with Naberius. Sarah was a reckless
witch; she was never afraid to take chances. Unfortunately, that is what led to all her problems.
When she reached the spell, she hesitated for a moment, her stomach twisting uncomfortably as
she thought about facing the demon inside of her again. With a heavy sigh, she flips open the
book to a specific page, where she stops. She scans the page, examining the spell, hoping she

would not make a mistake. The last thing she wanted was to be stuck forever with her demon,
