especially after what happened earlier in the week. Slowly she lowers herself to the ground as
she places the book open in front of her on the rug. Her heart beats faster and harder than ever,
she feels as though her heart is going to jump from her chest at any moment. She takes a breath
and then exhales shakily, closing her eyes as she concentrates on the spell. She needs to
concentrate, to think. The only thing that comes through her head are these words: ‘In blood and
fire, let us burn away the evil.’ Sarah’s eyes snap open. This spell had worked perfectly; the
demon was bound inside of her. She sits back in relief and lets out a long, shaky breath. The next
step was to enter her on mind, to confront the demon. She knew exactly how to do that. The
magic within her was very much alive. It flowed through her veins like water. It called out to her.
She focused on the feeling of it rushing through her veins. She visualised a picture of the demon,
pictured it in her mind. When the image finally came, she opened her eyes again. She took a
shaky breath as she stared at the book. ‘How do I summon this?’ She thinks nervously. She
stares at the page once more and repeats her actions. The image she created in her mind becomes
clearer. The image grows larger until it fills the entire pages. Then it slowly fades away until a
tall figure stands in front of her. It has dark red skin, two sharp horns protruding from its skull,
black eyes, the wings of a raven on its back and pointed ears. Sarah can feel an immense aura of
darkness surrounding it. It surrounds her as well. ‘That must be Naberius’ She realises in shock,
recognising it immediately. She hadn’t summoned another demon since before Naberius
possessed her. Fear and hesitation creep through her. Naberius smirks at her, clearly amused by
her fear and nervousness. “What’s so funny?” She says angrily. Naberius laughs. “Fear makes
fools of us all.” He whispers, sending Goosebumps down her spine. The demon slowly floats
closer towards her, causing her breathing to quicken. It’s cold air seeping through her jacket. She

looks up into those sinister black eyes and sees pure hate staring back at her. “I would never
harm one as innocent as you. Never. My dear Sarah May.” He murmurs. He reaches out his hand
and slowly caresses her cheek with his long-clawed finger tips. Sarah shudders uncontrollably,
trying not to tremble. She looks down, unable to hold his gaze. “Hmm, I sense a familiar
presence…so…. close to me.” The creature remarks, turning his nose up to sniff the air. Sarah
looks up again to meet those black glowing eyes. “I am going to destroy you.” She says
confidently. “We shall see about that.” He replies sarcastically. The demon then opens his arms
wide, spreading his claws in front of him. “Come here Sarah May. I am waiting for you.” He
calls mockingly. Sarah is frightened but she refuses to show it. As he speaks, the demon’s voice
echoes across her vast mind. “Come to me Sarah May.” She hesitates, not wanting to be tricked
by him. “Come to me Sarah May. Come to me.” He pleads mockingly. “Why? So I can become
your slave again!” She spits furiously. Naberius shakes his head as he smiles, enjoying Sarah’s
response. “I am sorry about that,” He replies. “But I have something special planned for you.”
He pauses for dramatic effect. “My friends.” He proclaims. Sarah narrows her eyes in confusion.
Naberius laughs as he continues. “They’re coming for you, Sarah May.” He says menacingly.
Sarah’s heart begins to race, panic beginning to set in. He then proceeds to say, “And when they
come for you…. they’ll kill you. But before then….” Naberius trails off and then suddenly
rushes towards her, grabbing her neck roughly as he drags her close to him. “Your fate is to
become mine. All you have to do is agree to become my vessel.” Sarah struggles as she feels her
windpipe being crushed. Her head feels lighter, she blinks and it is gone, replaced with complete
clarity as she looks around the room, confused. Her vision returns and she is back in the library.
She looks at the book in her hands, mortified. “What have I done!?” She asks herself in disbelief.
She closes the book tightly as tears begin to fall from her eyes. “I’m no good! I can barely

control myself when it comes to using my gifts. How will I survive a battle against Naberius?”
She cries out loud. She cannot fight Naberius alone, but she knows that if she does not succeed,
then the rest of her team could surely die. Even though she just became close with them only
recently, the idea of losing them was unbearable to her. What if she failed to defeat Naberius,
and she lost all hope of stopping him from taking control of her body permanently? What then?
What would happen to her? She concludes that she cannot live in constant fear of what might
happen, she needs to act decisively. She picks up the book with trembling fingers, still struggling
to calm her erratic heartbeat.
Sarah takes a deep breath and opens the book once again. “Let us burn away the evil.”
she recites to herself. “Burn away the evil. Let us burn away the evil.” She mutters under her
breath. She tries to focus as best she can. She must become stronger so she can defend her team
properly. As she’s doing this, Miles enters the library finding her on the floor. “Sarah? Are you
ok?!” He exclaims. “I need to learn this spell; I don’t think I can stop him from possessing me
again.” She says desperately. Miles kneels down beside her. “I take it you are ready to talk about
what has been happening with you.” He says softly. Sarah nods sadly. “Can we go somewhere
more comfortable?” She asks. Miles nods and offers her a reassuring smile. The pair sit down at
one of the tables in the library, Sarah leaning forward, elbows resting on the table and chin
propped up in her hands. Miles watches her closely. After a few moments of silence, Sarah turns
her head to look at him, eyes pleading with him to understand. Miles nods in agreement, sensing
she wants to tell him whatever it is she’s keeping bottled inside. He waits patiently for her to
speak. “Months ago, I started having nightmares. Nightmares about Naberius possessing me
again. And it was getting worse every time, the demon was becoming more and more insistent
each time.” Sarah explains. Miles frowns slightly, he knows how terrifying that is. “Did you tell

anyone about this?” He asks her gently. Sarah nods. “Yes, I told Atticus months ago, he hasn’t
been able to help.” She explains. Miles is silent as he contemplates her story. He wants to be
supportive of Sarah, he really does. He’s experience with demons is fraught with despair and
carnage. He can relate with Sarah. Amala and Steven also come to the library, “Hey, what are
you two nerds doing just hanging out at the library huh.” Steven teases. Sarah rolls her eyes at
him. “Just talking about things.” She answers curtly. “Mind if we take a sit?” Amala asks
politely. “Not at all.” Sarah smiles, waving her hand in their direction as she moves a chair over
so both of them can sit down. “So,” Amala says casually, “what are we doing right now?” Sarah
smiles sheepishly at her and clears her throat, “Oh, I was just telling Miles about how I think I
might still have my demon inside of me.” Sarah tells her friends quickly. Steven and Amala
collectively raise a brow at her in surprise. “Seriously?” They both exclaim. Sarah shrugs, “It’s
pretty crazy isn’t it?” They both chuckle at that. “Well,” Steven says excitedly. “I wouldn’t be
too sure about that. You’re still pretty human to me.” He reminds her, grinning. Sarah scoffs,
rolling her eyes playfully. “I have these dreams of him controlling me again. Every night I wake
up crying and shaking. I haven’t slept well since…well, ever.” She sighs sadly. Amala and
Steven exchange worried glances. They’re both aware of how difficult it can be for someone to
deal with having a careless demon in their head. “Have you spoken to Dr. Melblac about this?”
Steven inquires curiously. Sarah shakes her head. “No, I am a Deviant now, how’s it going to be
if I’m still attached to the other side? You saw how they treated us when we first got here. We
were no different than the demons we now fight against. I am afraid she will put me back in a
cage.” Sarah explains bitterly. “Hey.” Steve says softly as he grabs her hand. “Don’t worry, I
promise you, we won’t let Dr. Melblac hurt you or put you back in a cage. She will have to catch
us first.” He chuckles. Amala smirks. “Steve has a point.” She adds slyly. “Ok, fine.” Sarah says,

giving in. “But I don’t want any part of this for you. If you guys end up suffering because of
me…” She adds. “Sarah, stop worrying about yourself, we’re in this together.” Steven says
sternly. Sarah sighs. “Alright. Just make sure nothing bad happens to you guys.” She smiles
weakly. Amala and Steven share a glance, wondering if there’s anything they can do to make
sure she feels safe, especially considering their own experiences with demons. “I’ll protect you
guys. Trust me.” Sarah reassures them. Steven gives her hand a gentle squeeze and the team
exchange relieved smiles. They spend a couple of hours chatting quietly and exchanging jokes.
Eventually, the conversation is brought to a halt. Sarah yawns sleepily and looks up as the time
approaches 1:00 a.m., indicating it’s about time for bed. She stands slowly, stretching her
cramped muscles. Miles, Amala and Steven stand as well, preparing to leave. “Thanks for today.
It meant so much to be able to talk to you like this without any judgment or pressure.” Sarah
smiles gratefully at them. They part ways and everyone heads to their respective rooms.
It’s 3:00 a.m. Not a soul is awake at this hour. Sarah lies in bed staring up at the ceiling
as she thinks about the day she had. She is exhausted, physically and mentally. She lets out
another frustrated sigh, turning onto her side as she stares at her clock. It reads 3:02 a.m.
Reluctantly, she gets up from her bed and leaves the bedroom, heading for the bathroom. She
shuts the door behind her and removes her clothes as she steps into the shower. She turns the
water on, letting the warm spray hit her face. It was refreshing after a long day. She allows the
warmth to wash over her for a moment, revelling in the feeling. Finally relaxing, she shuts the
water off and steps out. Turning the knob, she notices that the temperature has dropped
significantly. She shivers in disgust. Stepping out of the shower, she wraps her towel around her
body, drying herself off as she makes her way toward the closet to retrieve her pyjamas. After
slipping the soft blue cotton t-shirt and sweatpants on, she exits the bathroom finding herself in a

cold, dark corridor. She can see her breath coming out in white puffs as she walks forward with
caution. As if on command, every footstep echoes loudly in her ears. The silence is deafening as
each step, in contrast, is heavier than the last. There is nothing but a sense of foreboding hanging
heavy in the air. She stops abruptly at one point, her eyes widening as she sees a black raven
with a pair of glowing yellow eyes watching her. She freezes. It flies closer. The raven opens its
mouth to reveal a thorned bird's tongue. As it begins to advance towards Sarah, her mind goes
blank. “What is happening?” She says to herself. Her limbs feel leaden. “Come with me…” A
low hoarse voice growls through the air. Her feet carry her forward without thinking as the
monster advances upon her slowly. “What am I doing?” She wonders as she continues to walk
forward. The raven raises its beak, causing her to freeze again. Before she realizes what is
happening, she finds herself on the grounds of the hidden storage cellars of Defiance. She looks
up. The beast looms over her. “Release him.” It tells her silently before it disappears inside a
locked safe, leaving only her shaking alone in the cellar.
Dr Avery Melblac is in her bed, deep asleep. In her dreams, there is a woman who
appears to be wearing a bodysuit made out of pure light. The figure is tall and slim, her black
hair cascading down her back. It is as if time itself is slowing down. The woman approaches her
slowly, stopping just inches from Avery's face. Then, in an instant, everything around her stops
moving. Everything is motionless until finally, she notices the ring on the woman’s finger. It is
as the apparition of Dr Andrew Reed described it. “Who are you.” She questions. The woman
does not respond and turns to walk away. “Hey!” Avery exclaims, following the woman. She
follows her through her bedroom door and suddenly she was at Defiance HQ. Dr Avery Melblac
stood confused. She looks around for answers. “Where am I? What is going on?” She asks.
Avery continues to follow the woman down the halls of Defiance until finally she realises where

she is. “How did I get here.” She wonders silently. The woman leads Avery towards the cellar.
“No. Please no. Leave me alone.” The person inside screams. Avery rushes inside to see what it
is the woman wants her to see. As she enters, Avery encounters Sarah May, holding the ushabti
statue which contained the spirit of Lucifer. It has been kept in a locked safe since they captured
him. Sarah raises her arms grabbing the statue by both hands, as she lets out a powerful scream.
She drops her arms, breaking the statue on the floor. “No!!” Avery screams at the sight,
immediately waking up in her bed.

chapters.title: Sarah May
chapters.text:
“Sarah May, do you like drums? Do you like cigarettes, dominoes, rum?” She sits there,
at the drivers seat of a stolen car listening intently to his stories, not saying a word, just listening
to him. He is watching her carefully as she remains quiet, simply nodding once or twice to
acknowledge he’s been talking, and then shaking her head slightly whenever he says disturbing.
“I love all music.” He says simply after a few moments. He grins widely at her silence. She does
not say a word. He shifts uncomfortably beside her and decides that now would be a good time
for a distraction as he reaches for a bag sitting at the corner of the dashboard. He pulls out what
seems to be an album. He flips it open and reveals the picture inside it; it depicts a small young
girl surrounded by many other girls, laughing happily as they pose for the camera. His eyes shift
back to look at her briefly, noticing that she has not said anything yet. He clears his throat as he
holds the album out to her. “Do you like this?” He asks cautiously. She shakes her head in
confirmation. He releases a breath of relief, smiling widely. He watches as she struggles to not
close her eyes, resting her head against the headrest as he flips through the album, admiring each
photo of the girls’ faces. Sarah turns the radio on to try and drown out his voice. A song starts
playing, filling the vehicle with an uplifting melody. She leans her body against the door,
allowing her head to droop forward. “My favourite band ever.” He says proudly. She closes her
eyes for a moment and listens to the song. He watches as her features change, her whole posture
changes as she relaxes more into the leather seats. She has been driving for 8 hours straight and it
begins to show. She is too tired to drive. He notices this as he speaks again, “You seem sleepy.”
He observes with a teasing smile as he glances at her. “Just shut up.” She mutters, her voice

barely audible over the music blaring in the car. She spots a rest stop by the road and pulls up to
refresh. She grabs the purse that was in the car when she stole it, standing up and exiting the car,
slamming the door behind her. She crosses the parking lot and makes her way to the restrooms,
locking herself inside a stall and sliding down against the wall. Her body is trembling and her
hands are shaking violently. She takes a deep shuddering breath and brings a hand to her
forehead. Her heart pounds relentlessly in her chest as she attempts to calm herself. She rests her
head on the closed toilet tank and tries to breathe calmly. “Oh god... what did I do…” She
whispers to herself. Her legs begin to tremble uncontrollably as she slides further down the wall.
Her breathing becomes laboured. She tries to pull herself together, forcing herself to stop crying.
But she just cannot help it. “Please…” She breathes softly. Tears stream down her face as she
tries hard to stop sobbing. Suddenly, she hears a voice outside the stall. “Hey Sarah, why aren’t
you getting out?” The voice called out. Without thinking about it, she opens the stall. Standing
just a few feet away stands a woman wearing a navy-blue windbreaker, her grey hair styled into
a buzz cut. “Are you okay?” She asks concerned. “Yeah.” Sarah replies, quickly wiping a single
tear off her cheek. “I’m fine…” She assures the woman, walking past her towards the exist.
“Let’s get something to eat.” He says to her snarkily. She walks into the convenient store
to her left, grabbing two bottles of water, a packet of chips, and several packets of chicken
nuggets. As she is standing by the till to pay, a man wearing a cassock, a long garment worn by
Roman Catholic and other clergy stands beside her. The man slowly begins to be nervous and
stutters as he speaks. “Satan, Satan!” He falls to the floor petrified by fear and begins screaming
out incoherent phrases. The cashier looks at the man strangely. The man keeps shouting. “Please
don’t kill me!” His cries echo throughout the small shop. Sarah quickly grabs her things and runs
back towards the car. The man is still lying on the floor, shaking and moaning loudly. When

Sarah gets to the car, she places the food and drinks in the back sit and slams the door shut. As
she is climbing into the driver’s seat, she can hear him still crying. She puts the key in the
ignition, starts the engine, and drives off fast, turning onto the main road. She drives for miles in
silence, the only sound being the occasional click of the wheel under her fingers as she pushes
the accelerator all the way down. The roads are narrow and twist and turn. She feels lost, trying
to remember which direction she came from. Finally, she reaches the exit of the town. There is
nothing around except hills and endless cornfields stretching endlessly in all directions. She exits
and heads down an old dirt road, passing by abandoned farms, barns and the occasional house
that had seen better days. Eventually, she sees the sign pointing towards the mountains,
indicating that she is near where she needs to go. After a couple of minutes of navigating through
the hills, she passes a large wooden sign that reads ‘The Rookery’, a place that is notorious for
being a ghost town, secluded from everything. All the while, she is trying to push down her fears
as she continues on her journey. “This will work… this will work.” She repeats in her head,
trying desperately to convince herself that the plan she has conceived of will work. Her hands
begin to shake harder as she speeds along, hoping for the best. “Where are you going Sarah
May?” He asks her. She glances at him briefly then back at the road. “Nowhere.” She says. She
continues on the winding mountain path, her mind filled with panic, her vision beginning to blur
as tears stream down her face. As she turns a corner on the mountain road and slows the car
down, she realizes that she cannot make it any farther. Her head drops down onto the steering
wheel, taking a few deep breaths to regain some composure before removing the key from the
ignition. The place she needs to go to was now within walking distance. As she climbs out of the
car she feels her stomach roll unpleasantly. She shuts her eyes tight, clenching her fists and
inhaling deeply, trying to clear her mind. The nausea subsides after a minute or so and she opens

her eyes looking around. She begins to walk, doing so for 45 minutes. She looks across the field
to her right at an old cabin standing amongst the corn stalks. It’s covered in rust and graffiti. The
roof of the cabin has begun to collapse, falling down onto the ground and covering everything
around it completely. The old rickety stairs leading up to the front door have rotted away leaving
only splintered wood remnants. “Come on Sarah May, we’re almost there. Just one more step.”
She encourages herself. She slowly climbs the steps towards the entrance. When she arrives at
the doorstep, she takes a deep breath, before pushing the door open, wincing at the smell of
smoke, blood and filth. Although the outside was in shambles, the inside of the cabin was
somewhat liveable. This small cabin in the middle of nowhere has been in Sarah’s family for
years, last owned by her dear grandma before she died and abandoned the place. “Lovely.” He
says to her sarcastically. She ignores him and moves inside, glancing around, noting the broken
furniture and mouldy walls, and dust coating every surface. She walks further in. “It doesn’t
really look like much.” She remarks to herself, before moving on to examine the area around her.
Everything is dusty and decaying and the floors are dirty and uneven with holes everywhere. In
one corner she finds what appears to be an old bed frame, lying on its side, having fallen off the
wall at some point, but no matter how hard she tries she cannot budge it from the spot where it
landed, stuck firmly against the opposite corner of the room. “You really are going to stay here
Sarah May? It’s filthy.” He comments once more. She rolls her eyes and sighs, turning to face
him. “I don’t plan on being here long.” She tells him. He stares at her curiously. “Oh?” He asks.
“I want to kill myself.” She retorts. He laughs quietly to himself, rolling his eyes. She frowns,
her expression changing. “Look, I understand I am not the best companion but what makes you
think doing that will get rid of me? I am telling you right now, it won’t. So why bother?” He asks
as he chuckles slightly, shaking his head. After she released him from the ushabti statue, Lucifer

had taken residence within Sarah. Sarah become obsessed with killing him. He was the devil
after all. She ran away from Defiance, from her friends, from Miles. She came here to kill herself
hoping she would take Lucifer down to hell with her. As well as her long-time tormentor,
Naberius.
She sits on a rickety chair, leaning against the far wall and gazing intently at the figure
seated before her. He is sitting on an overturned bucket, cross-legged and resting his elbows
upon his knees, his fingers interlaced together. He is silent for a few moments, seeming
contemplative, before speaking again, looking intently at Sarah. “So you know, Sarah May. If
you were really serious about killing yourself, why haven’t you done it?” He asks calmly. “Who
said I haven’t done it.” She admits. “Oh? have you now.” He asks innocently, smiling slightly,
waiting for Sarah’s answer. She lets out a heavy sigh and bows her head. “Before I left Defiance,
I ingested a death potion, it should be taking effect any minute now.” She answers. Lucifer nods
his head slowly, looking down towards the ground. “So you’re planning on ending your
incredible life, here of all places.” He asks rhetorically. “Of course.” She replies plainly. “And
what about Miles and the Deviants? What do you plan to do when they realize you’re gone.” He
questions, raising an eyebrow. “They don’t know where I am. It’ll be a while until they find me.
Besides, I am doing this to protect them, and everyone else.” She shrugs her shoulders. “Protect
them from me?” He asks. “Yes.” She responds bluntly, nodding. “Whatever gave you the idea
that anyone needed protection from me?” He asks amused. Sarah does not say anything. “I can
assure you, whatever you think you know about me just forget about it.” Lucifer continues,
raising his voice slightly. Sarah remains silent. “I have had many Avatars over my eternal
existence. The last one fancied himself a witch and he got banished for it. You my dear Sarah
May, you are…” Sarah cuts him off before he can finish, interrupting him with anger. “Just stop

talking!” She yells, standing up and walking over to Lucifer. “You are the reason for every bad
thing to ever happen in the history of ever! You are the literal ruler of all darkness. You created
demons, which have tormented me and my friends, I don’t want to hear whatever you have to
say to me!” She shouts. Lucifer stays silent, staring at Sarah in amazement as she storms out of
the room, slamming the door behind her. He appears beside her, looking around the field. “I have
missed views such as these.” She hears him whisper to himself, sounding quite calm. “Don’t you
dare say another word to me. You are evil and selfish and you caused so much pain for us.”
Sarah yells. They both remain silent. Sarah waits for a response. “Is that what you really believe
Sarah May? That all this pain and suffering was my doing? Well… if that is so, I must say, the
only satisfaction I find is when people see my truth, their minds change so quickly.” Lucifer says,
smirking. He begins pacing around the field, seemingly lost in thought for several minutes,
before he stops abruptly. “Do you want to know the truth Sarah May? Hmm, shall I,
enlightenment you?” He asks mockingly. Sarah stays silent. As they walk, the death potion she
ingested begun to take effect, making the world spin faster and faster. “No…” She responds,
feeling dizzy. She falls backwards onto the grassy field, hitting her head on a rock near by.
Darkness begins clouding her vision.
When she comes to again she is lying flat on her back, with Lucifer hovering over her,
smirking and seemingly relieved that she woke up. Her body is writhing around in pain, causing
her heart to race. Lucifer leans over her worriedly, looking straight into her eyes. “Did you die?”
He asks. “Sarah May… Sarah… May….” His smile grows larger as she starts gasping for air,
struggling to breath. “Stop it!” Sarah cries out. She tries to lift herself up. “Where are we?” She
asks him, looking around noticing they were no longer on the field anymore. They appeared to
be somewhere in the woods now, surrounded by trees and bushes. “This isn’t real is it? It’s all

part of your twisted game. You want to torture me? Is that what this is about?” Sarah is starting
to feel more and more weak. She is exhausted and barely able to move. “I’m getting tired of your
games.” She whispers, letting out a small cough. She sees a shadowy figure approaching them
from behind. She turns around, squinting her eyes, trying to focus her vision. She sees it’s a
person standing a few meters away. The silhouette becomes clearer as he approaches. “Hey, can
you help me sir.” She pleads, desperately wanting the person to help her. “Where am I?” She
asks, but the man does not answer. “He cannot hear or see you, Sarah.” Lucifer says to her.
“What do you mean?” She asks. “Look around, we’re clearly not in Kansas anymore.” He tells
her. She glances around and realizes that she is indeed in an entirely different landscape; the
forest surrounding them is full of tall trees and thick underbrush. A bright blue sky stretches
above them, and the sun shines down brightly upon the earth, warming the leaves below her feet.
“Watch closely, I’m about to make my entrance.” Lucifer announces. With a slight wave of his
hand he disappears, reappearing a foot in front of the man. A smirk plastered across his lips.
“Hello friend.” Lucifer greets with a warm tone. Sarah watches as Lucifer interacts with the man.
The man is short with dark brown hair and brown eyes. There is a hint of fear in them. Sarah can
see that something else is there too, something darker and much more terrifying. “Friend, you are
trying to rule the world I see. Well, I can give you everything. Power, everything you touch can
be golden.” Lucifer assures the man who stands before him, a look of confusion covering his
features. “How can I help you?” The man asks. “The question is, how may I help you.” Lucifer
muses. As Sarah watches, she wonders why he is showing her this. Lucifer smiles, placing a
finger underneath the man’s chin, lifting his head up to look straight into his deep brown eyes.
“Tell me, what exactly are you afraid of?” He asks slyly. The man blinks, confused. “Afraid of?”
He asks unsurely. “I can show you what you fear, but I need to know your name.” Lucifer states.

“My name?” He repeats, even more confused. “Your true name.” Lucifer adds. “Why?” the man
asks, furrowing his eyebrows. “Because I am the king of creation and I want to know.” He
explains simply. “What?!” The man exclaims, completely shocked. “I am lord of creation. This
whole place is mine.” Lucifer informs him. “I’m…I’m afraid of everything.” The man confesses.
“What things?” Lucifer asks curiously. “Everything.” The man answers. “I can make it so you
are afraid of nothing, all you have to do is become my vessel. Carry me through time and all
which you desire will be yours.” Lucifer proclaims arrogantly. “I don’t understand.” The man
insists. Lucifer laughs lightly. “There is more, friend. The world is filled with wonders yet
untold.” He explains. “I will show you.” He adds. This goes on for a while as Sarah watches.
Moments pass by as Lucifer convinces this man to allow him to possess his body, until finally
the man relented. As the man walks away with new found power, Lucifer reappears beside Sarah.
“If you are trying to convince me that I am wrong about you, you did a terrible job.” She remarks.
“Just watch.” He says to her. Suddenly the world around them begins to move and change. Sarah
is taken through a time lapse of the man’s whole life. She sees how he lived with Lucifer
possessing him and all the things he did with that power until the day that he died. Lucifer begins
to explain. “I did not make him do all those things. That is not what demons do. They don’t make
you do anything; they merely lend you the power to do what you truly want. It is not demons that
corrupt humanity, it is humanity which corrupts demons.” Sarah begins to wonder. “That is not
true, people who... people become monsters; we do terrible things we wouldn’t normally do. I
did terrible things when Naberius had me in his grasp. Miles he... that was not who he is and that
is not who I am.” Sarah argues, shaking her head. Lucifer seems very pleased at her words. “Yes,
yes you are starting to get it.” He replies smugly. “There was one who first pointed out this
inherent flaw in humanity, and when he did, he was punished for it. Sentenced to the very end of

time.” Lucifer reveals. “I am clawing my way forward, surviving through countless hosts across
time. And each time I possessed a host, I saw how their souls corrupted my light. Sarah, hell is
not a place. It is within all of you! Demons are forged within the darkness of your very souls.”
Sarah looks at Lucifer with wide eyes. “You can’t possibly be telling the truth.” She says quietly.
Lucifer shakes his head slowly. “It’s true, I’ve seen it myself.” He answers honestly. Sarah looks
horrified. “But I don’t want to hurt anyone. I love my family, my friends.” She says, tears
running down her cheeks. Lucifer stares at her, puzzled. “But you did.” He replies. Sarah lets out
a sob, burying her face in her hands. She does not want to believe him, but a part of her does. It
all makes sense to her. Suddenly, she wakes up back in the field.

chapters.title: Miles Kelly
chapters.text:
Trudging through the bush in the darkness, Miles fought the urge to call for help. He was
alone with Steven on a mission for Defiance tracking a legion deep in the woods. It was their
first real mission on their own, and so he was understandably nervous. They were both still
reeling from the loss of their dear friend Sarah May. They had heard from Dr Avery Melblac that
she was once again under the control of Naberius and that she is the one who released Lucifer.
The entire situation had left Miles incredibly shaken and conflicted. Despite their efforts, they
had yet to locate Sarah. Miles is beginning to think that Sarah might be lost after all. He shudders
thinking about it. He could see the stars above them twinkling in the night sky. The moon glares
down at them like a cold, unforgiving eye, and Miles feels as though he is being watched. “We
are not being followed, are we?” Steven asks him in a hushed whisper. Miles shakes his head and
takes a quick glance around. “No, it has to be wind, the building where the legion was located is
at the far end of this forest.” He informs Steven. “How many demons are there exactly?” Steven
asks him. “Atticus said there are only about three lower class demons, I’m sure we can handle
it.” Miles explains. Steven sighs deeply, looking up at the stars. “Yeah, I’m sure we can.” He
agrees. “Do you have any idea where Sarah might be?” Steven continues, “If Naberius was able
to manipulate someone as pure as Sarah...” He pauses and swallows thickly, “What hope is there
for us.” Steven frowns sadly, remembering their encounter just three days ago. Miles looks over
at him. “She seemed fine the last time I saw her. She is probably scared right now.” Miles says
softly, biting his lip slightly in thought. “I miss her.” Steven admits, his expression growing
softer with every word. “Me too.” Miles responds. They walk for a couple more minutes until

they reach the edge of the woods. Miles stops, “Okay, here goes nothing.” He mutters to himself.
He looks up at the sky and closes his eyes, concentrating hard. For several minutes nothing
happens. Miles tries again and again but still nothing happens. “Maybe we should go inside.”
Steven suggests. “I’m not sure that’s a good idea.” Miles tells him nervously. “Come on. They’ll
definitely hear your giant footsteps if you transform now, I thought this was a stealth mission.”
Steven insists, walking towards the building. “Steven, come back!” Miles yells. He knows that
Steven is not listening. Miles follows closely, trying not to fall behind as he runs through the
trees. They quickly arrive at the building. Miles starts searching around for demon scent. His
nose is suddenly overcome by the sweet smell of blood as he approaches the structure. “This
isn’t right.” Miles whispers to himself. Steven glances back at Miles and shrugs, going in without
him. Miles sighs loudly again before following. As they get to the door, Steven whispers to
Miles, “it’s locked.” “Well, open it.” Miles responds. Steven nods, turning the knob and slowly
opening the door. They enter cautiously, weapons ready. As soon as the door opens fully, Miles’
heart drops. There are so many bodies lying all around the room. “Fuck!” Stevens curses.
Miles scans the room, horrified by the sight. “This is bad!” He exclaims. Steven turns to
look at him. “They’re dead! Even the children!” He states worriedly. He looks down at the
carnage and Miles joins him. There are corpses everywhere. “Atticus warned we might find
something like this. Everywhere there has been a sudden rise of mass deaths. I guess this was
one of them.” Miles states calmly. Steven sighs deeply. “Wherever we are, I hope we get out of
here soon.” He replies. “Let’s check the perimeter.” Miles suggests. Steven nods and the pair
carefully begin to search the area. As they walk along, Miles’ heart starts pounding faster. “Shit”
He mutters, pulling out his weapon. “What?” Steven asks curiously. “Look.” He points to an
unconscious girl lying next to a table in the centre of the room. Miles rushes over to her. Steven

stands frozen. The child is lying on the floor, covered in cuts and bruises. Her face is badly
beaten. “What are you doing?!” Steven shouts, panicked. Miles kneels next to her and puts his
ear to her chest. He gasps loudly in shock. “Oh God.” He whispers. “What is it? What’s
wrong?!” Steven asks frantically. “She’s still alive.” Miles says. Steven stares down at the child
in disbelief. He shakes his head and kneels down next to her. “Is she okay? Is she breathing?” He
questions urgently. Miles shakes his head. “I don’t know. If it wasn’t for the fact that I can feel
her heartbeat, I would say she is dead. But it’s faint. We need to get her somewhere safe.” He
says. “Are you nuts!? We don’t even know if she is normal. What if she’s a demi?” Steven snaps.
“We don’t have any other options at this point. Let’s just bring her with us.” Miles counters.
Steven hesitates for a moment but eventually relents. “Fine, we’ll take her with us. Come on.”
He says. The two of them pick up the girl and carefully make their way back outside. Once they
are outside again, they carry her in silence until they reach a clearing and then place her gently
onto the ground. They turn and stare down at her helplessly. “Please wake up.” Miles pleads.
Steven remains silent, his heart breaking. “I’ve never seen anything like this. She’s got to be like
13 years old.” Steven says worriedly. Miles turns to look at him and then returns his gaze to the
girl. She begins to open her eyes slightly, groaning lightly. “Hi, are you alright?” Miles asks her
gently. “Huh? Where am I? Who are you” She asks, confused. Miles smiles down at her
reassuringly, holding out a hand for her to grab hold of. “My name is Miles Kelly, and this is
Steven.” He says kindly. The girl slowly sits upright and stares at him blankly, clearly dazed.
“What’s your name!” Miles adds excitedly. She flinches slightly at his loud voice but finally
speaks. “Belial...” She breathes shakily, staring up at them. Miles smiles. “That’s a beautiful
name. Where do you live?” He inquires. The girl seems surprised by the question, blinking
owlishly. “I…don’t know.” She answers quietly. Miles looks at Steven who stares at her

curiously. “Where did you come from?” He questions. The girl frowns slightly. “I don’t
remember. My parents took me to a church service. Then I was taken away.” She explains,
looking around fearfully. “Who took you?” Steven asks her, feeling concerned. “A man called
Mr. Vexon. He said I was special. That I had the talent for something special. He didn’t tell me
what it was, and then…” She trails off and lowers her gaze. “And then what?” Miles probes. The
girl looks back up at them defiantly. “Then I woke up here.” She says bitterly. Miles’ heart drops.
Steven notices how upset the girl appears and steps forward slightly. “Hey, hey, it’s alright.
You’re safe now. Nobody will hurt you.” He assures her, placing a gentle hand on her shoulder.
She looks up at him and frowns, shaking her head slightly. “It’s true. You don’t need to worry
anymore. Nobody’s ever going to hurt you again.” He promises her warmly, looking her in the
eyes. She looks back into his green eyes, mesmerized by his beauty. It’s clear that he really
means it. She smiles gently, tears welling up in her eyes. “Thank you.” She murmurs, her voice
soft and quiet, almost inaudible. Miles smiles gently at her before stepping closer to her. “Would
you like to come with us?” He offers. Belial nods quickly. “Yes please!” She smiles, smiling so
brightly. Miles grins widely. “Well, we better start heading back to base now before trouble
arrives. Come on.” He smiles softly at her, reaching out his arm once more and helping her stand.
As Miles, Steven and Belial make their way through the forest, Miles keeps glancing over at
Belial worriedly. Ever since their encounter earlier, everything feels strange. She hasn’t done
anything remotely demonic or abnormal yet, but he can sense that she is hiding something.
Something important. “How much farther?” Belial asks tiredly. “I don’t know.” Miles admits.
“Are you cold? You’ve lost a lot of blood.” Steven observes. “No, I’m fine. Can we keep going
please?” She begs. “Of course.” Miles answers. Belial walks close to Miles, grabbing his hand.
“Thank you. For helping me.” She whispers. “Don’t mention it.” He responds happily. She

smiles at him and holds onto his hand tightly. They continue walking along side each other while
Steven watches them curiously. When they reach a small clearing, Steven looks up at the sky and
sighs softly. “We should probably rest soon. This is starting to wear me out.” He says. “Yeah.”
Miles agrees reluctantly. “Alright.” Belial says. “Why don’t we all sit down, yeah?” Miles
suggests. Steven and Belial nod and sit down on a nearby log, Belial resting her head in Miles’
lap. Miles chuckles a little at her, running a hand through her hair. After a while he begins to fall
asleep, leaning against the tree behind him. Steven glances up at the trees above him and raises
an eyebrow. “What’s that” He asks pointing upwards. “Hm?” Miles hums sleepily, lifting his
head up. He follows Steven’s finger upwards and looks up as well. He frowns. “Do you think I
have night vision?” He asks sarcastically, trying to peer through the darkness to see. Steven
shrugs. “Could be.” He responds. Miles rolls his eyes at Steven’s response. “If I do, then I’ll let
you know.” He grumbles, laying his head back down. His eyelids grow heavier and heavier.
Soon enough he falls asleep.
Miles is woken up by Belial’s scream. Startled awake, Miles lifts his head and looks
around wildly, panic evident in his eyes. “Help, he’s attacking me, help!” She shrieks desperately.
Miles’ eyes widen and he scrambles towards her. “I’m here! Don’t panic okay, Just stay calm!”
He tells her in an attempt to calm her. She grabs his arms and looks into his eyes. “You’re here,
I’m safe. Steven he…just started attacking me.” She says, gripping onto him tightly. Miles
frowns and hugs her tightly, holding her close to him. She wraps her arms around him as well,
nuzzling into his neck. Miles feels her hands run up and down his back. She rests her head on his
shoulder and closes her eyes tightly, trying her best not to cry. He looks at a dumbfounded
Steven who is frozen in shock. He does not understand why Belial was screaming and why she is
saying he attacked her. “Miles wait, she is lying.” Steven says, finally speaking up. Miles looks

at him angrily. “Why would she lie?” He asks. “She wouldn’t scream unless she was afraid of
something. Right Belial?” Miles asks calmly. Belial shakes her head and buries herself deeper
into Miles’ chest, still clinging to him. He pulls her face away from him gently. “Belial, please
speak to me.” He says softly. She blinks and turns her gaze toward him, her eyes wide and
terrified. “You will protect me from him, right?” She whimpers. Miles nods solemnly and wipes
the tear from her eye softly. “Don’t worry, no one is going to hurt you anymore.” He reassures
her. “Miles what are you talking about. She’s lying, I didn’t touch her.” Steven protests, still
standing there, in shock. Miles looks at Steven coldly. “Miles she’s probably a demon, demons
lie. Get away from her now!” He shouts. “Kill him.” Belial whispers to Miles. Miles hesitates
briefly before nodding and standing up. He charges towards Steven, aiming a punch straight for
him. Steven dodges out of the way. “I will kill you!” He yells at him angrily. “Just listen, I don’t
know what’s happening right now, but I’m sure she’s possessing you somehow! So get away
from her and stop being stupid!” Steven shouts back at him. Miles glares at Steven before
charging again, punching him. Steven ducks out of the way, avoiding Miles’ attacks easily. “I do
not want to fight you Miles!” He yells. Miles swings his fist at Steven again, catching him on the
cheek. Steven stumbles backwards slightly before charging back at Miles. “Stop! Please!” Steven
pleads, dodging another blow. Miles punches him one last time, knocking Steven down. Steven
gasps before rolling on the ground. Miles stands over him menacingly. He begins to change, he’s
now black eyes staring at Steven threateningly, breathing heavily. As he is about to deal one last
blow to Steven, which would surely kill him, Miles stops short, confused, as his body suddenly
grows numb, his arm goes limp, his fist clenches at his sides, and he loses his balance, falling
backward on his butt. Steven gets up quickly and races over to where Miles lay on the ground.
Miles tries to push himself up, attempting to regain his lucidity, but he cannot move. “What’s

wrong with me? What’s happening?!” He cries desperately, panicked. “Miles are you Ok
buddy?” Steven asks worriedly. “I can’t move! How did this happen?” Miles exclaims frantically.
“Calm down, relax!” Steven assures him. “That demon bitch over there, she did this to you. It
must be some kind of demon possession.” He adds. Belial drops her innocent girl act as she
stares up at Miles and Steven. “Oh, damn…” She mumbles sadly. “You resisted me, interesting.”
Belial smirks as she approaches the duo slowly. Steven steps in front of Miles protectively,
glaring daggers at Belial. “Stay away from us.” He warns. “Why should I?” Belial asks
mockingly. “Because I won’t hesitate to rip your heart out.” He replies sharply. Belial scoffs,
shaking her head slightly. “So dramatic.” She snickers, taking another step forward. She laughs,
her red eyes shining maliciously. Steven glares at her. “Don’t even bother thinking about hurting
us.” He threatens darkly. “I wouldn’t dream of doing such a thing. You will rip each other apart
as I stand here and watch.” She smiles innocently. “Or…” She continues ominously. “Or what?”
Steven questions suspiciously. “Or one of you can sacrifice himself to save the other, either way
I require an offering.” She explains. “A sacrifice?” Miles questions, looking at Belial curiously.
“Yes. A mortal sacrifice.” Belial states. “I can assure you neither of us wants to die.” Steven
remarks, still holding Miles close protectively. “I thought so.” Belial murmurs. “Fine I will…”
As she is speaking, she is interrupted by a familiar scent. “Surgat?” She asks herself, raising an
eyebrow in confusion. She looks up at Steven questioningly. “Who are you?!” She demands. “I
am Steven. Why does it matter?” He answers simply. She laughs, “Well, well, it looks like I have
stumbled upon a little Surgat demon. This changes things.” She smirks. Steven glares at her
furiously. “What does that mean?” He asks. “I need you to open a locked door for me Steven.”
She smiles evilly. Steven’s eyes widen in surprise. “A locked door?” He repeats. “Yes, that’s
right. Can you do that for me?” She asks. Steven’s expression immediately hardens. “No.” Belial

scowls. “You refuse my offer?” She asks incredulously. Steven glares at her defiantly, refusing
to answer. “Oh, Steven you’re so cute when you’re angry. But you will do this for me.” She
giggles. “What door?” Miles asks weakly, struggling to hold in his coughs. She sighs, turning her
attention to Miles once more. “The door to hell. There is something I desperately want to retrieve
from there.” She says bluntly. Miles chokes on air and nearly collapses, clutching his chest.
“Hell!?” Steven screeches. “Even if he could do that, why would he help you?” Miles asks,
trying to sound calm, though it comes off rather pathetic sounding. “Because I said so.” Belial
answers, smiling deviously. “Shut the hell up” Steven snaps, getting increasingly irritated. Belial
chuckles and crosses her arms over her chest. “As you wish Steven.” She retorts. “One last thing,
I can tell that your demon has been suppressed. For this to work I am going to need you at full
strength. I am going to let you live for now but, I will return for you Steven. You can count on
that.” She sneers. “Now go! Before I change my mind.” She says harshly. Steven glares at her
before walking away, pulling Miles along with him.
Once they’re far enough away, Miles lets out a huge gasp and falls to his knees. “Are you
ok!?” Steven asks worriedly, crouching next to him. Miles just nods, wiping the sweat forming
on his forehead. “Breathe Miles. Breathe. You can do this.” Steven encourages quietly. “Ok. I
think I can walk now. Let’s go. We should hurry.” Miles says. They walk briskly forward and
away from Belial. Once they’ve left her sight, Miles looks at Steven and notices that he is
bleeding from a wound on his upper lip. “Hey. Are you ok? Did she do that to you?” He asks
cautiously. Steven looks at him and frowns. “I’m fine. Just a scratch.” He says. “You’re bleeding
though…” Miles points out. “Let’s focus on getting home.” Steven mutters, wiping his bloody
lip. They continue their journey for quite some time, until they finally reach Defiance. Miles and
Steven walk inside and sit down on the couch while waiting for Atticus to show up. After a few

minutes, Atticus shows up, carrying a bottle of wine, a bag of chips, two bowls of fruit and
various other foodstuffs. “Here you go boys.” He smiles brightly. “Thanks.” Steven says
gratefully. He takes a deep breath and leans back against the sofa. Miles watches as he takes a sip
of wine and sighs deeply. “How are you feeling?” Miles asks worriedly. Steven glances up at
Miles and shrugs. “Pissed we failed our first solo mission, and we lost to a little girl no less.” He
complains. “Yeah...” Miles agrees sadly. “But we did learn something didn’t we?” He adds
hopefully. Atticus sits down beside them, “Now, tell me how you failed so spectacularly.”

chapters.title: Jonathan Reed
chapters.text:
When you begin a journey of revenge, start by digging two graves: one for your enemy,
and one for yourself. This could not be truer in Jonathan’s case. His pursuit for Damien was
about to lead him down a dark path. In the end he would wish he was dead. And perhaps he
would deserve it. Jonathan stood by the edge of town, staring into the darkness where a small
light flickered. It was night time and it had been almost three days of Jonathan tracking the
suspect in the Rosebud killings hoping he would lead him to Damien. Unfortunately for Jonathan,
the killer was determined and elusive. Jonathan continued to stare intently from the dark
alleyway where he was positioned, waiting patiently for the killer to reveal himself to him. “This
is pointless. It is getting late and I am tired. My target is nowhere near here, nor will he ever be!”
He growls angrily to himself. Jon sighs loudly as he starts to walk back towards town, deciding
to stop for the night. He turns around to look behind him when he hears a rustling in the alley. He
stops dead in his tracks, glancing around warily. Suddenly, a lone man darts towards Jonathan,
tackling him to the ground. Jonathan struggles frantically to get loose, succeeding when he
manages to knock the man’s head on the ground. The man groans softly and rolls off Jonathan.
Jonathan stands up quickly, glaring at his attacker, who raises a hand to his head to examine the
damage. Jonathan rushes towards him and punches him hard on the side of his face. He reaches
for his dagger that he kept strapped to his leg, ready to use it if necessary, and slowly approaches
the attacker. “Why did you come at me like that who are you?” Jonathan asks, glaring
menacingly at his attacker. He is met with no reply. Instead, the man pulls his own knife out
from his jacket sleeve, unsheathing the blade, and lunges towards Jonathan. Jonathan dodges out

of the way, grabbing his attacker by the neck and holding him back as his attacker attempts to
slash at his face. Jonathan twists the man’s arm backwards and shoves him onto the ground,
causing him to lose his grip on his knife. The man tries to grab Jonathan again but fails as
Jonathan grabs him by the neck once more and throws him into a nearby wall. The man groans
as he slides to the ground. Jonathan takes his chance to pin the man down. The man gasps
sharply in pain before lying still. “Answer me damnit, who the hell are you?” Jonathan snarls.
The man stays silent, panting heavily. “Who are you!?” Jonathan demands impatiently. “Damien
Videl… he told me to kill you…” The man mumbles, barely audible. Jonathan glares at him
furiously, lifting him up. “You have ten seconds to explain yourself before I snap your fucking
neck and leave you laying here.” He growls. “Damien…he knows you have been following his
man. He doesn’t like cops like you messing in his business.” The man continues. “And what do
you know about Damien?! Where is he?” Jonathan screams, shaking the man slightly. The man
winces slightly from the movement but remains defiant. “I’ll never talk, he’d kill me for less.”
He rasps. Jonathan throws the man against the wall, knocking him out cold. He kneels beside the
man, ruffling through his pockets for any clues. Eventually he finds a phone with Damien’s
number in it. He calls the number and holds the phone to his ear, waiting for someone to pick up.
After a while of ringing, a male voice picks up. “Is it done?” The voice asks. Jonathan hangs up
without answering and stares at the man’s unconscious body, before walking away with the
phone in hand. He gets back into his van, opens the door and places the phone on the console
before starting the car and backing out into the street. As he drives, he begins to plan his next
move. He would find Damien, he decides, no matter the cost. If it meant he had to kill him, then
so be it.

Jonathan arrives home and walks into his apartment, immediately going straight to the
bathroom to clean up the cut on his face. “Jon?” He hears a woman’s voice, followed by
footsteps approaching the bathroom. He closes the door and leans back against it, trying to hide
his wounds. “Where were you? I’ve been waiting here for hours!” She exclaims, opening the
door slightly. Jonathan gives her an innocent smile and gestures to his cuts “I got a bit hurt
fighting some guy in an alley.” He explains. “A guy!? What guy?! You tailed someone without
me?” She scolds him, worry evident in her tone. “The trail was hot; I couldn’t let him slip away
again.” He says smoothly. “Well, don’t do that again. Your father would kill me if I let you die
hunting demons alone.” She replies sternly. “Alright, Avery” Jonathan agrees. “We’re leaving in
thirty minutes. There is something important I need to show you.” She tells him. He nods “Yes
ma’am.” He replies politely before she leaves the room. He proceeds to clean his wounds and
heads to the shower. When he finishes his shower, he dresses comfortably and heads downstairs
to join Dr Avery. He walks into the garage where Avery is sitting on a motorcycle in front of his
black Honda van. “Are you ready to go?” She asks him cheerfully. “We’re taking your
motorcycle?” Jonathan asks worriedly. Avery smiles at him sweetly. “Of course, why not?” She
says excitedly. They make their way outside and drive off, Avery talking non-stop throughout
most of the trip. Eventually they arrive at their destination. “Avery, what is this what are we
doing here?” Jonathan exclaims as they park by a familiar building. Avery just smirks and leads
him inside the building. “You been here before, right?” Avery asks. Jonathan nods reluctantly.
“Yes, I was here for a case. The South east grotto holds their meetings here. But I’m sure you
knew that already.” He says smugly. Avery sighs. “You really have no sense of caution, do you?
Joining a cult, your father would be beside himself.” Jonathan shakes his head and admits
sheepishly “Dangerous I know, but my father knew I was working a case and why do you talk

about him like he is still alive.” Avery simply chuckles and leads him inside the hall filled with
tables and chairs. At one of the tables sat a man wearing a red hoodie and black pants. Avery
walks over and whispers something into the man’s ear. He looks at Jonathan and grins. “Oh! So
you must be Detective Reed. A pleasure to finally meet you.” He says in a gruff voice. Jonathan
bows slightly. “Likewise.” Jonathan greets him back. The man turns to Avery and smiles
brightly. “Thanks for bringing him. I think we can finally have our meet up now.” He says
jokingly. “I hope that’s true because we have all night if that’s what you want.” Avery responds.
The man laughs and claps his hands together. “Now we can talk business. Detective Reed, what
I’m about to tell you can in no way incriminate me understand? I am going to need your
guarantee that I will not be prosecuted for what I’m about to admit to you.” The man requests.
Jonathan pauses for a moment, unsure of whether or not to give him such a promise. Finally,
after much thought, he decides that it would be best to keep his word, so he agrees. The man
nods in satisfaction and turns to Avery. “Thank you dear, now I believe you owe me a drink?”
He asks her playfully, winking at her suggestively. Avery giggles lightly. “Not quite yet, but if
you insist, I’ll buy us each another round once you tell us what you have to say.” She says,
motioning to Jonathan who simply nods in agreement. “Ok so, I have been part of this little
Satanist group, pretty harmless. Each time we meet we just perform some silly mystical ritual
contacting demons to ask for favours or whatever. But lately things have been getting too hard-
core for me you know.” He says, leaning back in his chair and looking away from the detective
and Avery, clearly uncomfortable. Jonathan sits in silence, listening intently. “But I found
something strange recently. I’ve heard rumours that these demonic creatures are attacking people
randomly, and that demons have somehow managed to gain the ability to possess humans.” The
man pauses, “that’s impossible, right?” Jonathan stays silent. He feels bad for the man.

“Anyway… One time I was curious. I wanted to see for myself if this whole thing was real. I go
to this uber secret location with some friends of mine to check it out. And then they bring out
three little girls by the alter and they just straight up murdered them. Slit their throats right in-
front of us as a sacrifice to their lord or whatever. I didn’t think any of this was real, I just went
to these things to get drunk with my buddies and I thought hey, it’s technically a cult maybe
they’d be orgies at some point. But this, this is truly evil and I want no part of it.” He concludes.
Jonathan stays quiet, still processing what he has heard. “Are there any more details you would
like to share?” Avery asks calmly. The man shakes his head. “What, that’s not enough for you!?”
He exclaims, annoyed. Avery sighs softly before giving him a look. “No of course it is, we’ll
take it from here. Thank you for coming to us.” Avery assures him. “Jonathan, you have to bring
him in now, I’ll stay here and investigate further.” She adds. Jonathan nods quickly. “Right. Yes.
Um…” He stutters awkwardly. “Be careful alright? Don’t go off alone again and keep him safe.”
Avery begs. “I won’t. Don’t worry about me, worry about yourself. I won’t let anything happen
to him.” He assures her. She smiles at him gratefully. “Thank you.” She says sincerely. He nods
again and quickly exits the building with the man.
After placing the man in protective custody, Jonathan made his way to the real South east
grotto. They usually hang out at a bar nearby. When he arrives, he pulls his gun out, checking to
make sure there was a clip left, before slowly making his way towards the entrance. As he
approaches the entrance, he hears a voice. “Jonathan! Where have you been man!” The voice
yells. Jonathan sighs, stopping when he sees Ambrose standing in front of the door. “Ambrose,
where the hell is Carlos?” He asks irritably. “Yeah, I saw him leave with a few other guys.”
Ambrose tells him. “He left?” Jonathan questions. Ambrose nods slowly, “Yeah. You know him,
he never stays put.” He continues. Jonathan frowns slightly, before speaking. “What are you

guys into? Why I’m I hearing you’re murdering children now.” Jonathan asks. He is furious.
Ambrose snorts, “We’re not killing kids, man. Those kids were just some unlucky ones.” He
replies sarcastically. Jonathan raises an eyebrow, not believing what he is hearing. Ambrose
takes notice of this and tries to explain himself. “Come on Jon, you know I’m not gonna lie to
you. I thought you knew what you were getting into when you joined us.” Jonathan then puts his
gun to Ambrose’s temple. “Do you even care for these people, Ambrose?” He asks furiously.
Ambrose stares at him with his usual arrogant smirk before answering. “Why should I? He’s
back Jon, and things are about to change.” Jonathan let’s go of Ambrose and shakes his head in
disbelief. Avery had not yet told Jonathan about Lucifer so he had no clue who Ambrose was
talking about. At this moment he was too consumed with anger to process anything else.
Jonathan storms off angrily and finds his car and drives home. Once he arrives, he parks the car
and throws it into park. He sits there for several moments, lost in thought. He gets out of his car
and begins walking towards the apartment, his mind occupied with thoughts of how much he
hates the grotto right now. He enters his apartment and slams the door shut loudly, startling Alice,
his cat. Later that evening as he is resting, Jonathan hears people attempting to enter his
apartment through the window. He rushes to the window and sees two young women trying to
force it open, knocking down a few plants along the way. The two women were Jennifer and
Ruby, two other members of the South east grotto. Jonathan fully opens the window and lets
them in. “Jennifer, Ruby what are you doing here?” He asks exasperatedly. “You saw Ambrose
today, right?” Ruby asks anxiously. Jennifer and Ruby were clearly afraid and Jonathan could
tell. They were both new members of this Satanist cult and they had not yet realised what that
truly meant. “Yeah...” Jonathan answers. “They killed those kids, Jon.” Jennifer blurts out, tears
threatening to spill from her eyes. “We have to do something right?” She finishes courageously.

Jonathan nods slightly, “Of course we do. We have to find them first.” He says. “Carlos told me
there is a meeting with the big boss, something big is going down.” Ruby informs him.
Jonathan’s expression darkens immediately. “Do you mean Damien Videl?” He growls. The two
nod. “So what exactly is the plan? How do we find them and stop this from happening again?”
Ruby asks Jonathan. “Do you know where this meeting is supposed to be?” He asks. “No, Carlos
does not tell us much. We are as new to this as you are Jon. I mean I only got into it for the
magic, not killing bloody children.” Jennifer admits. Jon rolls his eyes at her response. “Okay,”
he pauses for a second as he thinks. “The phone, yes!” He shouts suddenly. He walks over to the
desk and retrieves a small red phone from the drawer, handing it to Jennifer. “This is a phone I
got from one of Damien’s men. If we could trace where the last call it made came from, I bet
Damian will be there.” He explains. “That makes sense.” Jennifer agrees nodding slowly. Ruby
becomes suspicious of Jon. “Wait, what do you know about tracing phones Jon? And why do
you have that?” She asks. Jonathan shrugs indifferently. “Don’t worry about it.” He responds. A
look of realisation crosses Ruby’s face. “Oh shit! He’s a fucking cop Jennifer, I knew it, I
fucking knew it.” She says angrily. “Yeah, and you’re a Satanist. Let’s not worry about what we
are and just do this.” Jonathan says firmly. Ruby huffs loudly but decides to hold her tongue. “So,
what do you need us to do?” Jennifer asks curiously. “Jennifer! We’re not going to trust him now,
are we? He lied to us.” Ruby snaps defensively. “Ruby! Shut up.” Jennifer growls. Jonathan
looks between them nervously for a moment, unsure of where to start. “Well…. First of all, you
guys don’t need to come. This is actually personal for me; I have to do this. Plus, I don’t think
you guys are experienced enough for it either.” He says. Jennifer glares at him briefly before
uttering a strange phrase, “Ignis.” And with a wave of her hand, the magazine on his coffee table
burst into flames. Jonathan jumps back, startled. Quickly putting out the flame with a slight look

of annoyance on his face. “Great, you can do demon magic too.” He says sarcastically. “Well,
it’s not hell fire like Damien’s but it will still do the trick.” Jennifer states proudly. “Ruby, how
about you? Do you have any magic up your sleeves?” Jonathan asks seriously. Ruby scoffs. “Yes,
and so should you. That’s why we gave you the grimoire, to practice magic. What have you been
using it for?” She asks accusingly. Jonathan hesitates, he knows this is going to cause trouble,
but he also knows if he does not give them a chance to help, they might do something disruptive.
“Look…I haven’t used it very much lately. So you see, I may or may not be a little rusty at this
whole witchcraft thing.” He admits reluctantly. Jennifer and Ruby glare at him but decide not to
push it, since they probably know he’s lying anyway. “Fine. Whatever.” Ruby huffs out.
Jonathan sighs lightly and rubs his hands together, deciding it would be easier to start with some
basic outline of his plan. “Right, I will do the trace while you two stay as far away from the
precinct as possible. Then we will meet up back at the bar and iron out the plan. Which is simple,
Ruby, Jennifer, you burn whomever gets in the way and I will shoot Damien in the face, arrest
everyone else and receive a medal or something. Got it?” He asks sternly. They nod. “Great.
Now get out of my place, before I arrest you for trespassing.”
Avery came back to Defiance headquarters feeling disappointed. She did not find
anything substantial while investigating the meeting place of the fake South East grotto. All she
found was a bunch of sycophants who had no clue what they had gotten themselves into. She
sighed heavily and headed to her office to finish her work. Her cell rang. She pulls it out of her
pocket. “Hello?” She said. She looked at the screen to see the caller ID. “Hey mom,” she
answered softly. “How’s your case coming along?” Avery’s mom asks her excitedly. “Um, good
mom, I’m almost done actually.” Avery replied. “Really? Great! Listen honey, we got a
problem.” Her mother says, sounding serious. “What’s wrong? Is everything ok?” Avery asked

concerned. “There’s this thing at church…I won’t be able to have dinner with you tonight.” She
explained. “Mom. That’s fine, but I can pick up some extra groceries while I am out.” Avery
replied apologetically. Her mother hummed quietly, “It’s okay honey. Just try not to be late
okay?” She requested before hanging up the phone. Avery was pleased that her mom cancelled
their dinner plans. Avery’s mother does not know the true nature of her work, and Avery was
growing tired of having to lie to her all the time. She needed some sort of break from her, a
chance to relax, and she thought tonight may be the perfect opportunity to take it. She leaves her
office and heads to the operations centre in search of information. She finds Amala sitting by the
monitors eating a bowl of noodles, her headsets in and lost in her own little world. Avery taps
her shoulder gently, breaking her from her trance. “Dr Melblac, what are you doing in here?”
Amala asks surprised. Avery shrugs, “I’ve finished with my day and wanted to see how things
were going.” She replies. Amala frowns momentarily as she watches Avery sit down next to her.
She turns to look directly at Avery. “Are you feeling alright, Avery? You seem distracted today.”
Amala asks worriedly. Avery gives her an unreadable smile. “I’m fine.” She replies. “Ok, well,
let’s get down to business then.” Amala continues, turning back to the screens. She grabs a USB
stick from the tray and places it in the slot on her laptop. “what’s our progress so far?” Avery
asks as Amala scrolls down her browser. “Well, as you can see, I still haven’t located Sarah.
She’s proving to be a bit to elusive even for me but I do however have something very
interesting.” Amala says triumphantly. “What is it?” Avery asks intrigued. “You told me to keep
an eye on Jonathan. Earlier tonight he got a visit from to women at his apartment. After that he
immediately went to the precinct and conducted a trace on a number. I peeked inside their
database and the number leads right here.” Amala explains, showing Avery the screen. “Why are

you showing me this?” Avery questions. “Because I’m pretty sure that is where Damien Videl
is.” She says confidently. Avery gasps slightly. “Damien?!”

chapters.title: Avery Melblac
chapters.text:
Long ago, a celestial was cast out of Heaven. As the celestial was falling, pieces of him
broke apart. Eventually, these fragments formed the first demons. Belial is one of the most
powerful of these demons. She currently sat on a high ledge, watching the city below her. She
did not particularly care what happened around her, just that the humans stayed ignorant of her
existence. Only a handful of humans knew of her existence. Those few being mostly members of
the Devil’s den, a cult her and three other devils founded. Many who came across her did not live
long enough to talk of their encounters with her. Damien walked in the room to discover Belial
sat atop the ledge looking down at the city below her. He stands there and simply watches her as
she observes the city. Finally, Belial notices him standing there. “Damien. I assume you are
wondering why I am here?” She asks calmly. “Lord Belial, I didn’t know you were back.”
Damien replies with a smirk. “I have been gone for some time, but I am back. There has been a
change of plans with operation perdition.” Belial informs him. “Oh? How so?” He asks curiously.
“I’ve found Surgat. I assumed they were all voided. I was wrong.” She replies, a smug grin
forming on her face. Damian smirks as well. “Good job. You should be proud, I’m sure this
means that you will soon make your return to hell.” He says. Belial laughs mockingly. “Not yet.
But I am working on it.” She says coldly. “But enough about me, how’s your search for Lucifer
going?” Belial demands. “The moment Alexandra informed me that he was indeed not destroyed,
I began a campaign of sacrifices. My agents have been conducting sacrificial ritual magic across
the city, but nothing yet. We still have not been able to locate Lucifer’s spirit.” Damien sighs.
Belial hums in response, thinking for a second. “Do you remember when we first met in the

woods? It seems like only yesterday…” Belial mused, reminiscing about the past. “Oh yes, you
were quite impressive, I must say. Your power rivalled mine.” Damien replies. “Of course, I
would never forget such glorious times.” She smiles wickedly. “I told you I would reveal the
truth in due time, that I alone would lead you to enlightenment.” She says, a dark aura
surrounding her. “I feel it is time I tell you about mine and Lucifer’s origins.” Belial begins to
explain. But before she can continue, they hear a loud crash from somewhere outside. Both turn
to look outside. They spot a large vehicle crashing through the building. The vehicle crashed into
the side of the building with a sickening thud. Damien and Belial exchange nervous looks.
Belial’s head snaps downwards suddenly. “That sounds exactly like the sound of an explosion!”
She exclaims, annoyance etched on her face. “Lord Belial, you have to leave now!” Damien
shouts urgently. Outside the building, Dr Avery Melblac and Jonathan Reed have mounted an
attack against Damien.
Six hours ago, Avery stood beside Amala who had just discovered Damien’s location.
Avery has been waiting for this moment, to finally avenge the death of her dear friend and
mentor Dr Andrew Reed. “Where is Jonathan now?” She asks impatiently. Amala quickly does
something on her computer, checking for a location by tracking Jonathan’s phone. After a minute
or two she looks up with a satisfied grin plastered on her face. “He is here.” She says proudly,
signalling at the bar on the map. Avery nods and takes a deep breath as if she were preparing
herself for the fight to come. “Alright! I’m ready. What’s the plan?” Amala asks eagerly as her
hands balled into fists, her nails digging into her palms. Avery chuckles at her enthusiasm and
tells her, “We have to do this right, keep working and find out as much as you can about this
place where Damien is. I’ll go see Jonathan and we’ll take it from there Ok.” Avery instructs.
“Sounds good to me.” Amala responds. With another nod towards each other, the two split up to

begin their tasks. Avery first heads to her apartment to get some things. She puts on her black
leather jacket and a red scarf before heading out the door. The streets are busy. A police officer
passes by as she pulls a motorcycle helmet onto her head. When she arrived at her apartment, she
finds her mother busy in the kitchen cleaning dishes. “Hi Mom.” Avery greets, grabbing an apple
off the counter and walking upstairs to her room. “Did you have a nice day at work sweetie?”
Rachel asks, stopping her daughter. “Yeah, it was alright. Can we please stop talking about
work?” Avery pleads, setting down her things on the kitchen counter. Rachel sighs, putting a
dirty plate in the sink, and walks over to Avery, hugging her tightly. “I missed you sweetheart.
You work too hard and you have not been to church with me in a while.” She states. Avery
shrugs nonchalantly. “You know I’m not into that stuff mom.” She answers, pulling away from
her mother’s hug. Rachel frowns and starts fussing over her daughter. “God loves all of us dear.
Even if you don’t believe in him.” Rachel assures her daughter. She sits down on the couch, pats
the cushion next to her and motions for Avery to join her. Avery reluctantly joins her on the
couch. She leans against her mother’s side, enjoying the warmth of her embrace. “You help
people heal for a living, yet you do not believe in God. Tell me why.” Rachel asks gently. Avery
sighs heavily. “Mom, it is because of the things I see at my work that I don’t believe God gives a
damn about us. There is so much darkness in the world, where is he? Where is God at a time we
need him the most? I don’t doubt that there is a God out there, I just do not believe he cares
about any of us.” Her voice trails off as she stares down at the ground. Rachel looks at her, a sad
expression on her face. She reaches her hand up and caresses Avery’s cheek with her thumb.
“Sweetheart…I think you may be overthinking things.” She tries to convince her daughter.
Avery scoffs. “Mom, I can’t stay. I have something important I have to take care of.” She insists.
Rachel nods sadly. “Okay, you do whatever you need to do. Just please, call once in a while.”

She requests. Avery nods, giving her mother a quick kiss goodbye and getting up off the couch.
She quickly runs up to her room, grabbing what she needed. She says goodbye to her mother
again vowing that she will call her later. With that she heads out the front door to her motorcycle
which was parked just outside. She gets on the bike and revs the engine speeding off towards the
bar where Jonathan was. Once she arrives there, she parks the bike outside the bar and enters.
Jonathan was sitting by the corner with two women, none of whom she recognized. One was
dressed in a white dress, her blonde hair flowing down her shoulders, and her eyes blue like
water. The other female was wearing simple blue jeans and a black t-shirt, her black hair pulled
neatly into a bun. Both women turned as they saw Avery walk towards them. “Avery! So glad
you could make it.” Jonathan said awkwardly. “Hey Jonathan.” Avery replied quietly, not
wanting to attract attention to herself. “Where you planning on going after Damien without me?”
Avery questioned. “I...uh…well you see…” Jonathan stammered nervously. Avery stared at him
with an unimpressed expression. “Relax, I’m here to help.” She assured him, sitting down next to
him. “Well, uh,” Jonathan began “This is Ruby and Jennifer, they are sort of like, my friends.
They are helping too.” Avery looked at Jennifer who was smiling shyly and Ruby who seemed
indifferent. “Nice to meet you.” Avery replied politely. “So, are you going to do what you
normally do, just go in all gung ho without an actual plan?” Avery asked Jonathan. “Um, yeah I
guess.” He admits. Avery rolled her eyes slightly. “Jonathan, you are being an idiot.” She stated.
“What are you suggesting then?” Jonathan asks. They talked a bit more. A few hours later they
were at Damien’s door, ready for a battle.
“Are you sure he’s inside?” Ruby questioned. “I’m sure.” Jonathan responds. “He’s
definitely in there!” Avery adds. “Is everyone ready?” Jonathan asks. “As ready as we’ll ever
be.” Jennifer responds. Jonathan nodded determinedly. “Good luck everybody!” He yells, before

rushing towards the entrance. Avery, Jennifer and Ruby followed after him. Before he could
even enter, however, they heard a loud roar. Looking back, they saw a gigantic black dog
charging towards them, its mouth dripping blood. “Damn!” Jonathan hissed. “Don’t worry I got
this.” Ruby asserted. “Ok, but try not to die!” Jennifer exclaims. Ruby nodded confidently then
took off running towards the dog with inhuman speed, swinging at it with her bare hands. Avery
and the rest made their way inside the building. After entering the building, they walked through
a hallway, eventually finding themselves in a room filled with hundreds of shelves stacked with
alcohol. On one of the chairs sat a woman drinking. As soon as she spotted Avery and the rest,
she immediately put her drink down. “Dr Melblac, I wasn’t expecting you!” She exclaimed
enthusiastically. “You know me?” Avery asks, smiling smugly. The smile fades when she
notices a gun pointed straight at her head. Alexandra Turner had a man with her who pointed the
gun at the intruders. She was unbothered. “You cannot possibly expect to win here.” Alex says to
Avery, Jonathan and Jennifer. “Actually, we do.” Jennifer replies, grinning. Suddenly the lights
shut off. Jonathan fires a round into the man who was pointing the gun at Avery. Alexandra
looks around wildly until she sees a flash of a blade cutting through the darkness. The flash of
the blade cuts through the air, inches above her head. Her eyes widen and she grabs the wound
that was caused by the blade that has stabbed her shoulder. Blood oozes out of her wound. The
person who swung at her was Avery. She was using a special blade which had ancient runes
etched into it. “What is this?” Alexandra asked with surprise and fear. “I realise that demons
can’t be killed by run-of-the-mill cutlery. At the very least, you’d need an ancient demon-killing
blade! Like the one that just made you bleed.” Avery smirked, twirling the blade around in her
hand. Alexandra’s eyes widened. “That’s impossible.” She whispered under her breath. If
Alexandra were to be fatality wounded by this blade, she would lose her demon powers, making

her unable to heal, merely human again. “Da mihi vide, ut videam in nocte. Sic pugnare
possum.” Alexandra muttered under her breath. “There you are!” She called out looking directly
at Avery, Jonathan and Jennifer. The group looked between each other confused. “Jon, go now,
find Damien.” Avery ordered as her grip tightened on her weapon. Jonathan nodded, turning and
bolting out of the room. Before he can get away, Alexandra had him in her grasp. He cried out in
pain, struggling and kicking. “No!” Avery shouts. “Repelle!” Jennifer calls, summoning an
energy which is meant to push an enemy. But this only succeeds in slowing Alexandra down.
“Now! Go!” Jenni6 shouts at Jonathan. Without hesitating Jonathan leaves the room, leaving
Avery and Jennifer alone to deal with Alex. As Jonathan is running, he starts speaking into the
earwig which was in his left ear. “Amala, are they in position!” He asks urgently.
Ten minutes earlier, there’s a huge explosion on the side of the building where Damian
has been located. Avery Melblac and her team set it off. Their goal is to find Damien and take
him out. The secondary, but just as important goal was to locate anyone Damien might be
holding to use in his sacrifices. Miles Kelly and Steven Grant were also present for this. “Miles,
go with Steven. Amala will direct you towards the back entrance where you will not be spotted.”
