Avery instructs. “Amala!?, Everyone make sure your earwig communication devices are
working so Amala can direct you from HQ. Amala, can you hear me?” Avery requests. “Yes, I
am here!” Amala responds. “Good, if anything goes wrong, call for backup immediately. If we
can’t locate Damien, I want you to get every cop here, understood?” Avery informs her.
“Understood!” Amala confirms. They split off. Miles takes Steven Grant with him while Avery
takes Jonathan, Ruby and Jennifer. Miles and Steven move silently towards the location of the
entrance at the far end of the building. It was quiet, except for the noise of the traffic nearby.
Eventually they came to a large metal door. “How far is it?” Miles whispers into his radio.

“You’re almost there.” Amala responds. Steven opens the door slowly, allowing Miles to slip
through first, followed by himself. They sneakily make their way through the building for a few
minutes, all the while going undetected. “Amala where is this damn grid?” Miles asks. “Just
ahead!” She replies. They continue further until they come across an electrical switch to the
whole building. “Ready?” Steven asks. Miles nods, pushing the switch. Immediately, the whole
building goes dark. It was about 00: 06 PM. The building was now practically pitch black.
“Steven, I have night vision now.” Miles quips as he reminds Steven of that night in the woods.
The grimoires which the members of the South East grotto possessed had very powerful
witchcraft phrasing magic within them, one of which was a spell that enabled the user to see
through the dark. “Great. Okay let’s keep moving, the sooner we find Damien the sooner we’ll
leave.” Steven tells Miles. They make their way further into the building, positioning themselves
in different spots. “They’re in position.” Amala says into Jonathan’s ear piece. “Good, make the
call.” Jonathan responds. Amala listens to the instructions, then starts calling Damien’s phone.
“When his phone rings, I’ll be able to track the precise location of the phone. You and the boys
each have with you the widget I gave to you, with that you should be able to follow the signal
straight to Damien, even in this ridiculously large skyscraper.” Amala explains. “Thanks
Amala.” Jonathan responds gratefully. Avery was still contending with Alexandra. She held her
blade up as she tried to attack her again, but Alex was just too powerful, her blade did not phase
her. Avery growled angrily at Alex, throwing a punch, which Alexandra easily dodged. Just as
Avery was about to throw another punch, Alexandra grabbed her wrist with her free hand,
causing Avery to drop the blade. Avery screamed as she was suddenly thrown against the wall,
the impact knocking her unconscious for a few seconds, “Avery!” Jennifer exclaims, rushing
over to her, “Are you ok?!” She asks worriedly. “Yeah, I’m alright.” Avery assures her.

“Where’s the bitch?” She questions. “Pathetic! How can you Possibly hope to defeat me!?”
Alexandra taunts loudly. “We’re not weak!” Jennifer speaks. Alexandra grows frustrated with
them. “Then prove it!!” She screams. She throws all her demonic energy at them, lifting her
hands up and making both Avery and Jennifer levitate in the air without even touching them.
They start struggling violently against Alex, but she refuses to let go of them. She begins
chanting something in a language none of them can understand. Suddenly, their movements
become much more erratic and harder to control. Avery suddenly starts shaking intensely, a look
of terror plastered onto her face. Just as it appears they were about to perish, Jennifer utters
another phrasing spell, “Modus!” This limits Alexandra's power momentarily. Once it had
dissipated, Avery and Jennifer dropped to the ground, panting heavily. “What the hell was
that?!” Jennifer gasped. “Not sure but it doesn’t feel good…” Avery replied nervously. “Dammit,
I thought you got her!” Jennifer says to Avery. “I did, it takes time to take effect.” Avery
responds, panicked, as she watches Alexandra get up again. “I suppose you’re not weak after
all.” Alexandra remarks sarcastically before sending another blast of demonic energy their way.
However, before she can grab them once more, Alex falls to the floor, weakened. She lets out a
cry of pain and clutches her wound, blood pouring out of it. “Damnit!” Avery curses as she picks
herself up and walks towards Alexandra. “It shouldn’t have taken this long. We almost died.”
She adds. “I cannot believe that worked.” Jennifer comments. “Powerful spells are bound by
something even more powerful. I bound this blade to the three most powerful demis I know. The
demonic energies within Amala, Steven and Miles imbued the blade with the power to take you
down.” Avery says as she is looking down at a now weakened Alex. She turns around to face
Jennifer. “Let’s finish this.” She said sternly. Jennifer nods in agreement. Alexandra stares wide
eyed at them. “Do what you must.” She states as she holds onto her injury. Avery does not

hesitate as she takes her spelled blade and stabs it right into Alexandra's chest, piercing her heart.
Avery pulls the blade out, watching as crimson red blood gushes out of Alexandra’s chest. The
blood continues dripping from the wound. Alexandra gasps for breath. Her eyes are filled with
contempt. She looks like she wants to say something but instead collapses completely onto her
side. Avery’s expression turns cold. She was satisfied that she had finally gotten revenge on one
of the people responsible for Dr Reed’s death, her friend and mentor. “Come on, let’s go find the
other.” She says to Jennifer. Jennifer nods and follows behind her. The two of them head upstairs,
following the same signal to where Damian might be. About five minutes later they reach the
location. “Oh my God…!” Jennifer gasps in horror when she sees Damien’s body lying on the
floor burned to black, Jonathan standing over him.
Elsewhere, Atticus Finch has just learned of Sarah May’s whereabouts from his
subordinate. He sends out some men to search for her, knowing she must be somewhere nearby.
After about an hour they report back saying they have not found her. Finch frowns in worry, but
shakes it off, he needed to focus. This was still good news for him. After days of searching, they
had finally picked up her trail. Six hours have passed since the confrontation between Avery
Melblac and Alexander Turner. The scene was still undisturbed. Damien’s burned corpse still
lied on the top floor of his suite. Alexandra's body remained unmoved with a glaring hole in her
chest and shoulder. The scene is sombre and dark, as if the last rays of sun had fallen into
oblivion. In that moment, something peculiar and significant begins to happen. Alexandra’s body
starts to glow bright pink. Slowly, a light comes to life, spreading throughout her torso. As it
does, the glow intensifies. Then, it begins to spread to her feet. Before long, Alexandra’s entire
body is covered in a brilliant pink colour. She then begins to stand. Her body glows brighter and
brighter until everything else fades away. In the wake of this, she is revealed to be alive.

chapters.title: Atticus Finch
chapters.text:
Atticus is suddenly interrupted by a panicked Avery. “We’ve got a problem!” She
exclaimed. Finch looks at his watch as he sighs deeply. “Tell me.” He instructs. “Jonathan
he…he did something very stupid. I couldn’t protect him, Finch.” She explains frantically.
“What do you mean?” Finch asks. “He went after Damien himself. He fought with Damien and I
wasn’t there to stop it.” Avery rambles. “Wait, was Jonathan killed?” Finch asks, concerned.
“Worse.” Avery replies. The night that Jonathan came face to face with Damien, he had followed
Damien’s phone signal and found him sitting in his study, as if he was waiting just for him.
When Jonathan confronted Damien, it went just how he expected. Damien was too powerful for
him. His hell flame scorched the entire room. Jonathan barely survived. Yet somehow, he did.
“Jonathan made the deal. He’s soul for Damien’s.” An emotionless look replaces Avery’s
usually tough demeanour. “He must have used his grimoire to call upon a Faust demon, plus
those women he was with, I don’t trust them. They let him do this.” She added quietly. “Where is
he now?” Finch asks quickly. Avery hesitates for a moment. “He ran off with them.” She replies
cautiously. A Faustian bargain is a pact whereby a human trade something of spiritual
importance, such as the soul, for whatever he so desires. These kinds of pacts where different
from normal demon possession or summoning because they are not limited by much. Only
limited by the summoner's imagination. They do however, require a great cost. One that not
many are willing to pay. Jonathan has presumably bargained his soul in order to defeat his
father’s murderer. “Can you help him, Finch? Can you undo this?” Avery asks desperately.
Finch is silent, contemplating on what he could possibly do. If it were only possible to remove

the deal, he would do it. A Faustian contract is impossible to break. “No, I cannot.” He answers,
regretfully. “Is there nothing we can do, anything at all?” Avery asks urgently. Finch is silent
once again before speaking. “I am sorry Avery, but I can think of no way to break this pact.” He
apologizes as he gets up from his desk. “That’s not true!” Avery protests, “There must be some
way!” Finch shakes his head in response. “There isn’t.” He admits. “If there were any way, I
would have already done it.” He insists. Avery stands up and clenches her jaw tightly, feeling her
determination waver slightly. She knew he was right; it was impossible. But it does not mean she
was not going to try anyway. “Damn you Jonathan!” Avery growls in frustration before storming
out of the office. Leaving Finch alone. He takes a moment to calm down, allowing his mind to
wander elsewhere. Finally, he decides he should leave as well. He leaves his office, closing the
door silently behind him. On his way to the elevator, he encounters Miles. “Hey Finch.” Miles
greets him. Atticus returns the greeting. “Hello Miles.” He says. He knows Miles is anxious to
ask him about Sarah. “Have you heard anything about her yet? Did you find her?” Miles asks
anxiously. Finch nods his head in response. “I’m afraid we then lost her again, but we are close.”
He says to Miles. “That’s good news, right? At least we have a lead now.” Miles replies
excitedly. “Actually, I am following another lead right now, you could come with me.” Finch
says, offering Miles a smile. Miles looks hesitant for a moment, but accepts Finch’s offer
nonetheless, deciding that this was his best chance to see Sarah again.
After a quick ride, they arrive at the destination Finch has been pursuing. “This is the
place.” Finch comments as he and Miles arrive on the scene at a large church convention centre.
“A church? I don’t think a girl carrying around the literal devil would be at a church. Are you
sure you have the right place?” Miles questions sceptically as he takes in the large building.
Atticus chuckles. “Trust me, we will find her.” He replies. They enter the building through the

main entrance and walk up the steps into the overhead lobby. There are plenty of people milling
around, some talking to the staff members, others reading magazines, there are also quite a few
groups of students, talking to each other. “What a crowd…we’ll never find Sarah if we keep
wandering like this!” Miles complains in annoyance. “Relax Miles. We are going to find her
eventually.” Finch assures his companion. Eventually he spots a tall, handsome, dark-haired man.
He approaches the man who smiles warmly at him as soon as he notices him. “Welcome...
welcome. I am pastor Ellis and I appreciate you coming to join us today. Hallelujah.” He says
enthusiastically. Atticus shakes the man’s hand politely. “No, thank you pastor.” He responds.
Pastor Ellis laughs. “You’re too kind... you’re too kind.” Ellis gives the appearance of a very
happy-go-lucky guy. He has got a good life, a successful empire and is incredibly charming. He
was also the key to finding Sarah. Once they were done exchanging pleasantries, Atticus and
Miles disappear into the crowd and wait for the service to begin. An hour passes and Ellis finally
begins his sermon. “It looks like they’re starting, let’s head over there.” Miles says as they start
to walk toward the nave. They get there they make their way to the front of the group of people
that have gathered, sitting in front of Ellis along the left side. “Ladies and gentlemen, welcome.”
Ellis greets as the congregation turns their attention to him. “My name is Pastor Ellis and I am
pleased to welcome you if you are new here and to all my faithful flock who return every Sunday,
God appreciates your dedication and so do I. Hallelujah!” The congregation cheers. “The Lord is
our strength…” Ellis continues, “…the Lord is our refuge…” The congregation echoes along
with Ellis. “In Him we live, and move on…” Ellis pauses and looks out to the crowd.
“Hallelujah.” He concludes as everyone cheers. “Why are we here Finch?” Miles whispers to
Atticus as they continue to listen to Ellis’ sermon. “Just keep watching.” Finch replies. Miles
scoffs, and decides to just trust him. Ellis continues preaching. “And so the Lord has given us

this day to gather together and celebrate Him. Let us praise Him!” The congregation repeats
Ellis’ words. Atticus and Miles exchange glances. “Hallelujah.” Ellis continues as he raises his
arms in an open gesture. “So let us praise Him! Praise Him for he has given us a gift today; we
have gathered together to celebrate a miracle Jesus Christ our Saviour has showed to me.” Ellis
exclaims ecstatically. The congregation falls silent, anticipating what Ellis had to say. Ellis starts
to pace back and forth, seemingly agitated. Unsure if he should reveal to the people what he had
witnessed. After several seconds, he stops pacing abruptly and shouts. “Hallelujah!” He cries out.
“Hallelujah!” The congregation joins him, raising their hands to the heavens and shouting “To
the Lord.” The music starts playing loudly throughout the convention hall and the congregation
sings with all their might. “Holy, holy, Holy. Blessed are you among the poor. You have
overcome your adversities. Your trials shall not last forever. Salvation, salvation, salvation, is
near at hand! Amen. Amen. Amen. Amen.” The congregation finishes their hymn, and sits
patiently, listening as Ellis continues to preach. “Thank you, dear friends.” Ellis says as the
congregation claps. “Now, about a week ago, a met a young woman. She came to me seeking
salvation, something. All I know is she needed me. She needed the warmth of our Lord Jesus
Christ.” Ellis explains as he places his hand on his chest, looking extremely serious. “Her name
is Sarah. Blessed be her name.” He adds. Miles and Atticus perk up at this revelation and share a
look. They both remain silent, surprised that Ellis knew her name. “She was in terrible condition.
It was as though someone had torn out her soul.” Ellis continues as he gestures towards his chest.
Atticus and Miles exchange another glance. “But, as she was sitting there next to me, crying,
I…I felt something inside of her. A Light I could not explain, that was buried deep underneath
all that hurt. It felt like I had found the one thing I have been searching for my entire life.” Ellis
states passionately as he puts his hands together, clasping them tightly. “She showed me a

miracle I could not explain. I will bring her out so she can show you herself.” He declares
confidently, his voice full of conviction. “Hallelujah.” Everyone in the room cheers with delight,
except Atticus and Miles. Neither are convinced by Ellis’ story. Ellis gestures to his deacon,
instructing him to bring out Sarah May. “Please go ahead and bring her out.” Ellis requests. His
deacon nods affirmatively, and rushes off into the distance. Within a minute, another man
appears in front of the congregation, bringing with him a dark haired, brown eyed woman. As
soon as she is brought to Ellis, he grabs her hand and leads her up to the pulpit. “Sarah, my dear
sister. We are reunited again.” Ellis exclaims happily as the rest of the congregation applauds.
Sarah looks around but does not notice either Miles nor Atticus. The moment he sees her, Miles
gets the impulse to rush over to her. But Atticus grips onto Miles’s arm firmly, stopping him
from doing so. “Not yet!” Atticus says softly as he watches intently at what Sarah is about to do.
Sarah begins to speak, her voice soft and low. “Speak up Sarah. They all, need to hear this.”
Sarah speaks again, this time a little louder than before. “Deliverance. That is what I offer.” She
pauses for a moment as she and looks at the congregation. “I am merely a messenger, and the
message is this.” She continues. “Demons are real. They bring with them many vices to keep you
swimming in the illusion of the darkness. To keep you from your best life and shield you from
your own light. To break you until you want to die. They feed on your fear to survive. I will take
it all inside and I will be your guide. To free you from the dense illusion of the darkness inside
all of us. To free you from your own minds. I will guide you through your dark night of the soul
you can no longer fight!” Sarah continues, her speech filled with passion as she speaks with such
clarity that she almost seems rehearsed. “If you follow me you will rise above the darkness. You
will see yourself as you truly are.” She adds. Atticus watches as she becomes more confident in
herself and in her words. The congregation stands in silence for a few moments as Sarah

continues to speak. She points at a young woman in the crowd, “You.” She says. Ellis instructs
his deacon to bring the young woman onto the stage. She walks forward timidly and comes up on
the dais next to Sarah. Atticus appears to be nervous but he does not want to interrupt before he
figures out what Sarah is up to. “Are you willing?” Sarah asks the girl who then nods reluctantly
in agreement. Sarah holds up her palm, reaching out and grabbing hold of the girl’s forehead.
She then starts whispering a phrase no one else could hear. At that moment, Atticus could make
out what she was whispering. It was the same invocation adapted and refined by Dr Andrew
Reed. The one he used to turn his Deviants. The girl begins to show signs of being exorcised,
surprising both Atticus and Miles. “How is she doing that right now?” Miles whispers as he and
Atticus watch the girl. Atticus then noticed a new tattoo on Sarah’s hand. It was a sacred circle of
Baphomet tattoo on her left dorsal. The circle of Baphomet is an essential element in the
Deviants ritual. The girl begins to scream out in excruciating pain as the circle burns itself into
her skin. Miles and Atticus look at each other once more, concerned. “Miles! Now! We have to
do something!” Atticus yells urgently as he starts running toward the alter. Miles focuses his
power before turning into his usual monstrous state. The congregation screams in terror as they
witness this. They all scatter, running away in all directions. Atticus jumps up on the alter and
faces Sarah. “Sarah? Stop, you’re hurting her. What are you doing?” Atticus demands
desperately. “Turning darkness into life.” She responds. Atticus looks around helplessly, unsure
of how to help the young girl, but he knows there isn’t much time left until it works or Sarah
loses control. “Sarah, stop! You don’t know what you are doing.” He pleads. Miles rushes the
alter as well and attempts to forcibly stop Sarah. “No! Don’t touch her!” Sarah protests as Miles
attempts to push her away from the girl, but Sarah continues to resist. “Stop!” Ellis exclaims
anxiously, fearing for the young girl’s safety. “This girl has to be saved! Do not touch her! She

needs to be purified!” Ellis says angrily. Finally, the girl falls to the ground, having been purified
of the demon that inhabited her body. Atticus takes advantage of a now distracted Sarah,
grabbing her hand to use the circle of Baphomet tattoo against her. With it, he begins reciting an
incantation aloud. “Deduc me in mundum tuum in area inferni..!” Sarah struggles, trying to
escape his grip. The incantation commands the demon who hears it to bring the castor into his
realm. The demon Naberius obeys, and drags Atticus into an ethereal plane. “Atticus Finch...”
the demon hisses menacingly. It attempts to launch itself at Atticus, only stopping when it hits an
invisible barrier in-front of Atticus. “Is this the key of Solomon? It won’t hold for long!”
Naberius warns as it prepares to attack once more. “No, this one is my own special design. Circle
of Solomon with elder runes and Enochian script. It does not break!” Atticus smirks at Naberius
who is now trapped inside this sigil. Naberius growls in frustration. “You cannot defeat me,
Finch! It’s only a matter of time before your soul belongs to us.” It shouts as its eyes glow red.
Atticus grunts slightly as he clenches his teeth, holding back his anger. “Hell must be short on
souls if you’re bothering with a sorry one like mine.” Atticus replies as he closes his eyes for a
brief moment. Suddenly, he feels a strong pull coming from all sides. Naberius appears to be
doing something. “In this place, I am God...!!” Naberius cries in outrage as it attempts to rip the
circle apart. “You shall burn and you shall perish!” Atticus speaks as he sets the circle aflame. At
that moment, a familiar figure appears inside the circle beside Naberius.
“Atticus is that you?” The figure inquires curiously. Atticus opens his eyes to find
Artemis standing before him, looking exactly as she did before. She is the woman he failed to
save all those years ago. The woman he let get dragged down to hell. He blamed himself. Atticus
stares in shock and disbelief at her face. “Artemis?” He asks breathlessly. Naberius grabs her
violently. “Atticus, please get me out of here.” Artemis pleads. Naberius raises its arm, pointing

towards the edge of the circle. “Release me, and her soul is free.” It bargained. Atticus does not
hesitate, whispering to himself, “Lord forgive me.” As her prepares to de-spell the circle.
However, Sarah quickly intervenes. Suddenly appearing right next to Atticus. “Stop Atticus, it’s
not Artemis. It’s a trap.” Sarah explains. Atticus becomes increasingly agitated, furious at the
revelation. He shouts, “You think you can mess with my life!? Well, I’ve got news for you
demon! I am going to destroy you, and then I am coming for every last one of your kind.” As he
begins to recite a different incantation, “Figere et purificare. Ignes gehennae ad perdendum tuam
essentiam voco. Omne reliquum tuum per fines oppositos si spatio et tempore pandetur!” A
bright white flash blinds everyone as fire engulfs Naberius completely. Atticus lets go of the
spell as he watches Naberius disappear into the roaring flame which is quickly reduced to
nothing but black smoke. When the light fades from his eyes, Atticus turns to look at Sarah
standing before him. The world around them begins to fade away as they slowly reappear in the
church. Sarah’s face is lit up with a smile as she pulls Atticus into an embrace. “You killed
Naberius. He’s gone for good!” She smiles. Her voice filled with relief. Atticus is not as relieved
at all. He realises that he did not feel Lucifer’s presence in the other realm. “Where is Lucifer?”
He asks quietly. Sarah pulls back so that she can meet his gaze. “Lucifer is here.” She tells him.
Atticus looks deep into her eyes. There is still hope in them. Miles rushes over to them. “Sarah.”
He whispers in a sad tone. “She’s fine.” Atticus assures him. He turns to face Miles. “Check on
the girl.” He says to him. Miles approaches the girl and kneels beside her. He carefully inspects
her. “It doesn’t seem like anything serious. Just a burn.” Miles says as he gently touches her face.
“Sarah what were you doing to her?” He questions her worriedly. Sarah smiles as she looks back
at him. “I had to make sure she wouldn’t become a slave to another demon ever again.” She tells
him. Atticus stares blankly at her, unsure of what to say to her. Miles gets to his feet and goes

over to Sarah. “We need to get you home. Wouldn’t you like to see Steven and Amala?” He asks
her. “Yes.” She replies happily. “Sarah! I will ask you again, where..is…Lucifer?” Atticus snaps
at her, unable to hold back anymore. “He’s right over there.” She says as she points behind him.
Atticus turns and sees that she is pointing at Pastor Ellis.

chapters.title: Jonathan Reed
chapters.text:
On May 13th, Saturday evening, a group of 13 university students went to the Upper
North Side to celebrate the end of their exams. They booked an expensive house with a
swimming pool and massive garden next to the beach and paid almost all the rent in advance. On
the first night they had a dinner consisting of mostly cocktails. They decided to spend the rest of
the night at a really trendy tavern, in the coolest neighbourhood in town. When they got there,
they lost one another and everyone ended up having totally different nights. The night ended in
death. “Tell me, what do you remember from that first night?” Detective Driscoll asked
Catherine Riker. Catherine Riker was one of only two surviving members of the original 13. “I
drank so much while clubbing that when I woke up, I wasn’t just hungover, I also lost my high
heel and my phone. I felt like an alcoholic Cinderella. Then I tried to remember what happened
the night before with my high heel so I went from bar to bar with my other high heel asking if
somebody had seen it. When I came back to the house, I found that my prince was my asshole
Ex, he had my phone and my high heel.” John Walker, the prince Catherine was talking about is
the son of a Tan Sri, whose father is involved in the business of construction and property.
Throughout his entire life, he has never been very humble nor kind. “You guys ditched me last
night. That was very uncool. You should be ashamed.” Catherine complained. John just laughed
it off. “If anyone’s the uncool one here it’s definitely you. You were probably more drunk than
all of us combined!” He says to her, laughing. Catherine punts at him on the arm, hitting him
right on his bicep. “Don’t start that, you know I like to drink when I am not working all the damn
time.” She said to him in a playful tone. He started laughing again. “Ok then, maybe we weren’t

completely cool for ditching you.” His laugh died down as he looked at her. In a matter of
seconds, she looked away embarrassed. “What was that?” He asked her, smiling. She just looked
away from him again. “Nothing, nothing!” She responded quickly. “Oh, come now, spill the
beans.” He pushed on, trying to get her to speak. After a few minutes of silence, she still was not
speaking and she did not look like she wanted to either. “Catherine? He says to her softly. She
took a deep breath and exhaled loudly before jumping on him to kiss him. “Hey, hey, I want to
know about your dead friends. Not your obviously toxic relationship!” Detective Driscoll
exclaimed as she got up from her seat. She proceeds to slap the steel table in front of her in
frustration. “Well, I don’t know what happened. Like I just told you, they ditched me before the
night even started.” Catherine said leaning her whole body on the interrogation table. “Did you
see them anywhere else after the tavern?” Detective Driscoll asked as she rubbed her tired eyes.
“I don’t remember anything.” Catherine replied looking confusedly at her feet. “Ok well, try to
think, did you see anything that could help us find who did this to them?” Detective Driscoll
added as she walked behind Catherine. She grabbed her shoulder. Catherine does not say
anything, causing Driscoll to storm out of the interrogation room even more frustrated. “I’ll be
right back for you! Don’t go anywhere.” Driscoll yelled over her shoulder before slamming the
door shut and locking it. The officers sitting in the small interview room looked at each other
with raised eyebrows and smirked. “Is that how she closes all her cases?” One of them whispered
to his colleague beside him.
Detective Driscoll goes to interrogate John next. “Hi, I’m Detective Driscoll. Are you
well? Did they get you something to eat or drink?” She asks him as she sits down in front of him.
“Yes.” John replies. He crosses his arms and gives her an annoyed expression. “Good, I’m glad.”
She answers. “So, tell me, do you understand why you are here?” Driscoll asks him. John begins

to talk. “We were going to celebrate a farewell-exams party with a group of friends. It was late at
night, and what was to be a nice cosy dinner soon turned into drunken bar crawl. There were 13
of us including myself. We went to a tavern nearby. It got pretty crazy very quickly. People
where on the dancefloor having sex, other people puking on the coaches, flying food and
condom balloons everywhere, so a bunch of us decided to get out of there.” John stops himself
and takes a sip of his water. Driscoll nods and leans forward with her elbows on the table, resting
her chin in her hands. John continues. “We left through the back door but, as we were passing by
the bar, we heard something coming from the VIP area. At first, we ignored it. We kept walking,
it sounded louder and clearer.” The sound John and his friends could hear was the sound of
breaking beer bottles on the floor. A clearly intoxicated young girl was dancing in the shards of
glass in her bare feet. The dance floor cleared and formed a circle around her, watching as she
began rolling around on the floor cutting himself on the glass. John tells detective Driscoll that
he started yelling at her to get off. “She didn’t listen, it was like she was in a trance or
something.” He recalled. “That’s enough.” Detective Driscoll says as she stands up. This has
been a particularly brutal case for detective Driscoll. Just as John was about to go into the gory
details, she felt she needed a minor breather to pull herself together. She walks outside. She takes
out a cigarette from her jacket pocket and starts puffing. One of her co-workers comes out as
well, sees her and walks towards her. “Detective.” He greets, taking the cigarette from her lips.
“Heard this one’s a doozy, how are you coping?” He asks. Driscoll does not look up to face him.
She speaks. “The scene I have seen, it doesn’t matter what kind of a heart you have, you will be
sickened. Firstly, the sight of those bodies lying there, but when you look at their faces you
realise that you’re dealing with kids. You’ve heard the story that they are young, but when you
see them, you realise that it’s a disaster.” A tearful Driscoll remarked. “I got to get back.” She

said, turning around and walking back inside. “Alright John, tell me what happened next.” She
said as she sat down on the steel chair in front of him. “I rushed inside the room to make her
stop.” He continued. When John and his friends noticed this insane scene, they all did rush inside
to try and make the girl stop hurting herself. Instead of helping her something else happened.
They themselves got caught in the trance as well. Alternative music was blasting through the
speakers as everyone present danced and raved on the dancefloor. They did not know what they
were doing but they seem to enjoy every second of it. When the song finished playing, it would
start over again. Everyone started shouting their throats raw. They spoke litanies in a language
none of them previously spoke. ‘Ô toi, le plus savant et le plus beau des Anges, Dieu trahi par le
sort et privé de louanges.’ They all sang out. ‘prends pitié de ma longue misère.’ They all
screamed. “What happened when you went in the room, John?” Detective Driscoll insisted.
“That’s the thing detective…I don’t…remember. I woke up fine at our rental house one minute
and then you and your cop buddies came in busting through the door.”
Jonathan Reed is currently sat at a bar reading police files he should not have taken out
from the precinct. On the TV, a news report about the gruesome deaths of 23 young adults at a
tavern is playing. It has been labelled a massacre. Jonathan puts the files down on the table in
front of him and looks at the television screen to see the story. ‘WARNING: The content in this
next report contains graphic material that may be harmful and/or triggering to sensitive
audiences. Viewers’ discretion is strongly advised.’ A voice from the TV says. Jonathan sighs.
‘Yeah, well…I’ve gotten used to this shit so far.’ He thinks to himself as he continues watching
the report. Jonathan looks at the pictures on the screen and sees the victims. They appear to be of
college age, most of them in their early twenties. “…toxicology results await, but generator
fumes ‘highly unlikely’ as cause of deaths in bizarre Tavern massacre, where 23 North Siders

lost their lives. The results of autopsies to determine the cause of the deaths of these young
people likely to be made public before their mass funeral on Wednesday next week. A senior
official with department of health’s forensic pathology services said: It is highly unlikely the
cause of death was fumes from a generator. However, we are waiting for toxicology results.”
Jonathan listened intently as the lady on the TV read the news. As soon as she was done,
Jonathan picked the folders up from the table. He stood up and went outside. “Excuse me.” A
woman said to him. Jonathan stopped in his tracks and turned around to face her. She was
wearing a suit, her black hair pulled up to a ponytail and thick reamed glasses. “Are you Mr.
Reed?” She asked. “Officer Reed, yes that’s me.” Jonathan replied. “I’m Detective Driscoll. Can
we talk?” She said to him as she extends her hand for him to shake. “Umm, sure let’s go
somewhere else.” Jonathan replied politely as he shook her hand and led the way to his car. He
opened the car door for her to take a seat. “What can I do for you detective?” Jonathan asked her
while closing the door. “I wanted to ask you some questions about the tavern massacre.”
Detective Driscoll explained. “What exactly did you want to know? I’m not working on that
case.” Jonathan asks curious, sitting in the driver’s seat. “Well, I know about your secret police
force. Listen, I’m not here to out you or anything. I just think there’s a lot more to this case and I
need your help.” Detective Driscoll replied. “I don’t know what you are talking about, you need
to leave now.” Jonathan told her sternly. “You don’t think it’s strange that 23 people were
murdered in that pub and that no one can explain why? What would your father do?” Detective
Driscoll retorted, starting to raise her voice. Jonathan clenched his fists tightly. “How do you
know my father?” He yelled. Detective Driscoll glared at him and folded her arms across her
chest. “Your father and I met before.” She replied coldly. “No.” Jonathan replied. He did not
believe her. She was not lying. Her eyes bore into his soul. He stared into them as they seemed to

search for something, some sign of uncertainty. After a few moments he looked away and sighed.
“Look, just leave.” He told her as he looked down at his hands. “No, I won’t!” Detective Driscoll
snapped. She took a deep breath. “Andrew he…he helped me before. Strange creatures were
killing people and no one at my precinct knew what to do. Your father came in and he took care
of it. We didn’t even ask him and he just helped us because it was the right thing to do.”
Detective Driscoll said calmly. Her voice was soft but filled with pain. Jonathan still remained
silent. He looked up again, trying to avoid looking her in the eye. “My father died three months
ago.” He replied after a little while. He then looked down at the steering wheel again. “I’m
sorry.” Detective Driscoll replied. “I’m sorry too.” Jonathan says. Detective Driscoll looked up
at him and he glanced over at her briefly before quickly looking back at the wheel. Her
expression softened as she saw tears forming behind his eyes. “Are you going to help me or
not?” She asked quietly. Jonathan looked at her and nodded his head slowly. “Yes, I’ll help.”
When the incident at the tavern was happening, no one in that room was themselves.
After a while they all lost one another and everyone ended up having totally different nights.
“The survivors from that night do not recall much.” Detective Driscoll told Jonathan. “They all
were pretty out of it.” She added. “I know, I’ve read the reports.” Jonathan said as he rubbed the
bridge of his nose. He then grabbed the files from the back sit and threw them on Driscoll’s lap.
“Here.” He said. “Yeah, I wrote most of these. Where did you get them?” Detective Driscoll asks,
reading through the files. “I know, I wasn’t supposed to remove them from the precinct but I
could not help it.” Jonathan responded as he started the engine. Detective Driscoll looked down
at the papers as she scanned through them. “Well, there is something that is not in any of these
reports.” She said. The night of the massacre, as the 23 danced and raved on the dancefloor. It
was clear they were under some sort of spell, or possession. Their eyes were completely glazed

over staring forward; almost blank and unseeing. Some of them were swaying side to side
dancing in slow, mesmerizing motions. The whole thing was disturbing. At some point they
separated, each of them going somewhere else, still completely in a daze. Wandering off in
different directions. Some walked alone, others in groups. All of them had vacant stares on their
faces. “It’s like the entire group had become…blank…” Detective Driscoll said aloud. “What
does that mean exactly?” Jonathan asked as he looked over at her. Detective Driscoll shrugged
her shoulders and put the files back on the backseat. “What is missing from these files is what we
had to do to get one of the survivors to remember details from that night.” Detective Driscoll
explained to Jonathan who looked back at her with a confused look on his face. “What do you
mean?” He asked her. “We had a hypnotist come in. It’s highly unethical but what choice did we
have.” Detective Driscoll continued. “What did they remember?” Jonathan questioned her. “He
said he saw a demonic creature hovering above them right before everyone dropped dead.”
Detective Driscoll replied. The night of the tavern massacre, John Walker was with two other
victims who had trailed off together with him to the centre of the tavern’s main dancefloor. “So
what? They were killed by demons?” Jonathan asked, sounding sceptical. “Is it crazy to think so?
Working with your father I have seen things I didn’t even think were even possible.” Detective
Driscoll replied. “We’re here.” Jonathan announced, pulling the car up to the tavern entrance.
“This place is creepy.” Jonathan whispered to himself as he got out of the car. Detective Driscoll
joined him. He tore off the police tape by the door, pushed it open and stepped inside cautiously.
There was nothing but silence and darkness around them. The only light source was coming from
a flickering bulb hanging from a wooden beam in the ceiling. The dim lighting making the
shadows on the walls and floor appear to grow larger with each step. The stench of alcohol,
sweat and blood wafted towards their noses as the pair crept deeper into the tavern, the scene of

the massacre. Driscoll felt goose bumps rising on her skin. She followed Jonathan’s lead,
carefully moving through the empty tavern. “What do you think you’re going to find? We
already did a thorough sweep of this whole place.” Detective Driscoll whispered to Jonathan. He
ignored her remark and kept walking. He removes a device from his pocket and turns it on. A
bright blue light emits from the small screen. “What is that thing?” Detective Driscoll asks. “It is
an EMF detector. We use it to detect demons or anything witchy.” Jonathan explains to her. “So,
you do believe me then? That they could have been killed by occultists.” Detective Driscoll said
as she moved closer to Jonathan. “We’ll see.” Jonathan replies, still scanning the room carefully.
“These things, demons, what do they want?” Detective Driscoll continues to question him. “They
don’t want anything, just death and destruction for its own sake.” Jonathan answers. Suddenly,
the EMF detector beeps loudly. It goes off and Jonathan grabs the flashlight from his coat and
shines it in front of him. “Do you hear that?!” He asks as he starts to quickly move across the
dancefloor. They enter the area where the young girl was dancing on the broken glass. Jonathan
looks around and sees nothing. On the night of the tavern massacre, the 23 victims were seen to
be perfectly healthy minutes before they suddenly dropped to the floor, dead. They were happy
and euphoric as they danced the night away.
“Hey, Asinewe where have you been all night. Hello…!” One woman says to her friend
who was so deep in her trance she could not respond. John Walker was beside her when he
momentarily regained lucidity. He fell backwards onto the dancefloor and started to scream in in
terror. He could not stop shaking. He tried to crawl away but could barely keep himself upright.
The young woman noticed the commotion. Before she can go and check on John, her friend also
dropped to the floor, convulsing violently until she eventually stops altogether. The remaining
dancers stopped dancing as they noticed the disturbance. The young woman held her friend

Asinewe Sanarhana as she died in her arms while pleading for help. No one knew what was
happening to her. All around the tavern, people started to drop to the ground, convulsing. Soon
everyone was screaming and running around like chickens pecking at the dirt. It was chaos. The
sound of terrified screams pierced the air and echoed throughout the tavern. In the end, 23 young
women and men were all dead. Their corpses lay scattered about the tavern like fallen leaves,
twisted and contorted. The scene haunted detective Driscoll. She and Jonathan currently stood at
the site of the brutal scene, attempting to figure out exactly what had caused it to happen. As they
both looked around, they heard footsteps approaching. Jonathan turned toward the source.
Detective Driscoll pulled out her gun, ready to defend herself. A tall and muscular man emerged
from behind the bar and charged towards them. His dark brown eyes glazed over, staring straight
ahead. His lips curled into an evil smirk as he approached them. Detective Driscoll immediately
aimed at him but before she can shoot, the man shoots his weapon first. Hitting Jonathan straight
in the chest. “Jonathan!!” She cries out in shock. She quickly fires off several bullets at the tall
man, causing him to run off and away from them. “JONATHAN!!!” She cries out in alarm once
more, kneeling down to meet him as he lay in the floor seemingly drawing his last breath.
Detective Driscoll quickly checked his pulse but found none. Her mind was racing trying to
figure out what to do. She stood up and began chasing after the assailant. After several minutes
she returns to check on Jonathan’s body. She walks in the room and is met with a shocking sight.
Jonathan had been shot in the chest and she clearly saw him die right before her eyes. But there
he was, standing as if nothing had happened. “H…how…” Detective Driscoll stuttered. “You
were dead. I saw you die, how are you standing here?” Detective Driscoll said to him. “I sold my
soul to the devil.” He answered, his voice low and monotone. “Let’s finish this.” Jonathan said as
he picked up his EMF. Detective Driscoll stared at him in total disbelief.

Chapter 25
Avery Melblac
Everything in this world exists in its own unique form. People, trees, oxygen. The same is
true of the devil. It is ever present in this world in its own particular form. The devil is a reality.
A force that moves in and out of existence causing death and destruction wherever it lands.
Lucifer is a devil everyone is familiar with. To all those who live, he is the embodiment of pain,
suffering and sorrow. He is everything everyone fears and hates. It came as a surprise to the team
when the man who currently is possessed by the spirit of Lucifer came with them willingly back
to Defiance. The demi’s presence unsettled everyone at defiance greatly. He sat in the white
room where they sequestered him, not putting up a fight or even complaining, just looking off
into space. The door opened gently and the harsh artificial lighting flooded Avery’s eyes as she
entered the tiny room. She squinted, attempting to defend herself from the accursed light.
Lucifer’s presence lingered in the brightly-lit threshold. Avery entered the room and locked the
door behind her. Fear and uncertainty gripped her. As she sits down opposite him, she realized
that she was not going to enjoy what was coming. “Hello, Dr Melblac.” Ellis spoke. His words
were laced with genuine concern. It felt as though he could sense her unease and fear. “I am
pleased to finally meet you, Dr.” He continued. “Why?” She asks curiously. “Because we have
much to talk about.” Ellis replied cryptically. Avery was not sure exactly how to proceed next.
With the stakes being higher than usual. She thought back to Andrew, lying in a hospital bed,
injured and bleeding badly. He had been shot by Damien’s men. The very people who devoted
their whole entire existence to serving evil, to serving the devil. Now the devil sits right next to
her. “I know what you are thinking. Why don’t I just kill everyone in here.” Ellis suggested

casually. “You could, you are him after all. The prince of darkness, Lucifer.” Avery pointed out.
She remained defiantly calm, knowing that he could hurt her if he wanted to. He could try to
scare her. Make her feel weak. Yet he remained human and cordial. At this moment, he had no
real reason to harm her or anyone else. “And yet I’m not the devil, am I?” Ellis replied slyly. “I’d
assume… you are.” Avery responded in a whisper. There was a long pause as they eyed each
other across the table, sizing each other up. Finally, Avery speaks again. “But let us speak of
you.” She paused again before continuing with caution. “What would you like to know?” Ellis
asked. Avery shrugged her shoulders. “Well, your plans for starters. Why did your people revive
you, what do you want?” She asked her questions, all the while keeping eye contact with Ellis.
She did not blink, nor did she take her gaze off of the devil the sat across her. Ellis watched her,
his face emotionless. Then, suddenly, he burst out laughing. Avery furrowed her brows at him
quizzically. He laughed hard and loud. “You’re funny!” Ellis exclaimed between laughs. She
cocked her head to the side, waiting patiently for him to stop. “Forgive me, I just find all those
devil worshippers laughable.” He continued, his laughter dying down slowly, his smile still
prominent in the expression. “They worship an imaginary god. Fools is what they are.” Ellis
continued, sitting back in his seat, folding his hands together and resting his elbows on the table.
“Then what do they worship?” Avery asked him directly. “An idea…” he answered simply. His
tone of voice gave nothing away. His eyes never left hers. “What kind of idea?” She pushed
further. Ellis shook his head slightly and chuckled. “One that should not exist.” He said calmly,
his eyes returning to focus on hers. A knock on the window interrupted the conversation. Avery
glanced out the window and saw Mrs Williams calling her to come outside. “Go.” Ellis said
nonchalantly. Avery rose from her chair and walked to the door leading outside. The day had
ended and the moon shone bright above Defiance headquarters. Mrs Williams waited outside the

white room for Avery. As soon as she was outside, the two made their way across the facility
towards where the others had already gathered. They arrived at the guildhall. Most of the leaders
of at Defiance were there anxiously waiting for them. They all looked up at Avery expectantly.
“Is it true? Is he really thee Lucifer?” Daniel inquired nervously. “We will have to wait to find
out, but I think it’s safe to say that he has returned.” Avery told them. Everyone’s eyes widened
in shock and disbelief. They were all silent as they processed the information. The silence lasted
for some time before Mrs Williams spoke up. “So, what do we do now?” She asked. Everything
they knew about the Devil’s den told them that they have always had a grand plan, an endgame.
They are always up to something. They are very active and very persistent. Everything they do is
very thoughtful and they invest in the future as well as the present. Defiance has just never been
able to figure out that plan. So many people have died already because of it. “Well,” Daniel
started, taking charge. “What should we do first? We can’t just sit around doing nothing.” Daniel
stated firmly. “No, we cannot. We need to attack them head on. Take down all their bases. That’s
where the action is.” Mrs Williams added. “The question is what do we do with that man, Ellis?”
Daniel questioned once more. “I think the best course of action will be for us to kill him. Destroy
Lucifer’s new vessel before he can manifest fully!” Mrs Williams suggested, looking to everyone
else. They all nodded in agreement. “Very well.” Martin Creswell also agreed. “We must act
quickly then.” Daniel concluded.
Elsewhere, in a small mosque on the outskirts of the city. The night sky was clear and
quiet, except for the crickets singing their nightly song. A man is praying Ishaa prayers when he
is interrupted by a little girl in black clothes trying to enter between him and the next man
praying by his side, leaving no distance between them. As the little girl tried to enter between the
two praying men, they both felt some form of spiritual pressure. It surprised them both. They

were uncomfortable. “This is so boring, how can you put up with this torture every day?” The
girl whispered aloud to them as she read a Qur’an. She reads through a sentence she recognized
all too well. “And behold, we said to the angels: ‘Bow down to Adam’ and they bowed down.
Not so Iblis: he refused and was haughty: he was of those who reject Faith.” She recited. The two
men turned their heads towards her. She smiled wickedly, while they scoffed in annoyance.
“Leave now. Why are you even here if not to pray!?” The second man inquired in disgust. “I am
here for a sacrifice.” The little girl replied cheerfully.
Avery returned to the white room to have one last conversation with Ellis. They were
seated at the table once again across from each other. Both of them staring at each other in
silence. Her mind was racing. Every single thought raced through her head. What was happening?
What was he? Could he really be Lucifer? The man before her appeared human in appearance,
but inside… there could be darkness and evil. If he truly was the Devil, then why was she still
alive? Why was the world still intact? She was starting to wonder of the answers to these
questions. The more she studied the man, the more her heart began to soften. She became aware
of his warm brown eyes looking intently into hers. She took notice of a deep scar on his cheek,
along with a patch of hair that grew over his eyebrow. She could see his lips move, but could not
hear what he was saying. Her eyes wandered over his face and landed on the tattoos covering his
arms. There were intricate designs carved into them. Each design stood out against the pale skin
of his arms and chest. Avery’s attention shifted upwards, eventually focusing in his dark, brown
eyes once more. She could see herself reflected in his umber irises, staring right back at her. Ellis
leaned forward, moving closer to Avery. “Tell me, Avery. Are you afraid of me?” He questioned
softly. Avery remained silent for a brief moment. She opened her mouth, ready to respond to his
question, but no words came out. She closed her mouth and blinked rapidly several times. She

felt uneasy. She looked away from him. “Are you afraid of what I’ve become?” He repeated
again, this time, louder. Again, she remained silent. “Answer me.” He demanded sternly. Avery
looked back at him. Her eyes narrowed. She raised her hand and swiped away the strands of hair
that fell over her forehead. “Of course, I am.” She declared, her voice echoing throughout the
room. Ellis leaned back in his seat. “So, what is it going to be then, Dr Melblac?” He asked,
smirking. “Are you going to kill me?” Ellis looked amused as he asked the question. The truth is,
that was exactly what she was going to do. She was going to cut him down regardless. “Yes, I
am.” She assured him quietly. Ellis’ smile vanished. “It seems Sarah was right about you lot….
You are quite ruthless.” He commented sarcastically. “You should be proud.” She admitted with
a frown. Ellis’ expression darkened. “Let me let you in on a little secret. By the end of the day,
you are going to release me by your own volition.” He said this so casually, with no hint of fear
or remorse. It was as if he was predicting a future that was inevitable. “That won’t happen. Why
would I do that.” Avery countered. She wanted him to panic or show fear, so he revealed his true
form. But he remained stoic. She could tell by the slight quiver of his jaw that he was restraining
himself from reacting to anything she might say. She sighed heavily and shook her head. This
was frustrating. “Well, I am going to enlightenment you Dr Melblac. Then I am going to make
you an offer you just won’t want to refuse.” Ellis said with a smirk. Avery’s face stiffened. Her
eyes flashed dangerously. “Oh? Enlighten me pastor Ellis, I’m eager to know what that looks
like.” She challenged him. Ellis paused for a moment, seemingly lost in his own thoughts, and
then, with a sudden burst of energy, he exclaimed; “History! Do you know your history?” He
asked. “I believe I know enough.” She answered curtly. “Good. Do you know that story from
long ago? The one where God, in his Infinite wisdom cast Satan out of Heaven?” Ellis
questioned. Avery shrugged. “Sounds familiar.” She answered him. Ellis looked at her

knowingly. “Well, let’s rewind then. Let’s take a look at that part.” He commanded. Avery did
not bother to reply. Instead, she stared back at him defiantly. Ellis took a deep breath and exhaled
slowly. “Everyone always assumes that I’m the only devil... Lucifer... Lucifer oh he made me do
it oh Lucifer the ruler of all that is wrong with this reality.” His tone was low, almost reverential
as he went on with his rant. “So, you are him? Lucifer?” She inquired curiously, raising her
eyebrows slightly. That was the confirmation she needed. Ellis glanced at her, then looked back
down to the table. “Yes. Yes, I suppose I am.” He admitted with a sad sigh, turning his gaze back
towards Avery. “But you’re missing the point.” He continued. Avery gave him a perplexed
expression. “What point? Enlighten me.” She requested, leaning forward eagerly awaiting his
response. “All this hatred in our lives, this hatred…this bitterness, this hate, these feelings, these
emotions. All this pain that we feel. It is the devil’s work…. There are devils in this world and;
Lucifer isn’t even the worst one.” His voice was rife with contempt. Avery sat upright in her
chair; her hands tightly clenched. “Lucifer isn’t even the worst one?” she repeated incredulously.
Ellis nodded. He had peaked her curiosity. “I don’t understand.” She responded. Ellis took
another deep breath. “Do you remember your life when you were young, Avery? How beautiful
everything was? How happy you were?” He wondered. Avery pondered for a moment. She
remembered a time when she used to dream of living in a big house. With lots of money, having
a pet dog and playing with a bunch of cute kids of her own. She also loved music and dancing.
Things like that, she remembered. “I did have all of that.” She finally spoke. “When I was young,
I dreamed of being a singer and being happy. Then I grew up. I learned the hard way that I
cannot just sit back while creatures like you still exist in our world.” She told him firmly. Ellis
looked her directly in the eye. “I’m sorry I exist and….” Before he could finish his thought,
Avery interrupted him with a loud thud as she threw her hands on the surface of the table in

frustration. “Enough!! Get to your damn point!” she shouted. Ellis looked at her. She surprised
and intrigued him. He was not expecting her outburst. “Sorry, but this was not the way I planned
things to go.” He muttered under his breath. Just outside the white room, the thundering sound of
footsteps could be heard coming nearer. The full might of Defiance was coming to collect Ellis
and lead him to the chamber, to his death. He knew what was about to happen, so without
hesitation, he rose to his feet, ready to surrender once more. Avery gazed upon him and was
overcome with an emotion she did not recognise. Her face hardened as she looked at him. She
knew that he was the enemy and that he needed to be put down. Yet, she still felt bad for him.
The door swung open violently. A slew of armed men entered the room and subdued Ellis. They
started to drag him out of the room as Avery looked on. He looked back at her briefly, giving her
a small nod. Before he was completely gone, he said one last thing to Avery. “Take good care of
Amala.” With that, they pulled him out of sight. Avery watched as they disappeared around a
corner.
Mrs Williams and Martin were watching this all unfold; Martin was the one who gave the
order. Avery walks up to them and asks, “Do you need me for this?” Her voice sounded distant.
Mrs Williams shook her head. “No, thank you, Dr Melblac. We can handle it from here. We’ll
send word if there are any complications.” She replied, trying her best not to show how actually
terrified she was. They had dealt with demons before, but never one that could be potentially as
strong as Ellis. His calmness unnerved her. “Ok…well I guess I will go now then.” Avery
announced. “Wait.” Martin stopped her. “There is something we must discuss.” He informed her
as he turned to Mrs Williams. He nodded slightly in her direction, motioning to her to leave and
go deal with the Ellis situation. Mrs Williams nodded back and walked away, leaving Martin and
Avery. Once she left, Martin turned his gaze back to Avery. “What that man just said to you just

now…” He trailed off, hesitating slightly. “Why did he mention Amala?” He questioned. Avery
hesitated for a moment. She was not sure why Ellis had mentioned Amala’s name, or what
concern Ellis had with her. “Come, walk with me.” Martin ordered as he started moving towards
the stairs leading up to his office. As they ascended to the second floor, Martin led Avery
through a hallway and into his office. He gestured for Avery to sit down on one of the chairs,
which she did so reluctantly. After taking his own seat opposite her, Martin looked at her with a
serious expression on his face. “Tell me about her.” Martin began. Avery stared blankly at him
for a few moments. Martin waited patiently until Avery finally replied. “She’s an asset.” Avery
stated plainly. Martin leaned closer and fixed upon Avery with a firm gaze. “I don’t want a
repeat of what happened with that Sarah girl. You have to keep an eye on your demis Dr Melblac.
Lest we forget, they are still demons who could turn on us at any time.” He warned her sternly.
Avery narrowed her eyes as she studied Martin carefully, her arms folded against her chest
defensively. “As I said sir, she’s an asset.” Avery reiterated. “Amala is fiercely loyal and brave,
and always at the front line. They all are. What happened with Sarah May was unfortunate, I will
not let it happen again.” Avery added forcefully. “I understand. We just don’t want another
incident.” Martin agreed. “What are her abilities?” He queried. “She has been working with
computers her whole life, that alone makes her quite useful. With regards to her demi powers, it
appears her demon has given her an affinity with foresight, not so much divination but an
instinctual sense of preparedness.” Avery explained. Martin nodded as he considered all of this
information. “So, she could hypothetically know when you plan to attack her or the others.” He
finished. “Precisely. That’s What I have observed at least” Avery affirmed. “Very well. I want
you to keep an eye on her, alright?” Martin instructed. “Of course.” She replied. Martin stood up
and headed for the door. “You are dismissed.” He told her. “Thank you, sir.” Avery replied

formally. “Don’t worry, I trust you.” Martin assured her as he gestures towards the exit for her.
She leaves Martin’s office, leaving him alone with his thoughts. She did not want to show it in
front of Martin, but she was also concerned about what plans Ellis Could have for Amala.

chapters.title: Sarah May
chapters.text:
A teenage Sarah stood in a dark forest. The sound of thousands of insects buzzing filled
the air. She begins to walk through the dense fog. Her pace is slow and clumsy. The pink dress
she wore was covered in blood. So were her arms and legs. She stops at a clearing in the woods.
The people chasing her were drawing closer. She was too weak to move. Two figures dart
through the trees. Sarah picks up her pace, moving faster now. Suddenly, out of the shadows,
someone throws a black hood over her head and violently pulls her backwards. She falls to the
ground. She screams and kicks as the two figures chasing her pin her down. They were covered
in dirt and mud and smelled of sulphur. One of them smiles, flashing the few teeth he had left.
“We finally caught the little witch.” He says. The other one pulls Sarah’s hair roughly, causing
her to cry out loudly. He leans down close to Sarah’s ear. “We got you bitch.” He whispered.
Sarah keeps Kicking and screaming. Then, one of them hits her in the head with the base of a
sawed-off shot gun which he carried. Sarah instantly stops moving. At that moment, present day
Sarah wakes up from the terrible dream. She sits upright, breathing hard. It took a few seconds
for her mind to process what had just transpired. It had been a bad dream. Something that had
plagued her nightmares for so long. It had gotten worse over time. It seemed every time she fell
asleep, something horrible happens. Her dreams were memories from her past; she hated having
to relive them every night. She sat in silence for several minutes, trying to get rid of the lingering
image of the nightmare that remained imprinted in her brain. She was tired and sore. It would
take some time to wake herself up fully. She decided that she needed some fresh air. She climbed
out of bed, walked across the room and opened the window, letting the breeze blow gently inside.

It calmed her somewhat. She leaves the window and walks towards the mirror. She stares at her
reflection. She sighed and rubbed Her temples, feeling pain throughout her head. Her mind kept
drifting back and forth. She could not help but feel worried. To calm her mind, she heads to the
library, the only other place which brought her peace. There was a fireplace in the centre of the
room, it burned brightly as it cast an orange glow on to the surrounding bookshelves. When she
got to the library, she found her friends all seated on the sofa together. Amala and Steven were
engrossed in a book, whilst Miles, looked up at her as she entered. “Hey, are you feeling ok?” He
asked her. “Yeah…” She replied quietly. “Are you sure? You look like you’ve seen a ghost.”
Miles continued. “Yeah…” She answered again. Amala looked up from her book to glance at
Sarah, whose face showed nothing else but anxiety. “Is everything ok?” Amala inquired. “It was
just a really bad dream.” Sarah responds vaguely. Miles puts his arm around Sarah’s shoulder
and pulled her closer to him. She immediately sank into his embrace, resting her head on his
shoulder. “We’ve all been through a lot recently.” Miles commented. “That’s true…Me and
Miles got our ass handed to us by a little girl in the woods.” Steven admitted. Everyone laughed.
Even though it was not completely funny, it was still nice to be able to laugh. It helped distract
them all from their worries. Miles squeezed Sarah tighter, as if to reassure her, he was right here.
Sarah relaxed into Miles’ embrace, enjoying the comfort he provided. She closes her eyes. She
felt like she could drift back asleep, but she was disturbed once more. Then a drift picked up,
blowing papers from a shelf in the library. Some fell onto Amala’s lap. Steven noticed it. He
quickly bent down to retrieve them for her. “Thanks.” Amala uttered. “I am so sorry guys.”
Sarah apologised sincerely, with tears forming in her eyes. “I’m sorry I released Lucifer from
that statue, I’m sorry I abandoned you.” She added. “Don’t apologize Sarah…” Miles said to her
softly. “We are all here for each other and, whatever happens with that man, I’m sure we’ll be

able to handle it…” Amala added firmly. “I hear they’re killing him, you know, before the
demon can fully manifest.” Steven mentioned. “It won’t work.” Sarah stated plainly, staring at
the flames. “How do you know?” Amala questioned curiously. Sarah remained silent for a
moment. “I just know it.” She answered. She closed her eyes again, remembering the dark time
from her nightmare. “I’ve been going through something lately too.” Amala said quietly,
breaking the awkward silence. “What’s wrong?” Steven asked her cautiously. “I’ve been having
really vivid visions. They scare the crap out of me.” She explained. The rest of the group nodded,
understanding exactly how Amala felt. “Sometimes I see the world in flames. The literal end
times at our front gate.” She added. “Sounds intense…” Miles commented. “They are.” Sarah
agreed. “This is serious, you know your demon has the power of divination, right?” Sarah asked
seriously. “Yes, but…the world is not going to end….is it?” Amala said nervously. Steven and
Miles exchanged concerned looks. Amala frowned slightly, thinking about these strange visions.
Sarah noticed her expression. “I know it is scary but, you need to see them again, that could tell
us what’s really going on.” Sarah reasoned. “I cannot control it. The visions, they come on their
own. I haven’t had one in weeks.” Amala revealed. She was starting to panic. “Don’t worry, I
know what to do.” Sarah reassured her friend.
Long ago, a great Duke who was said to be possessed by a divinatory demon known as
the Abigor, commanded vast armies. He was one of the finest war-chiefs of his time, respected
for his ability to reveal the coming of wars, unrest and battles. His followers claimed he could
see the enemy before they came. Amala Jones had that demon inside of her now. “Some time
ago, while we were all in the Devil’s den, I found this awesome grimoire called The Book of
Shadows. I only had a few minutes with it, in that time I learned of a great method to help with
divination.” Sarah informed the others. “Let’s hear it then!” Miles encouraged her. “It’s called

Osteomancy.” She began. “Osteomancy?” Steven repeated. He like the others, was confused.
They had never had of it. “Basically, it is the art of divining the future. Using bones.” Sarah told
them. “How does that work?” Steven questioned. Sarah shrugged her shoulders. “I have no
idea.” She replied honestly. “But I know someone who does.” She continued. “Who?” Miles
inquired. “Atticus, he is world class when it comes to these types of things. I know he probably
has a book on Osteomancy somewhere in his study.”
“Let’s go get it.” Miles suggested excitedly. “Sounds good to me.” Steven chimed in.
Sarah nodded her head in agreement. They sneak into Atticus’ office and begin searching. In no
time, they find a book on ancient magic. They flip through it quickly, looking for anything that
may give them a clue about how to do divination rituals. Miles finds a page dedicated to
ritualistic spellcraft. It contains instructions, along with various spells and charms which will
help increase the accuracy of the divination. “Great, you found it.” Sarah said. She grabbed the
book from Miles and flipped through more of the pages, making note of any information which
seems helpful. “Amala grab that box over there, we’re going to need it, and Steven, bring the
candles.” She instructed. After some time, they have managed to gather all the materials they
needed. They gathered at the library, where they could have their privacy to perform this highly
secret ritual. Amala placed the box carefully on the table, and then removed the cover. “Ew, are
those human bones!?” She remarked as soon as she saw the contents of the box. “Yeah, and I
they may be bones from…a baby.” Steven joked, picking up one of the bones to examine it
closely. The bones in the box were extremely old, possibly even over 200 years old. “Why do we
have to use baby bones though, it’s so creepy.” Amala complained. She places a hand over her
mouth and noise to cover up for the foul smell coming from the bones. “Think of bones as living
things, as you might plants. Just as you would choose various plants for your workings based on

their magical purposes like protection, love, money or whatever. Animals also have associations
in witchcraft. Not all bones are used for the same thing: Snake energy from the bone of a snake,
is very different than say, fox energy. Whichever part of the body a bone comes from can also
play a role in this, just as the age of the body the bone come from.” Sarah explained. “Hmm,
baby bones are good for witchcraft! Got it, what’s next?” Miles exclaimed enthusiastically.
Sarah prepared them for the ritual. She gets ready to begin. “You remember what to do? Okay.”
She instructed. Miles nodded his head confidently, whilst Amala looked on nervously. “Just
breathe Amala, everything’ll be fine.” Steven told her reassuringly. “Here we go, guys.” Sarah
announces. She lit the candle with Steven’s lighter and held it high above the bones. Miles
watched on anxiously, as the fire began flickering rapidly across the bones, before growing
stronger. He then cut into his palm and allowed blood to spill into the flame and on the bones
below. Once it dripped, the flame suddenly flared, lighting up the room and casting the bones in
an ominous glow. Sarah took a hold of Amala’s hand and gently placed it above the spelled
bones. “Vide in posterum fatum meum venire. Look into the bones Amala, tell us what you see.”
Sarah prompted her. Amala closed her eyes and focused on the bones. A moment passed and she
suddenly opened her eyes. Her face took on a look of fear and horror. “The bones are
speaking…they’re showing me…. I…I can’t…” She said in a trembling voice. “You have to see,
Amala, we have to know what’s coming.” Sarah urged her urgently. Amala struggled to keep the
image in her mind. “No..no I don’t want to, please stop, I’m scared!” She cried. “We’re right
here with you Amala.” Steven pointed out. Amala looked around the room desperately. The
others waited anxiously, unsure of how to help her. All of a sudden, Amala’s eyes turn white,
then pitch black, as she fell backwards onto the floor. She is unconscious.

A young girl is strung up on a wooden post in a dark basement. Her orange flower dress
is torn and covered in blood that drips down her leg. Sarah is there, so is Miles, Jonathan and
Avery. To her surprise, Amala can see herself there as well, standing over the young girl. It
appears the Osteomancy ritual worked. Amala was currently inside a vision. She watches silently
as Sarah cuts the head off a black-coloured bird with a knife. A raven. Dark blood gushes out of
the wound, leaving the now headless raven flapping around. Sarah takes its blood and begins to
draw strange symbols all over the young girl’s body as the girl screams for help and for them to
let her go. Amala is horrified at the sight, more so that the version of her who is actually there,
does not do anything to stop this apparent madness. Sarah is still drawing strange symbols on the
girl's stomach when, Ellis emerges from the darkness, his hands covered in blood. The girl
continues to beg for mercy. “Somebody help me… please…. I Just want to go home.” Sarah
ignores the girl, focusing completely on the ritual in front of her. The others are hesitant, but
Ellis reassures them. “Do not let this thing! Fool you. There is nothing left but evil inside of her,
maintain your resolve.” The group nods their heads determinedly. Amala watches on helplessly.
Sarah has with her something covered in black plastic. She tears it open to reveal The Book of
Shadows. “Only this book can undo the evil that has been done.” Ellis declared confidently. “No,
please let me go.” The young girl pleads. Apparition Amala quickly makes her way towards the
book to have a closer look. On the open page, a hand written text reads: ‘PURIFICATION BY
FIRE.’ She turns around, now facing Ellis. “Do it now…” He instructs. Miles pours a dark, oily
liquid all over the head and body of the young girl. “I’m so sorry.” Vision Amala says to the
poor girl as apparition Amala watches all this unfold. The young girl gags and chokes as the
liquid flows down her face. Avery takes a box of matches from her pocket. She tries to light one,
it is damp and does not easily catch fire. Apparition Amala is horrified as she realises what is

about to happen. Finally, Avery successfully managed to get the match lit. She holds it up near
the young girl, about to do what must be done. Apparition Amala cannot take any more of this
and attempts to lunge herself towards Avery to stop her. “No….!!” She screams. With that,
Amala wakes up panting and sweating. Her heart racing furiously. She looked around the library
frantically, looking for something to ground her. She turned her head to the side. “Sarah?!” She
called out, her voice filled with fright. She sat upright again, her breathing rapid and shallow.
“What did you see Amala? Are you okay?” Sarah asked concernedly. “Why did you come back
here Sarah!” Amala yelled harshly, shoving Sarah away from her. “Hey! What’s wrong with
you!?” Miles questioned, alarmed by Amala’s behaviour. “It’s her, I don’t know how but,
somehow she’s involved with that man, Lucifer’s demi.” Amala begins to explain hastily. “What
the hell did you see Amala?” Sarah asked again. Amala sighed deeply, attempting to calm herself.
“You, us, we were burning a little girl. That man…Ellis, he was there.” Amala stammered out,
afraid of Sarah’s involvement in whatever she saw in her vision. “So we found her.” Sarah
whispered softly. “How can you believe we’d do such a ridiculous thing?” Steven begun to speak.
He is interrupted by Sarah. “We have to tell Avery everything you saw, now!” Sarah
commanded, looking over to Amala. Miles and Steven look worried, while Sarah looked
indifferent, as if she had already known about this situation. “You are crazy if you think….”
Before Amala could finish, Atticus walked in, “Guys? Everything ok? You all seem kind of
freaked out right now, what did you do?” He asked. Sarah looked at him and then over at Amala
again. “It’s ok Amala, you can tell him everything.” She assured her.
In Avery’s office, the team is hurdled around her desk. “Why are you practicing
witchcraft unsupervised again Sarah. Did you not learn anything from the last time.” Avery
scolded Sarah severely. Sarah sighed and tried to explain her why she had to do it. “I don’t have

Naberius controlling me anymore. I am a witch; it is in my blood and I will do magic when I
want.” She responded stubbornly. Avery is mad. She decides not to argue with Sarah about the
ethics of it all. “Alright then. Just remember, if it gets out of hand, we will stop you by any
means. Defiance does not take kindly to irresponsible supernaturals.” Avery warned. “Yes
ma’am.” Sarah replied defiantly. “She’s working with Ellis. All of us were there while she
sacrificed a little girl in some sinister ritual.” Amala begun explaining to the team, pointing at
Sarah accusingly. “Amala, relax. Take a minute to think this through, use that incredible talent of
yours. There must be a valid reason why you were all there, isn’t that right Sarah.” Atticus
insisted. “Yeah, and I am pretty sure I know who that girl might be.” Miles added. “Belial.”
Steven concluded. Sarah nodded her head, confirming his suspicions. The three of them now
know that they could not ignore that. The rest of them had no clue who Belial was or her
significance. “Ellis could be dangerous, but he is not an immediate threat. What Belial has
planned is far more deadly. If Amala saw her in her vision, it must be important. If we do not
stop her, then this whole situation could be much worse.” Sarah continued to explain. Avery
looked between Sarah, Miles and Atticus. “What exactly are you saying? how do you know any
of this?” She demanded. “My time with Lucifer…he showed me things. It opened my eyes.”
Sarah began. The team looks at her curiously and patiently waiting for her to continue. She
becomes silent, lost in her nightmares. “Sarah is right, when Miles and I went up against Belial,
she said she wants to use me to open some door to hell.” Steven said. As they talk, Amala’s heart
beats faster and faster with every new detail. She remembers every single part of her vision.
Every single detail of that nightmare. Avery looks at Atticus knowingly. “We have to inform the
others, now.” She tells him decisively. Atticus nods in reply before he disappears out from
Avery’s office. “Sarah, come with me.” Avery requests. The two of them also leave the office

and head down to the cells where Ellis is being kept. When Sarah steps into the cell, Ellis smiles
widely at her. “Good evening, Dr Melblac, are you here to let me out of my cage?” He asked
slyly. “You are never getting out of here.” Avery stated coldly. “Tell me about Belial.” She
demands firmly, walking straight over to the bars. “Finally, you are all caught up.” He smirked.
“But for corruption thou hast made Belial, a king of hostility. All his dominions are in darkness,
and his purpose is to bring about wickedness and guilt… I’ve read about it before. I Just never
realised what it meant.” Avery continued. Ellis laughs cruelly at what he perceives from her as
ignorance. “Oh Dr Melblac, you are still misinformed. I’d urge you to forget everything you
think you know about demons and devils.” He smiled smugly, clearly enjoying her frustration.
He continues to taunt he. As they continue to talk, Sarah moves closer to Avery. She sneaks up
close to her ear and whispers, “Somnum.” In that moment, Avery is saddened by the realisation
that Sarah had played them, and has been working with Ellis…the devil, this whole time. She
begins to fall. Sarah catches her before she hits the floor. “Quid nunc gloriosissime princeps?”
Ellis asks Sarah sarcastically.

chapters.title: The Deviants part I
chapters.text:
One of the great paradoxes throughout time is that the more we understand the
supernatural world, the more we discover that our everyday beliefs about it are wrong. The
dichotomy between God, all that is good and the Devil, all that is evil, in particular, is one of the
more misunderstood subjects amongst disciples of the faith. The powers of darkness have
captured the attention of the vast majority of people, Christian or not, for as long as there has
been time. Unfortunately, a considerable amount of the knowledge accrued on this subject is
misunderstandings and outright misinformation. Much of what society think they know about
Satan, demons, and other evil powers is guided far more by incorrect tradition and hearsay rather
than the objective matter of fact. Sarah May has just come to this understanding. It has changed
