her entire worldview, and her perceived purpose on this plane. She was about to impart that very
same knowledge unto Dr Avery Melblac, by force if she has too. “I am sorry Avery, but I had to
do it this way. You would have never agreed to come with me if I asked nicely.” She apologizes
to a still knocked out Avery. Ellis was there with her. Sarah broke him out and they both snuck
out of Defiance with Avery. They managed to not get noticed, able to slip away from Defiance
and return to the safety of their shoddy base to prepare for the inevitable confrontation with
Belial and her Devil’s den. The room was filled with a deafening silence, neither one speaking to
the other. They both sat anxiously waiting for Avery to wake up from her spell induced blackout.
It was not long until her eyelids flickered open. “Where…where am I?” Avery groaned weakly.
“Don’t worry, you’re alright.” A relieved smile appeared on Sarah’s face. Avery sits up slowly,
looking at Ellis, who is standing nearby. “You!!” She shouted angrily. “Avery please calm

down!” Sarah exclaimed. “Calm down! Calm down?! You betrayed us once again Sarah!!
Why!?” Avery shouts. She was rightfully angry and in shock. “Because…” Sarah trailed off.
“No.” Avery cut her off, shaking her head. “Please just tell me why you are working for him.”
She pleaded desperately. “He’s a danger to us all!” Avery retorted angrily. “That might be one
day true, I know. But we need him right now.” Sarah argued. Ellis chimes in. “Why don’t we all
just calm down, and relax. Let’s not lose our heads.” He suggested calmly. Avery gets
increasingly agitated and lunges towards Ellis, she was about to punch him in his smug face.
“Enough! Stop this or I will have to put you to sleep again.” Sarah warned Avery, her voice soft.
Avery backed off and calmed herself. “Why did you bring me here? To kill me?” Avery spats
angrily. “What? No! Don’t you remember what Amala told us, about her vision. Our path is still
intertwined for a long time to go, I’m not going kill you, I…we need your help.” Sarah tried to
explain to Avery gently. Avery took in Sarah’s words, trying to find any truths behind her
statement. “I’m the one who placed Lucifer’s spirit within pastor Ellis. He agreed to do it after
what I had to say. I’m confident that you will want to help as well once you hear it for yourself.”
Sarah continued earnestly. “Well then, let’s hear it.” Avery said impatiently. Sarah begun to
explain everything that happened from the moment Naberius used her to release the demon from
the ushabti statue that fateful night. “I was ready to die with it inside of me. But that did not go
as planned, instead I saw a truth that changed by whole entire perspective.” Avery’s curiosity
was peaked. “What happened?” Avery interrupts. As Sarah attempts to make it as clear as
possible to Avery, she begins to flashback to the moment when it was all revealed to her by the
spirit of Lucifer which inhabited her body. It was so surreal that even now, as it is merely a
freshly implanted memory, Sarah still finds it difficult to believe that everything she saw that day
was actually reality. Lucifer regaled a defeated Sarah with tall tales recounting each time he has

ever possessed a poor unsuspecting soul. The way he told it, each iteration of a demi or vessel
which contained the unique demon known as Lucifer, was vastly different from the previous and
onto the next. As if Lucifer was being reborn and reincarnated across the vast expanse of time.
Apparently, it is the same of all demons, all Devils. “Lucifer, well the latest iteration at least, he
told me that it feels like some sort of constant, never-ending Lazarus effect.” Every new demi,
carries the knowledge, experience and memories of the all the previous before him. “As he
showed me all of this, sure I found it difficult to believe but, then I remembered all the moments
I had spent being Naberius’ demi. I could feel every other existence it had ever experienced. It
was so difficult not to dissociate and lose myself in memories, motivations and drives and
impulses which may not have been my own.” Sarah continues trying to explain to Avery. “Then
the real kicker.” Sarah added. “The one thing which made it even harder to accept what was
really going on.” Her gaze shifted away from Avery as she recalled the conversation when that
Lucifer told her about the concept of the ‘Source.’ A creature of light and purest goodness, had
fallen under a dark influence. A corruption which caused it to be thrown deep into the furthest
depths of time, the genesis of our time. Imprisoned in, not eternity but, the beginning of one.
Avery listened intently as Sarah told this fantastical story. As our time emerged and life
began to sprout, pieces and pieces of this divine source were divided among all living things.
With time, this pure light within most of us, began to be corrupted by and itself in turn corrupt its
host in a desperate struggle for survival as it attempts to… “Claw its way out of the confines of
time.” Sarah finished. Dr Avery Melblac unsurprisingly finds all this ridiculous. She struggles to
hold it in until eventually she burst out laughing. “This is absurd!” she exclaimed in disbelief. “It
is the truth though.” Ellis chimed in as he too joins in on the laughter. “I know, it took time for
me to get it too. In time you might eventually realise it too but we do not have that much time.”

Sarah sighed exasperatedly. “So do I have a piece of this divine source inside of me too.” Avery
asked with none of the sublet nuance of sarcasm evident in her tone. “Yes, which is why I need
you.” She said simply. “But I thought….” Avery paused. She was not really prepared to deal
with all of this. “What is this great evil threat we must confront.” She asked Sarah with some
hesitation. She did not know what to expect. “There are others.” Sarah stated flatly. “More devils,
if you can call them that.” She goes on “Ugh Sarah, can I take it from here please, I haven’t done
much, sorta feels like this is my moment.” Ellis interrupted politely as he gestured vaguely
between the two women. Sarah nods her consent reluctantly. “Go ahead.” Ellis smiles
reassuringly to Avery. “The memories I now possess, they tell of a vicious devil. Belial is her
name. It is apparent that Lucifer, well Uhm hmm all this is confusing enough, let me start over.”
Ellis pauses for a second to recollect his thoughts. “Belial plans to tap into the divine source. For
millions and millions of years, every iteration of Belial has had a singular purpose, and that is to
reunite every piece of the divine source back into its original form. At various points in time,
some Lucifers have been helping her achieve this goal and that is how I know that the result of
the reconstituting would mean the end of us all.” Ellis said gravely. Avery does not appear to be
impressed by such a dire warning, retaining her academic scepticism in the face of such absurd
claims. “So, what, you expect me to put my faith in the hands of Lucifer and some witch who has
betrayed me twice already…!? No! No way in hell!” She exclaims angrily. Sarah cannot contain
her frustration. She realises the significant nature of the current problem, she grows impatient,
“we don’t have time for this.” She announces as she quickly grabbed a hold of Avery’s face,
placing a thumb on her forehead and initiating the Deviants invocation. Her ritual tattoo begins
to glow as Avery is transported into her own psyche to confront her divine source.

“Be thankful that your meaningless lives are now contributed to the balance… Rejoice!
Assholes, you have had the privilege of being sacrifice for a greater god. You may think this is
suffering. No… it is your salvation.” Carlos Valdez’s voice booms loudly throughout the entire
church. “Let us celebrate this glorious day…for we are blessed in our birth, and blessed in our
death! Be proud, you will be free!” He continues to speak ever so proudly as the hapless cries of
mercy fade into the background. “Lord Belial, they are ready for you.” Carlos says solemnly.
The young child walks towards him in triumph. “They’re almost perfect. After this sacrifice I
shall be strong enough and we can finally go and retrieve the key.” She proclaims proudly.
Carlos smiles at Belial as a devilish smirk appears on his lips. “Yes, my lord, I know exactly how
we will manage this.” He says excitedly. Belial laughs as they walk hand in hand towards the
alter. “Ambrose, she is ready to begin, you know what to do.” Carlos instructs Ambrose, who
bows down reverently and approaches the altar holding a knife. A ritual tool used by Belial and
her followers for such an occasion. He hands the knife to Belial and proceeds to collect one
victim from the throng of frightened church goers who were being held captive by the Devil’s
den. Belial has already slaughtered countless souls in her current life cycle alone. In a bid to
achieve her goal to reunite the pieces of the celestial from which she was born. “Now watch
closely because this is going to be quite interesting.” Carlos says to Ambrose before Belial raises
the knife above the sacrifice. A 60-year-old woman who comes to this church every Saturday
and Sunday. She is a mother and a grandmother to beautiful children whom she adores. On this
day, her youngest refused to came to the church with her. She was upset, and at this moment she
was relieved that her daughter was not there to witness her be murdered and be next herself.
Ambrose looks on, eager to see what Belial is capable of doing. The knife falls upon the innocent
woman and blood gushes freely outwards, staining her dress and her face crimson red. Then

slowly, ever so slowly, life slowly drains from the woman’s eyes. Belial is satisfied as she looks
at Ambrose with wild eyes. “Do not look away. Do not fear.” She says calmly to the trembling
Ambrose who stares at her with awe and horror in his eyes. This was his first-time being part of
these rituals. He was only recently recruited by Carlos into the Devil’s den and this was his first
introduction to what really, they were about. He loved it. Ambrose is overcome with lust and
desire at the sight of Belial in front of him, blood trickling slowly off of the knife, dripping onto
the ground below. It is absolutely mesmerising to watch. He is filled with excitement and ecstasy.
“Bring me another.” Belial orders and Ambrose obeys immediately. He turns around and picks
up one of the more timid of the crowd. He carries her into position and watches as Belial takes
another life.
When Sarah and Ellis snuck out from Defiance headquarters with Avery, it took some
time before anyone noticed that they were gone. When they finally did, Defiance was put under
lockdown. With Sarah missing as well, the Deviants were under suspicion for being involved in
the escape. Although there was no proof against them, no witnesses or evidence of their
involvement, everyone agreed that they should be dealt with accordingly. Atticus was called and
instructed to gather whatever forces he could muster to track down Ellis and Sarah. He was also
given explicit instructions not to allow any of the remaining Deviants to leave the premises, he
was to lock them up if necessary. Amala, Miles and Steven were all in the library, pacing back
and forth nervously as they waited for Atticus’ update about their missing friend. Amala tried to
calm them all as best she could but she soon grew restless, worried sick about her vision coming
to pass. Miles sits beside the window, his elbows resting on his knees as his head rests upon his
hands in a dejected manner. “Please god, just tell me she’s ok.” He mumbled miserably. Atticus
walked into the room looking hollow yet composed. There was something different about him.

Something in his eyes, something strange about the way he looked at them all. He had something
urgent to share. Everyone gathered close around him listening in anxious anticipation. “We ah,
we are going to look for them. We have an idea of where they might be going.” Atticus tells the
team. “Well? Where is it?” Miles practically yells. His voice is shaking with anxiety and anger.
He wanted to find them as badly as any other person. But at the same time, he was terrified that it
would turn out nothing. That she’d never come home again. That he would not see his partner
again, or worse that she’d end up possessed. “Listen to me, you have to run.” Atticus insists to
the Deviants. “I’ll explain later, you need to get away from here.” Miles interjects before
anything else can be said, his heart rate quickening as he notices the desperation in Atticus’ voice.
“Find Jonathan Reed, he’ll help you.” Atticus implores. They are hesitant, not realising the
danger they are in. “Why, why do we have to leave?” Miles asks urgently. “Just trust me.”
Atticus pleads with them. “No, we are not going anywhere until you tell us what is going on.”
Miles retorts determinedly, and even Steven nods his agreement. “It’s dangerous for you here,
we’re running out of time.” Atticus replies desperately trying to reason with them. “They want to
destroy us.” Amala interjects quietly. “What?” Miles’ expression changes from furious to
concerned, then desperate in an instant. “She’s right. The others, they don’t trust you, they never
have and now they are planning on completing your exorcism. Getting rid of every trace of
demon in you, I’m sorry.” Atticus tells them, apologetically. “Wait, isn’t that a good thing? We
can finally live normal lives again.” Steven chimes in. Atticus shakes his head adamantly, “no,
not for the world. Look at what you’ve accomplished in the few months you have been here.
With the way things are going, people are going to need you and the power you possess.” Steven
nods in understanding. “Ok, we will go.” The team agrees in unison, still unsure about leaving.

Jonathan Reed is walking along the darkened streets of the city. He was dressed all in
black attire that blended perfectly within the shadows as he approached his destination. He
arrived in front of an abandoned warehouse and entered through an unlatched door at the back of
the building. Walking across the dark empty room he made his way to a wooden crate covered in
dust and cobwebs sitting underneath some dusty curtains. Opening up the crate, the box was
filled with many candles, some of them burnt to the point of having reduced the waxen wicks
completely to cinders. He has been searching for something and he was sure this was the place to
find it. Reaching deep within the crate, he pulled out a single candle and carefully lit it. The
candle burned bright orange flames as it glowed brightly. He makes his way deeper into the
warehouse, aided by the faint trail of light left behind by the flame in the palm of his hand. He
stops abruptly as he hears footsteps approaching him rapidly in the distance. He holds the candle
tightly and prepares to attack whomever is approaching him. As the figure came nearer, it
became clear that the intruder was female. Her features appeared pale and weak as she stood
before him, her hair was dirty and tangled, and sweat streaked her face. It was detective Driscoll.
She had gotten lost in the warehouse and even fell down the stairs more than once. “What are
you doing here Driscoll?” Jonathan asked calmly. “What am I doing here? I am here to catch a
killer, what are you doing here?” Driscoll shot back angrily. “I knew you were going to leave me
out of the case, so I did my own investigating and I found this place, it….” “Belongs to the man
from the tavern, I know. I would’ve thought seeing me get shot in the chest and not die would
have scared you off of this case. Get out of here detective, you are out of your depth.” Reed
warned, pointing towards the exit. “Lucky for me I don’t work for you so I am not going
anywhere.” Driscoll replies defiantly. Jonathan sighs resignedly before telling her, “This place
could very well be full of demons, monsters that can kill you just like that, so don’t slow me

down.” He goes. “There is no such thing as demons.” She replies. They both stare at each other
for a while. “You really believe that, don’t you.” Jonathan replies as if stating a fact. “Yes.” She
answers resolutely. “How naïve.” Jonathan continues dryly. “Let’s keep moving.” He adds. She
follows obediently behind him. They continue their sweep until they reach the middle of the
warehouse, then stop suddenly. “What is that smell?” Driscoll questions as she sniffed the air
suspiciously. “Smell? What smell?” Jonathan asks, confused. “What’s wrong with you can’t you
smell that?” She responds as she points to the large door directly opposite them. Jonathan stands
perfectly still for an instant. He looks towards the bottom of the door. Something catches his
attention. It is a rug that is covering the small opening at the bottom of the door. They both walk
towards it and Jonathan yanks it back. When they see what is underneath, both their expressions
reflect the same thing- “what the hell?!”, is all that either of them can think to say. There is a big
black blood stain that stretches from just outside the door and disappears underneath it inside the
room. Detective Driscoll almost throws up all over the floor. Her eyes bulge out and widen until
she becomes too frightened to even breathe properly. “Come on, we have to see what’s behind
this door.” Jonathan says with urgency as he grabs hold of handle to try and push it open. The
door creaks open, revealing a sliver of light. They stare deep within the seemingly empty room.
A dank stench hits them both all at once causing them to instinctively cover their nose and mouth.
“What’s your cop instinct telling you to do now?” He asks her seriously. Driscoll hesitantly takes
a step inside the room. “Investigate.” She says without taking her eyes off the floor, unable to
take her gaze off the large bloody patch. They slowly walk into the room, Driscoll’s heart
beating rapidly in fear of what may lay inside. As they continue deeper inside the room, they
notice that the floor is smeared with blood, as if someone dragged a body on it several times.
Jonathan moves towards the wall, finding a switch which he flips. Poorly illuminating the room.

The room has stone walls and old shelves stacked with all sorts of stuff, like rusted tools and
containers filled with what looks like chemicals and poisons. They continue looking for what
they believed to be the source of the smell, but they do not find it right away. “What is this
stuff?” Driscoll questions aloud, touching one of the containers. Jonathan walks towards her to
inspect what it is “This stuff is poisonous. You can’t touch it!” He warns her. “It is mainly used
by Satanists and witches for sacrifice rituals.” He adds, continuing to examine the contents of the
container. “Oh…. k.” She exclaims in horror. They continue examining the shelves. On the
other end of the room, Driscoll discovers a box which she opens with ease. Inside there are
dozens of headless cats, each of which is placed neatly in rows on top of each other. She gasps
and quickly closes the lid of the box. She vomits all over the floor. That is when Jonathan walks
over to her, holding another box. This one is filled with hundreds of photos, including ones of the
23 college kids who died at the tavern massacre.

chapters.title: The Deviants part II
chapters.text:
It is early afternoon and the sun is high up, shining brightly against the blue sky. Jonathan
Reed walks past several rookie officers in charge of keeping all the reporters and on lookers from
crossing the police tape. Detective Driscoll is taking charge and telling everyone at the scene
what to do. “Don’t let anyone else enter the building, and if any of you start talking to reporters
without my permission, you are being charged with obstruction. Keep the peace, and stay alert.”
Driscoll commands firmly. After she had given the order, all the detectives and officers present
began working furiously to protect the crime scene. This was a big break in the investigation of
the tavern massacre. The press was already trying to dig up dirt and rumours about what the
police may have found at the abandoned warehouse. The news teams were constantly asking
questions and the cops were getting increasingly tired of not having a definitive answer for them.
Inside the area Driscoll and Jonathan discovered, the forensic team was busy examining the
collection of substances they found. “Detective! Come look at this.” The forensic scientist called
out, motioning for Detective Driscoll to come look at the evidence. “Valium, chloral hydrate and
Phenergan. Those are the compounds we detected from these containers. Interestingly, the 23
victims also had traces of this stuff in them. We were not one hundred percent sure then, but now
we can say with certainty that this is what killed them.” The forensic technician explains. “Thank
you, doc.,” Detective Driscoll responds with a friendly smile. She turns to Jonathan, “What do
you think Reed? What was the motive for killing those kids.” She asks him. “I don’t know
Driscoll.” He replied honestly.

While all this is happening, Miles, with his team were now running from Defiance and
their agents. They managed to slip away, but Miles knows that they will not be able to evade
Defiance for long, especially if they want to save their friend. They have an idea of where they
are going. They could not go to Jonathan’s apartment or precinct; those would be the first place
they would be expected to run towards. Instead, Amala uses her technical wizardry to find and
locate Jonathan’s whereabouts once again, via a tablet she always carried with her. They begin
heading east, passing through several towns before finally reaching the outskirts of the city
where Jonathan was. Their destination was a small town called Drerry. In that small town, the
whole population was preoccupied with the recent tavern massacre story. The Deviants arrived at
the warehouse crime scene and subtly disappeared amongst the crowd. The day turned to night
and the crowd slowly dissipated leaving only a few policemen who were standing guard around
the warehouse. “What are we still doing here? Everyone left and probably Jonathan too.” Steven
whispered to his friends. They hid themselves in the alleyway near the main building to avoid
being discovered by the officers who stayed behind. “Jonathan’s still here, look.” Amala said
quietly, pointing at the icon on her tablet. “We cannot wait any longer, let’s go in.” Miles replied
urgently. Amala shakes her head, “No, wait….!” She whispers, grabbing Miles’ arm, stopping
him from moving. “There could still be people in there. People who are cops.” The boys looked
at one another and reluctantly agreed to wait
“We don’t have time for this.” Sara announces as she quickly grabbed a hold of Avery’s
face, placing a thumb on her forehead and initiating the Deviants invocation. Sarah’s ritual tattoo
begins to glow. From Avery’s perspective, everything was suddenly moving in slow motion, as
if all the adrenaline in her body had released. She could feel and hear her heartbeat pumping
blood through her body, echoing in her ears. She had trouble moving, like a thick blanket was

resting on her. In that moment, she could feel something leave her body through the top of her
head, like a wave of energy being pulled like a handkerchief out of a pocket, followed by a
sudden emptiness. “What on earth?” She thought with alarm, her body devoid of all tension. An
apparition taking on the form of her former mentor appears to her. He is wearing a familiar
golden ring with the swirls on his right hand. His eyes look dead; they were empty of any
emotion. He looks at her with cold indifference, as if he was simply studying a specimen. The
apparition speaks slowly, his voice rumbling with an eerie calmness. His words confirm with
clarity everything Sarah has believed her entire life, about who she was, her purpose and how she
needed to become the person she was destined to be. She no longer feels lost or confused by any
of it. She accepts this spirit as a part of her, with the perfect understanding that it will bring a
positive change into her world. Her conviction to do what is best for the world becomes clearer.
She accepts her role and all the responsibilities that come with it. This is how she wants to be
forever more. The ghostly figure then fades back into the air leaving behind a large black hole.
Avery can see the darkness from where she stands. It is as if the very universe itself is crying in
agony. She knows now that even though the darkness will never cease to haunt her life, her light
will always be there to remind her of all the people who are gone, all those lost to darkness that
she could be able to save. All the suffering she can be able to prevent. The only way, she tells
herself, is to move forward and embrace the divine source. To move forward and protect those
who need protecting. It feels warm and comforting. She wakes up in Sarah’s arms with not the
same feeling she had before falling into her trance. A feeling of peace, justice and duty consumes
her. “I saw the light.” She says softly. Sarah tightens her grip. “You did well, Dr Melblac. I’m so
proud of you.”

Belial has finally completed all her ritual sacrifices. She murdered 12 people at the
church. That, plus all the other countless sacrifices, made Belial nearly as powerful as a
Demiurge. She felt good, refreshed and ready to accomplish her mission. “Lord Belial, we
missed one.” A voice said from across the room, breaking Belial’s concentration. Belial was
annoyed to see Ambrose drag a priest over to the alter. “This one was hiding away, luckily I
caught him.” Ambrose added smugly. The priest pleaded with Belial to spare him. Belial did take
kindly to the begging. “Why should I let you live?” She asked the priest. “Because…I have to
believe…. that your soul is still with God, young lady.” He answered timidly. Belial scoffed.
“And tell me exactly where is God, old man?” Belial questioned. The priest paused for a second
before answering. “So do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God. I
will strengthen you and help you; I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.” The priest
recited in a loud voice, which echoed through the vast cathedral. The priest’s voice was strong
and confident. Belial’s eye twitched. “Why did you speak those words?” She asked the priest
earnestly. The priest took a deep breath to try and compose himself. “Someone has to speak for
God.” He answered firmly, before lowering his voice even more. “And who says you speak for
God? You are wrong, I am utterly indifferent to the suffering and salvation of humanity. On the
other hand, I cannot abide hypocrites like you father. You believe your God will protect you yet
you stand here in front of death, afraid. Where is your faith. Accept the divine and fear nothing
before you speak for me.” Belial said, as she raises her left palm above the priest. As the priest
opened his mouth to protest, he spoke for the last time, asking, “And who the hell are you?”
Belial responded in a sinister tone. “I am God…” The priest started convulsing violently, trying
to breathe but choking on his own tongue, until finally he stops moving all together.

It has been an hour since Amala, Miles and Steven have been hiding in the alley. “Guys,
guys wake up, he’s moving.” Amala whispers frantically to her companions. The three follow
Jonathan, lagging behind a bit. Jonathan arrives at the bar, the usual meeting place for the South
east grotto. They don’t call it that anymore. Its name has long since faded away. Its original
members now wilted down to just Jennifer and Ruby. Jonathan was also still a major a part of it,
though he would like not to admit it. He has been spending a lot of his time with the two witches
Depending on them to have his back should the need arise. The group had gathered to talk about
their plans and to discuss some new details to help them fight off the missing members of the
grotto, Carlos and Ambrose. Now a bigger threat to the city and possibly the world. “The police
now have total control of the warehouse. Luckily, I found and destroyed the ledger before they
could find it. No one will ever know your connection to it.” Jonathan told Jennifer and Ruby as
soon as he sat down. “Good, it’s better that way. The way things are going, it would really suck
to be associated with this Voodoo crap.” Ruby replied, crossing her fingers. Jennifer placed a
hand on her shoulder, attempting to comfort her. “Carlos betrayed us both Ruby. We don’t mess
with dark magic, remember that.” She reminded Ruby. Ruby nodded in response, although her
face betrayed that she does not fully agree with her friend’s statement. “Yeah, yeah, whatever.
So, what did you find out, are Ambrose and that… Carlos involved with the tavern massacre?”
Ruby asks, changing the subject and turning towards Jonathan. “I am now certain they are. These
murders aren’t random. They are up to something big.” Jon replies seriously, his voice full of
resolve. “Something big…?” Jennifer echoes quietly. “What about that group your father formed,
can’t they help us with this?” Ruby suggested. Jonathan turned to her and shook his head. “I do
not trust Defiance. Their agenda is completely different from ours.” He explained coincidentally.
At that very moment, Amala and her team came crushing through the bar. “Hey guys, how’s it

going.” Amala said awkwardly. Jonathan was not pleased to see them. “What the hell are you
doing here?!” He snapped at them. Amala immediately started explaining their situation to him,
“We’re being chased by defiance, Lucifer is possessing some priest, Sarah ran away again,
Avery is missing and I’ve been having visions of the world burning…I…I need you Jonathan,
we need your help.” She said, looking at him intently, begging him to help them somehow. “Oh
great…” Jonathan sighed exasperatedly.
Every agent of Defiance is told that they are the last line of defence against the forces of
evil. They play an important role in the world of magic and witchcraft. Among their duties are
battling demons and the Devil, all agents of evil. Defiance agents are sent in response to these
supernatural threats. They must fear nothing. Even if danger is looming, they must remain
fearless and continue their work. Dr Avery Melblac was one of those agents who would die for
these ideals. “Let’s go kick some demon ass.” Avery exclaimed excitedly with a smirk plastered
on her face. She now has to face the villainous demon Belial and its sinister plan. “Though I’m
still technically your boss, you know more about this threat so you take the lead. What is the plan,
Sarah?” Avery questioned with a confident expression. Sarah smiled at her. “You’ve connected
with your divinity. That’s good, keep that reckless enthusiasm, you’re going to need it.” Sarah
said as she gave Avery an encouraging nod. She turned to Ellis and noticed that he had dozed off
sitting against the wall. “Ellis?” Sarah called gently, waking him up. “Huh? Wha-” Ellis
muttered sleepily, as he tried to sit upright. “Are you good?” Sarah asked with concern. “Yeah,
yes I’m fine.” Ellis assured her. “We’re ready.” She spoke. “Then let’s get this show on the
road.” Ellis responded. He got up, walked over to Sarah and gave her a pat on the shoulder for
good luck. Ellis leaves the hideout. “Hey! Where are you going!? Where is he going!?” Avery
yelled at Ellis’ retreating figure. He kept walking. “Don’t worry about him, he’ll meet us later,

there’s something important he must do.” Sarah said reassuringly. Avery stood there confused at
first but quickly realized that Sarah was taking the lead this time, she trusted her. “Ok, but what
are we doing?” Avery insisted. “We must go meet the others.” Sarah told her, already running
out of the building. Avery followed suit. They reached the meeting spot in no time, a small bar
near the outskirts of town. There they found Miles Kelly, Jonathan Reed, Amala Jones and
Steven Grant. The gang was all together again finally. When Jonathan sees the two enter, he is
bewildered by this inconceivable meeting, he is shocked really. “Oh look, the rest of the circus
has just arrived.” Jonathan commented sarcastically. He was annoyed by it all.
While Atticus and his agents were talking about what had happened, and what they plan
to do about the situation with Ellis and Sarah, and the Deviants. Ellis appeared and greeted
Atticus. At first, Atticus was frightened and startled because he thought he was seeing a ghost, or
worse, hallucinating one because of all the stress he is under. But after catching his breath, he
was all caught up. “Excuse me.” He says to the other agents who were in his office. They nod
their heads in acknowledgment, then Atticus calmly exits the office and heads toward the library.
His steps were steady and firm, and there wasn’t any hesitation in his movements. The library is
empty. It is always empty. After opening the door, he scans a bit, making sure no one was around
and closes the door carefully behind him. Then, he sits down. His eyes begin to glaze over, and a
heavy weight fills his heart. He leans forward resting his elbows on his knees. The room feels
cold and lonely. His mind wanders before quickly snapping back to reality. “Show yourself.” He
commands. A silhouette slowly starts to appear. The darkness of the room begins to shift and
change shape and form into a human form. The figure reveals itself to be Ellis, the Lucifer demi.
“Do not freak out.” Ellis says with a calm smile on his face. “Sara, and the rest of them…. they
need you. I might be the devil but the threat they face now can bring destruction to this world

hitherto undre…..” Ellis is cut off. “Stop, just give me the footnotes.” Atticus says coldly.
“Well….” Ellis begins.
After hearing the story, Atticus had a lot of questions, ultimately deciding to hold them
for now. He had to know one thing though, “How do we stop her.” He asks. “Simple.” Ellis
simply responds with a cocky smirk forming across his lips. "Do you understand how I am able
to project myself into your consciousness and yours alone? I am not really here but, in your mind,
you see me as clear as day.” Ellis explains patiently. “I use psychic energy. A bit of an
oversimplification but essentially, I am telepathically projecting myself onto your mind.” Ellis
continues explaining. “So, you can control people, right?” Atticus inquired curiously. Ellis nods
his head slightly, “Yes, in a way. I am not an expert on the psychic, though I seem to have had
more than my share of experience. Luckily, I know of a few deviant spirits who excel at these
kinds of things. They possess proper knowledge of the spiritual nature of the universe. And I
know you have one of these demons locked away……in your vault.” Ellis proclaims. “Yes, and
what of it? I am not ashamed to admit that we keep prisoners here.” Atticus admits. “We need it
to pull off the plan.” Ellis tells him. “What is the plan exactly?” Atticus asks, curious as to what
Ellis wants to accomplish. “We call it…”
“Partition.” Sarah explains her plan to everyone at the bar. “Partition?” Miles asks in
confusion. “It’s what we came up with.” Sarah explains. “We as in you and Satan?” Jonathan
enquires with a raised eyebrow. “Yes, and he isn’t really Satan, just possessed like I was, like
Miles and Steven and Amala.” Sarah answers. They start to argue as Jonathan thinks it is a
terrible idea to be working with someone possessed by thee Lucifer. “Right now, Ellis is with
Atticus.” She adds hastily, hoping that Jonathan will shut up about it. At Defiance, Atticus enters
the vault area where a psychic demi is being sedated. Sarah continues telling the others the rest

of the plan. “We will use Steven as bait. Belial wants him and so we will give him to her.” She
says. “Hurry up.” Atticus orders Ellis impatiently. “Just try not to kill him okay.” He adds
nervously. “Belial is powerful, too powerful. Given the key, she will enact the spell to open the
gates of hell and unleash the divine source into herself. A few seconds after that happens, at least
40 percent of everyone on earth will die where they stand, as their divinity is literally pulled out
of them.” Sarah informs the team. “That’s why it’s imperative that Ellis succeeds on his end,
before Belial can use Steven.” Sarah continues. “This is going to take a while. Keep a lookout.”
Ellis tells Atticus, giving him a knowing glance. “Will do.” Atticus replies with an unamused
grin. Back at the bar, everyone knows what to do. Sarah, Jonathan, Miles, Steven, Amala,
Jennifer and Ruby all head towards Belial to face her. They arrive at the manor of Belial which is
located in the middle of a forest in a very secluded part of the city. They make it to the gate. No
one is guarding it. “How do you know she’s here?” Jennifer asks Sarah. “I used a tracking spell.”
She explains. Before anyone can question her further, Miles speaks, “And we’ve been here
before, she’s here.” He says, looking around worried. Ellis is Inside the vault with the man who
has the psychic demon. He is attempting to use it to link every human who has the divine gene,
making them aware of it so Belial will not be able to take it from them. But as Ellis began
connecting with the demon, it started growing restless, and when Ellis was unable to control its
power, it started to attack him. Sarah and the rest enter the manor to confront Belial. They find
her sitting in her favourite armchair reading the book of shadows. She looks up. Her expression
is blank and emotionless. She has no reaction whatsoever. “I hope you don’t mind our intrusion.”
Sarah greets cheerfully. Carlos, who was besides Belial, runs towards them to attack. “Assido!”
Jennifer speaks her spell, forcing Carlos to fall to the floor. “We will deal with you later.” She
says to him. “We know you are essentially a God now; we cannot stop you so we brought you a

gift. Maybe you will let us live in your new world.” Sarah states as she pushes Steven towards
the front.” Belial looks sceptical. “Please Belial, you have to know that we want this.” Sarah
speaks up again. “Then why bring them? Are you aiming to go to war with me?” She demands.
Ellis is still struggling with the psychic demi. Once more he attempts to connect with it. He
manages to get in its head. There is a struggle. “Don’t leave, I’ve seen this feeling before. Trust
me.” The demi begins to feel a heavy hand on its chest. “Just breathe, it’s not just me that wants
more.” Ellis gets through to it. In an instant, he is in the minds of every person who has a piece
of the divine. “Trust me.” Sarah says to Belial. Just then, the door bursts opened, and six men run
in and attack the group. “Shit.” Sarah says to herself as she and the others fight against the men
attacking them. Belial gets up from her chair. She walks towards Steven. “I’m glad I did not have
to go chasing you, sweet Steven.” She comments with a sinister smile. “Oh, don’t even think
about it.” Steven warns. Belial ignores Steven’s warning and lunges at him. Before he could even
react, she has him in a choke hold. Her small teenage hand barely wrapped around his neck. “Let
go of me!” Steven exclaims desperately, trying to get away. Ellis is Inside the minds of many,
they fear his presence. He is Lucifer after all. He attempts to speak to them, to warn them of
Belial. The psychic demon fights hard to keep his voice inside. Belial squeezes harder. With an
unexpected force, Steven screams and drops. The pain is excruciating. He gasps, choking as the
pain spreads throughout him. “You’re such a stubborn boy.” Belial scolds Steven. She begins to
whisper a phrase as she holds Steven. Sarah as knocked down by the enemy. Avery defends by
stabbing him. “That’s Méníshè she’s speaking, Belial is opening the gates, everyone get ready.”
Sarah warns. As Belial chants the words, her hand goes white with demonic energy. It starts
glowing brighter and brighter. Amala, Miles, Avery and Ruby all fall to the floor in excruciating
pain. “What’s happening?” Jonathan asks shocked. “The seal is breaking.” Sarah replies. “I can

feel it.” She adds. At defiance, all the other agents have been alerted of Ellis’ presence. They
know Atticus is in the vault helping him. “Hurry up.” Atticus calls to Ellis. “No problem. I just
have to convince over a billion people to trust the devil, easy.” Ellis responds, concentrating on
the task at hand. Belial’s spell begins to work, she is reconstructing the celestial. Sarah also falls
victim to the spell since she too possesses the divine within her. All of the guards working for
Belial have been defeated. Giving Jonathan and Jennifer the chance to look after their fallen
friends. “What do we do.” Jennifer asks Jonathan helplessly. “We have to trust that Sarah’s plan
is going to work.” Jonathan replies quietly. Sarah tries her best to resist Belial. It works for a
brief moment but is then undone. Belial takes control of Sarah as well. This bought Ellis a few
crucial seconds to get through to everyone. With one final resolve, Ellis tells them, “Look at it, it
is completely real, and it will come to pass. You cannot doubt this.” They all struggle desperately
against Belial’s grasp on them. Their minds are already overwhelmed by the evil influence.
Fortunately, they begin to believe everything Ellis says. As they accept their fate, Belial releases
them from her clutches. “No, no no no.” Belial screams. She begins to bleed from her eyes and
ears. There is black gooey blood coming from her palms and feet. She collapses onto her knees.
She has been defeated. The realisation dawns upon everyone. “You will pay for this. I will rip
your souls out.” Belial growls darkly, before succumbing to her injuries and collapsing to the
ground, now unconscious. “What do we do with her? She’s just a little girl.” Jennifer asks.
“Purification by fire.” Amala replies grimly. She remembers the vision she had and in it, they
burn Belial alive. At the vault, Ellis disappears right before Atticus’ eyes. Martin comes
marching in with his agents to stop Atticus from doing whatever it is he was doing.
It has been one day. Atticus is being scolded by the boss on his actions. “You cannot do
this, this is madness.” Atticus insists adamantly. “I am sorry, but they leave me no choice. Your

people are reckless and stupid. They must learn that there are consequences.” Martin lectures
him angrily. “But you can’t just throw them under the bus like this. They saved the world for
Christ sake.” Atticus pleads with him. Martin sighs exasperated. “It is too late. It’s already
done.” His tone is final. Alexandra Turner enters a bar and walks towards two arguing men. “I’ll
have a beer.” She tells the bartender. One of the men gets shoved onto her, causing her to spill a
little of the drink. “Watch it little lady.” He scowls, before walking off. “Can you turn that up
please.” Alexandra asks the bartender who obligingly turns the volume up on the television. On
the tv, an anchor woman reads the news about what happened in the last days. She reads, “A
group of known devil worshipping cult members have now been linked to the tavern massacre.
The police now tell us that this same group has been involved in numerous other ritual killings
across the city, including the murder of 14 people at a church recently. These deviants, as the
police are calling them, are dangerous. If you see them, do not approach them. The police assure
us that these people will be brought to justice.” Alexandra watches intently. They then show
pictures of Sarah, Miles, Steven and Amala as suspects in several of these murders. “You know
these guys? Some of them have been in my bar. Hm, you think you know someone.” The
bartender says. Alexandra shakes her head, “No way, I’m sure I’d remember if I’d met them.”
The bartender laughs as he places another beer on the counter. “Well, it’s a shame, they are one
of a kind.” He says with an overly sincere look on his face.
THE END
